$comment: GENERATED by `cargo run -p manifold-renderer --bin gen_node_catalog`. Source of truth is the node registry; edit nodes in code, not this file.

nodes.label: Absolute Value
nodes.purpose: Per-pixel abs(input.rgb). Alpha passes through. Useful after node.sin_texture (abs(sin(x)) is a humped positive-only pattern with twice the spatial frequency) or after scale_offset_texture to fold a signed field into a 'V' curve.
nodes.summary: Flips every negative value positive, leaving positives alone. Handy after a signed field or a sine to fold it into a V shape.

nodes.label: Add Burst (radial)

params.label: Point X
params.name: point_x
params.tooltip: Horizontal centre of the burst across the canvas, from 0 at the left to 1 at the right. Wire it from a hit position to fire the shockwave wherever the action is.

params.label: Point Y
params.name: point_y
params.tooltip: Vertical centre of the burst, from 0 at the bottom to 1 at the top.

params.label: Amplitude
params.name: amplitude
params.tooltip: How hard the push is. 0 means no burst at all, and higher values throw particles further out from the centre.

params.label: Envelope
params.name: envelope
params.tooltip: Fades the burst in and out over the hit. Wire it from a decay envelope so the kick lands and settles instead of pushing forever.

params.label: Radius
params.name: radius
params.tooltip: How far from the centre the push reaches, as a fraction of the canvas. Particles past this radius barely feel it.

params.label: Active Count
params.name: active_count
params.tooltip: How many particles to push, counted from the start of the buffer. Keep it matched to your live particle count so dead slots stay still.

nodes.purpose: Per-particle radial impulse around `(point_x, point_y)` — evaluates the radial + tangent + noise-perturbed-radial + falloff math at each particle's exact UV and applies the resulting push to `position.xy` directly. The per-particle counterpart to `node.explosion_force` (which paints the same math as a texture for downstream sampling). Use this atom when bilinear smoothing near the impulse centre would muddy the visible kick — fluid sims, sparks reacting to beat hits, particle-text inject events.
nodes.summary: Pushes particles outward from a point in a burst, like an explosion or shockwave on a hit.

nodes.label: Add Burst (3D, radial)

params.label: Inject Zone
params.name: inject_index
params.tooltip: Picks which of the four fixed burst zones to fire into, numbered 0 to 3. Set it to -1 to switch the burst off entirely.

params.label: Inject Force
params.name: inject_force
params.tooltip: How hard the burst pushes new material in, from 0 for nothing up to 1 for a full puff. Wire a trigger envelope here to fire it on a hit.

params.label: Inject Phase
params.name: inject_phase
params.tooltip: Where the burst sits in its attack and decay envelope, from 0 to 1. Drive it from the same trigger that fires the burst so the push fades in and out.

params.label: Active Count
params.name: active_count
params.tooltip: How many particles the burst is allowed to touch. Keep it matched to the particle count feeding the sim so the whole field reacts.

nodes.purpose: Per-particle 3D injection burst around one of four hardcoded tetrahedron-vertex zones. inject_index < 0 disables it; 0..3 selects a zone. Applies a noise-perturbed radial push + vortex-ring tangent (within radius 0.25, quartic falloff, attack/decay envelope from inject_phase) directly to position.xyz. The 3D sibling of node.add_burst; decomposed from the injection step of the fused node.fluid_simulate_3d.
nodes.summary: Injects 3D particles in a burst around one of a few fixed zones, puffing new material into a 3D sim on a hit.

nodes.label: Anti-Clump Particles

params.label: Strength
params.name: strength
params.tooltip: How hard each particle gets nudged off its spot every frame. 0 leaves the cloud alone, and higher values spread it out faster. The useful range is small, so keep it low for a gentle drift.

params.label: Active Count
params.name: active_count
params.tooltip: How many particles in the buffer get the kick. It is clamped to whatever the buffer can actually hold.

nodes.purpose: Modulator-weighted Brownian kick on each live particle's position.xy. With a `strength_modulator` texture wired, samples it at the particle's UV and applies `kick = (hash3(i, frame) − 0.5) * strength * capped(m)` where `capped = m / (1 + m)`. Without a modulator, applies plain `kick = (hash3) * strength` uniformly. Canonical FluidSim use wires density (kick concentrates where particles cluster); equally useful with audio amplitude maps, masks, depth slices, or any scalar texture. Sibling to node.spread_out (which kicks velocity, un-weighted) — separate atoms because the math, the state field, and the modulator weighting differ.
nodes.summary: Nudges particles apart where they bunch up, keeping the cloud evenly spread instead of collapsing into blobs.

nodes.label: Arrange Copies

params.label: Max Capacity
params.name: max_capacity
params.tooltip: The largest number of copies this node can ever hold. It sets the memory allocation, so size it for your worst case and leave it.

params.label: Active Count
params.name: active_count
params.tooltip: How many copies to actually place this frame, default 64. Slide it freely up to the max capacity to thin out or fill the field.

params.label: Layout
params.name: layout
params.tooltip: The pattern the copies fall into, Grid, Ring, Spiral, or Random.

params.label: Seed
params.name: seed
params.tooltip: Changes the random layout. Move it to get a different spread. Only matters when the layout is set to Random.

params.label: Extent X
params.name: extent_x
params.tooltip: How far the layout reaches along X, default 4. Larger values spread the copies wider apart.

params.label: Extent Y
params.name: extent_y
params.tooltip: How far the layout reaches along Y, default 4. Larger values spread the copies wider apart.

params.label: Extent Z
params.name: extent_z
params.tooltip: How far the layout reaches along Z, default 4. Larger values spread the copies wider apart.

params.label: Base Scale
params.name: base_scale
params.tooltip: The size every copy starts at. 1 leaves the base mesh at its native size, higher grows them all together.

params.label: Rotation X
params.name: rot_x
params.tooltip: Tilts every copy around the X axis by the same amount, in degrees.

params.label: Rotation Y
params.name: rot_y
params.tooltip: Spins every copy around the Y axis by the same amount, in degrees.

params.label: Rotation Z
params.name: rot_z
params.tooltip: Rolls every copy around the Z axis by the same amount, in degrees.

nodes.purpose: Emit an Array<InstanceTransform> filled with one of four procedural layouts (slots beyond active_count zero out): Grid — side = ceil(active_count^(1/3)), 3D index (cx,cy,cz) = (i mod side, (i/side) mod side, i/side^2), position = ((cx/(side-1) - 0.5)*extent_x, (cy/(side-1) - 0.5)*extent_y, (cz/(side-1) - 0.5)*extent_z). Ring — t = i/active_count, theta = t*2π, position = (cos(theta)*extent_x/2, 0, sin(theta)*extent_z/2). Spiral — t = i/active_count, theta = t*2π*4 (4 turns), r = t, position = (cos(theta)*r*extent_x/2, (t-0.5)*extent_y, sin(theta)*r*extent_z/2). Random — wang-hash of (index, seed) per axis, position = (hash-0.5)*extent per axis, uniform within the extent box. Scale is uniform `base_scale`; rotation is uniform (rot_x, rot_y, rot_z) applied identically to every instance. Pair with node.render_copies to draw N copies of a base mesh. The unlock for NestedCubes / DigitalPlants-shaped graphs.
nodes.summary: Lays out a field of copies in a grid, ring, spiral, or random spread, giving each one a position to render at. Pair it with Render Copies.

nodes.label: Array Feedback
nodes.purpose: One-frame delay for Array<Particle>: this frame's input becomes next frame's output. Closes per-frame particle loops without introducing graph cycles. The internal state-backed buffer is sized to match the producer's pre-allocated wire capacity (item_size × max_capacity). Optional `seed` input initialises the persistent buffer on first allocation (mirrors `node.feedback`'s seed-bootstrap) — wire `node.fluid_seed` (or any particle source) here for non-zero first-frame state. Optional `reset_trigger` input fires a re-seed on integer-edge changes: when it advances, the next emission copies `seed` into the state buffer (treats the trigger event as a clear+reseed). When `seed` is unwired, `reset_trigger` is a no-op.
nodes.summary: Holds a list from the previous frame and hands it back this frame, closing a feedback loop for a particle or instance system without a graph cycle.

nodes.label: Filter Detections
nodes.purpose: Reject degenerate items from a Channels[X, Y, WIDTH, HEIGHT] detection array by size, aspect ratio, and frame coverage — all bounds in the detector's normalised 0..1 coordinate space. Keeps an item only when min_width <= width <= max_width, min_height <= height <= max_height, min_aspect <= (width / height) <= max_aspect, and (width * height) <= max_area_frac. Drops frame-wide horizon strips (huge aspect), vertical slivers (tiny aspect), specks (below min size), and bbox-covers-everything regions (above max_area_frac). Sits between a detector and a tracker so identity tracking never locks onto garbage. Defaults (width/height bounded to [0,1], min_aspect=0, max_aspect=1000, max_area_frac=1) pass everything through; set bounds to taste. Output preserves the Channels type and the order of surviving items; rejected slots compact out and the tail zero-fills.
nodes.summary: Drops junk detections that are too small, too stretched, or cover too much of the frame, before they reach the tracker. Stops a HUD from locking onto the horizon line or a stray sliver.

nodes.label: Array Math

params.label: Operation
params.name: op
params.tooltip: The operation run over every number in the list. Binary ops like Add, Multiply, Min and Mix read both inputs, while unary ops like Sine, Abs and Clamp01 read only the first.

params.label: Scale
params.name: scale
params.tooltip: The multiplier used by ScaleOffset and ShapePowClip, and the blend amount for Mix. Other operations ignore it.

params.label: Offset
params.name: offset
params.tooltip: Added after scaling in ScaleOffset. Only that operation reads it.

params.label: Exponent
params.name: exp
params.tooltip: The power applied in ShapePowClip, where 1 is linear and higher bends the response into a steeper curve. Only that operation reads it.

params.label: Bias
params.name: bias
params.tooltip: Shifts the input before the power in ShapePowClip. Only that operation reads it.

nodes.purpose: Element-wise math over Array<f32>. One bundled primitive (op enum) covering the common shaping vocabulary: binary (Add/Subtract/Multiply/Divide/Min/Max/Mix read `a` + `b`); unary (ScaleOffset, ShapePowClip, MirrorRamp, Clamp01, Abs, Sin, Cos — read `a` only, ignore `b`). Op-specific scalars (scale / offset / exp / bias) are port-shadow-param so they can be modulated by control wires. Divide-by-near-zero clamps to 0 to keep NaN/Inf out of downstream consumers. CPU-only — runs on the content thread so downstream CPU readers see same-frame writes.
nodes.summary: Runs the same math over every number in a list, like add, multiply, sine, or scale. The list-wide version of the Math node.

nodes.label: Atmosphere
nodes.purpose: Scene-wide atmosphere producer: exponential depth fog (colour + density + height falloff) plus an ambient/sky tint, emitted as a single Atmosphere struct consumed by render_scene's optional `atmosphere` input. Fog fades distant geometry toward fog_color by 1 - exp(-density·distance); height_falloff concentrates it near the ground (y=0) for a haze look; ambient_tint multiplies each object's ambient term. Every param is port-shadowed by a same-named optional scalar input, so fog density on a fader or beat_ramp is a live depth-mood knob. Unwired into render_scene = fog off (density 0), byte-identical to no atmosphere.
nodes.summary: Scene fog + sky tint for render_scene. Wire it into a scene's atmosphere input; put fog density on a fader for an instant depth-mood knob.

nodes.label: Bake Environment (equirect)

params.label: Width
params.name: width
params.tooltip: Width of the baked panorama in pixels. Keep it at twice the height for a correct equirect ratio, so 512 wide pairs with 256 tall.

params.label: Height
params.name: height
params.tooltip: Height of the baked panorama in pixels. Keep it at half the width so the longitude and latitude mapping stays undistorted.

params.label: Horizon Strength
params.name: horizon_strength
params.tooltip: How bright the horizon band glows. 1 matches the default studio look, higher pushes a stronger rim of light into reflections.

params.label: Azimuth Variation
params.name: azimuth_variation
params.tooltip: How much the lighting changes as you turn around the horizon. 0 is perfectly even, higher breaks up the ring so reflections shift with angle.

params.label: Environment Intensity
params.name: intensity
params.tooltip: Master brightness of the whole environment. 1 is the full studio look, higher brightens every reflection, and 0 bakes a black map so objects are lit only by the scene's own lights.

nodes.purpose: Procedurally bake an HDR studio environment map at the given resolution. Equirectangular layout (longitude × latitude). `mode = gradient` (default) matches the legacy MetallicGlass envmap at 512×256: ambient floor + bright horizon band + overhead softbox + floor fill + two strip lights + azimuthal modulation. `mode = softbox` bakes an exact-zero black base lit only by `emitter_count` bright horizontal emitter strips (soft falloff at strip edges only), plus one optional directional sun disc at `sun_x/sun_y/sun_z` sized by `sun_disc_size` and `sun_disc_intensity` (0 = no disc). Output is HDR — wire into `node.render_mesh`'s `envmap` input (PBR material) for IBL reflections, or `node.tone_map` if displaying directly.
nodes.summary: Builds a studio environment map for reflections, laid out as an equirectangular panorama. Feed it into a PBR material for image-based lighting.

nodes.label: Basic Light (Lambert)

params.label: Light X
params.name: light_x
params.tooltip: Horizontal direction the light comes from. Positive lights the surface from the right, negative from the left.

params.label: Light Y
params.name: light_y
params.tooltip: Vertical direction the light comes from. Positive lights from above, negative from below.

params.label: Light Z
params.name: light_z
params.tooltip: How much the light points straight out of the screen toward the surface. The direction is normalised so only the balance between the three axes matters.

params.label: Ambient
params.name: ambient
params.tooltip: A flat fill that keeps the unlit side from going fully black. 0 gives hard shadows, higher values lift the whole surface toward flat.

nodes.purpose: Lambert (diffuse) shading from a tangent-space normal map and a directional light: `out = max(dot(n, normalize(light_dir)), 0) * (1-ambient) + ambient`, multiplied by the light's colour. The basic directional-lighting atom — pair with `node.gradient_map` to tint further, or sum with `node.rim_light` / `node.shininess` for stylized PBR. Two ways to drive the light: scattered `light_x/y/z` scalars (output grayscale, white tint), or wire a `node.light` into `light` (output picks up the light's premultiplied colour). The scalar fallback keeps every existing OilyFluid-shaped consumer working unchanged.
nodes.summary: Shades a surface from its normal map and a single direction, brightest where it faces the light. The plain matte lighting term.

nodes.label: Basic Shape

params.label: Shape
params.name: shape
params.tooltip: Which shape to draw, square, diamond, or octagon.

params.label: Aspect Ratio
params.name: aspect
params.tooltip: Stretches the shape wider or taller.

params.label: Scale
params.name: scale
params.tooltip: How big the shape is.

params.label: Line Thickness
params.name: line
params.tooltip: Thickness of the outline when wireframe is on.

params.label: Rotation
params.name: rotation
params.tooltip: Rotates the shape, in degrees.

params.label: Wireframe
params.name: is_wireframe
params.tooltip: Draw just the outline instead of a filled shape.

nodes.purpose: Single-dispatch 2D SDF shape — Square / Diamond / Octagon — rasterised into an RGBA16F texture with anti-aliased edges. One instance draws one shape; pick which via the static `shape` enum param. `rotation`, `is_wireframe`, and the geometric params (aspect/scale/line) are port-shadows-param so cycling and easing live in the outer graph. Mux multiple `node.basic_shape` instances at the output for runtime shape selection (the BasicShapes.json pattern).
nodes.summary: Draws one of three simple shapes, a square, diamond, or octagon, as a clean anti-aliased fill. Pick the shape, then size and rotate it.

nodes.label: Beat Gate

params.label: Rate
params.name: rate
params.tooltip: How often the gate fires, as a note division of the beat.

params.label: Amount
params.name: amount
params.tooltip: The value output while the gate is on. Off is always 0.

params.label: Duty
params.name: duty
params.tooltip: How much of each cycle the gate stays on, from a short blip to nearly always on.

params.label: Phase
params.name: phase
params.tooltip: Shifts where in the beat the gate fires.

nodes.purpose: Beat-synced square gate. Outputs `0` when off and `amount` when on, flipping at the `duty` point within each cycle of the selected note rate. Stateless and seek-safe — the same musical pattern as `node.strobe`'s internal gate, surfaced as a wireable scalar source.
nodes.summary: A square pulse locked to the tempo, on for part of each beat and off for the rest. The strobe and chop building block.

nodes.label: Beat Ramp

params.label: Rate (cycles/beat)
params.name: rate
params.tooltip: How many times the ramp rises and resets per beat.

params.label: Attack
params.name: attack
params.tooltip: How quickly the ramp climbs at the start of each cycle. Lower is a sharper snap up.

nodes.purpose: Per-beat attack envelope: out = clamp(fract(beats·rate) / attack, 0, 1). Snaps to 0 each beat, ramps to 1 over the first `attack` fraction of the cycle, then holds. The musician-facing pop-in envelope (Voronoi Prism fades cells in over the first 15% of each beat). Stateless / seek-safe — reads FrameTime.beats (or a wired `beat`). Wire into any scalar (gain, mask multiplier, opacity) for a beat-synced attack.
nodes.summary: Rises from 0 to 1 across each beat then snaps back, a sawtooth locked to the tempo. Wire it into anything you want to sweep in time with the music.

nodes.label: Bend Mesh
nodes.purpose: Classic per-vertex bend of an Array<MeshVertex>. axis selects the bend coordinate A (X/Y/Z); the rotation happens about the third axis B (the one that is neither A nor its cyclic-next companion C), pivoting A's own coordinate by `center`: axis=X rotates (x,y) about Z pivoting x; axis=Y rotates (y,z) about X pivoting y; axis=Z rotates (z,x) about Y pivoting z. theta = angle * (coord(A) - center) * w, where w is the optional per-vertex `weights` input (a short or unwired weights buffer degrades to 1.0, never silent 0). Position AND normal rotate by the same theta — exact, not approximate (normal has no center pivot, since it's a direction not a point). `angle` is UNBOUNDED (range None) — a saw LFO doing full revolutions is a valid performer gesture, never clamp it.
nodes.summary: Curves a mesh into an arc around a hinge line, like bending a rod. Position and lighting normals both rotate exactly, so it reads correctly at any angle, including full revolutions.

nodes.label: Bilateral Blur
nodes.purpose: Depth-guided (bilateral) single-axis blur: fixed 9 taps at 1-texel spacing along `axis`, weight_j = K9_j * exp(-(dz_j/depth_sigma)^2) where K9_j are the same sigma~=2 gaussian constants used by every other 9-tap kernel in this codebase and dz_j is the linearized-depth difference from the center texel, renormalized by the weight sum actually used. Pair a Horizontal pass with a Vertical pass for a full 2D edge-aware blur that smooths noise (e.g. raw SSAO/GTAO occlusion) without bleeding across depth discontinuities (silhouette edges stay sharp). Alpha is a pure center pass-through. `camera` is read entirely via near/far derived uniforms for `linearize_depth` — never a GPU binding.
nodes.summary: A depth-guided blur that smooths noise without bleeding across depth edges — the standard denoise pass after any per-pixel noisy sampler (ambient occlusion, dithered effects) that needs to stay sharp at silhouettes.

nodes.label: Blend Copies

params.label: Mix
params.name: t
params.tooltip: How far to blend from the first arrangement toward the second. 0 is all the first, 1 is all the second, and 0.5 is the midway morph. Values outside that range overshoot.

nodes.purpose: Elementwise linear interpolation between two Array<InstanceTransform>s. out[idx] = (1 - t) * a[idx] + t * b[idx] applied to both pos_scale and rot_pad. The continuous counterpart to node.switch_array — pick this when the morph parameter is a real 0..1 slider and intermediate values must visually morph (the DigitalPlants cyl ↔ tor case). At t=0 the output equals a; at t=1 it equals b; at t=0.5 the elementwise midpoint. `t` is port-shadow-param so the morph factor can be modulated.
nodes.summary: Blends two arrangements of copies together by an amount, so you can morph a field of copies from one layout to another.

nodes.label: Blob Overlay

params.label: Color
params.name: color
params.tooltip: The colour of the boxes drawn around each blob.

params.label: Alpha
params.name: alpha
params.tooltip: How opaque the boxes are over the image. 0 is invisible and 1 is solid.

params.label: Border Width
params.name: border_width
params.tooltip: Thickness of the box outline, in UV units where 0.003 is roughly 2 pixels at 720p. Push it past the blob size to get solid filled boxes instead of outlines.

params.label: Blob Count
params.name: blob_count
params.tooltip: The most blobs to draw boxes for, up to 32. It is safe to leave at 32 since entries with no size are skipped.

nodes.purpose: Draw hollow rectangles around each blob in an Array<Blob> on top of a source Texture2D. Companion to node.blob_tracker for sparse blob visualisation. Minimal box-drawing — for the full HUD treatment (brackets, crosshairs, ticks, labels) use the legacy node.blob_tracking wrapper.
nodes.summary: Draws boxes around each tracked blob on top of the image, so you can see what the Blob Tracker is finding. A debug view for blob tracking.

nodes.label: Blob Tracker

params.label: Max Capacity
params.name: max_capacity
params.tooltip: The most blobs this can track at once, up to 8. It is read once when the effect is built, so changing it live has no effect.

params.label: Threshold
params.name: threshold
params.tooltip: The brightness cutoff for what counts as a blob. Lower picks up dimmer regions, higher only catches the brightest spots.

params.label: Sensitivity
params.name: sensitivity
params.tooltip: How eagerly nearby bright regions get merged into one blob. Higher pulls neighbours together into bigger blobs, lower keeps them as separate smaller ones.

params.label: Analysis Max Dim
params.name: analysis_max_dim
params.tooltip: The longest side the image is shrunk to before detection runs, in pixels. Smaller is faster but loses small blobs, larger is slower but more precise.

params.label: Update Interval (frames)
params.name: update_interval
params.tooltip: How many frames to wait between detection passes. 1 runs every frame for the snappiest tracking, higher values save work but lag behind motion.

nodes.purpose: Sparse blob detection (bright-region tracking) via the manifold_native BlobDetector FFI plugin. Input: any Texture2D. Output: Array<Blob> (16-byte items: x, y, width, height in normalized 0..1 image space). First N entries are valid blobs (N = detected count, capped at 32); remaining entries are zeroed. Pair with downstream blob-overlay render primitives to draw the boxes, or wire to any consumer that iterates Array<Blob>.
nodes.summary: Finds bright blobs in the image and tracks them frame to frame, handing back their positions and sizes as a list. The base for blob-reactive visuals.

nodes.label: Block Displace Field

params.label: Amount
params.name: amount
params.tooltip: How far blocks slide and how many of them move. At 0 nothing shifts, and as you raise it the displacement grows from sparse to dense.

params.label: Block Size
params.name: block_size
params.tooltip: The size of each block in pixels. Smaller blocks give a fine grain and larger blocks give chunky datamosh tearing.

params.label: Speed
params.name: speed
params.tooltip: How fast the random pattern reshuffles over time. Higher values churn the blocks more often.

nodes.purpose: Generator for a per-block random UV-offset field (the datamosh / block-glitch building block). Quantises the canvas into block_size-pixel blocks, hashes each (animated by time), and emits `offset` (RG = signed per-block UV displacement, gated so only a fraction of blocks move) and `hash` (R = raw per-block hash in [0,1) for downstream per-block accents that must align with the displaced blocks). Feed `offset` into node.remap (Relative mode) — alone or summed with other offset fields via node.mix(Add). `amount`/`speed` port-shadow their params; `time` is wired or read from FrameTime.seconds.
nodes.summary: Outputs a grid of random block offsets, the displacement map behind datamosh and block-glitch looks. Feed it into Remap.

nodes.label: Blur

params.label: Radius
params.name: radius
params.tooltip: How far the blur reaches, in pixels. 0 leaves the image sharp and higher values soften it more.

params.label: Mode
params.name: mode
params.tooltip: The blur shape. Gaussian gives a smooth falloff, Box is a flat even spread, and Radial smears outward from the centre.

nodes.purpose: Separable Gaussian blur, a horizontal then a vertical pass through a per-instance ping-pong texture. `radius` sets the kernel width.
nodes.summary: Softens the image evenly in all directions, with a radius that sets how strong the blur is. The everyday blur.

nodes.label: Blur (3D)

params.label: Mode
params.name: mode
params.tooltip: What kind of volume to blur. Scalar handles a density field that lives in one channel, Vector handles a force field across all four.

params.label: Axis
params.name: axis
params.tooltip: Which direction this pass blurs along, X, Y, or Z. Chain three instances, one per axis, for an even blur in all directions.

params.label: Volume Resolution
params.name: vol_res
params.tooltip: The grid size of the volume, default 128. It has to match the actual dimensions of the input and output volume or the blur reads the wrong cells.

params.label: Radius
params.name: radius
params.tooltip: How wide the blur reaches, in voxels. Higher values soften more and cost more. Wire it from a scalar to drive the width live.

nodes.purpose: Single-axis separable Gaussian blur on a Texture3D. Mode selects scalar (samples .r, writes single channel) or vector (samples .rgba, writes all channels). For a full 3-axis separable blur, wire three instances with ping-pong textures (axis=X(a→b), axis=Y(b→a), axis=Z(a→b)). Bilinear tap-pairing halves the sample count vs a naive Gaussian.
nodes.summary: Blurs a 3D volume one axis at a time, softening a density or flow field. Run it on each axis for an even blur in all directions.

nodes.label: Bokeh Gather
nodes.purpose: Single-pass occlusion-aware disc gather depth-of-field (docs/CINEMATIC_POST_DESIGN.md D5): 32 golden-angle spiral taps (r_i = sqrt((i+0.5)/32), theta_i = i*2.399963, rotated per-pixel by the committed hash) scaled by the CENTER pixel's CoC (read from `width`'s R channel, coc_from_depth/coc_dilate's [0,1]-fraction-of-max_radius convention), each tap weighted by step(distance_to_center_px, tap_coc_px) — a sample only contributes if its OWN CoC reaches back to the center (the standard scatter-as-gather occlusion approximation, generalizing node.variable_blur's ScatterAsGatherByCoC weighting from 1D taps to a 2D disc). Luminance-preserving normalization (divide by the accumulated weight; falls back to the center color if every tap is occluded). Circular aperture v1 — no blade-count shaping. center_coc < 0.005 (in-focus) is an exact pass-through, same convention as node.variable_blur's own in-focus early-out — a zero-CoC lens (f_stop = infinity) produces a bit-clean image through this atom. Same `in`/`width` port shape as node.variable_blur so it drops straight into a DoF chain in its place: coc_from_depth (-> coc_dilate) -> bokeh_gather.width, upstream color -> bokeh_gather.in.
nodes.summary: A true circular-aperture depth-of-field blur: each out-of-focus pixel gathers from a disc of neighbors sized by its own blur amount, and neighbors only contribute if they're blurry enough to reach back — the photographic 'bokeh' look, in one pass.

nodes.label: Brightness

params.label: RGB Weights
params.name: weights
params.tooltip: How much each of red, green, and blue counts toward the grey value. The defaults are the Rec.709 luma weights, which match how the eye sees brightness.

nodes.purpose: RGB -> weighted grayscale (luma) via per-channel weights. Defaults are BT.709 luma coefficients, so the default behaviour is desaturate-to-luminance.
nodes.summary: Collapses colour to a single brightness value using per-channel weights — the default weighting matches how the eye perceives luminance.

nodes.label: Camera Lens
nodes.purpose: Physical lens for a Camera wire: focus_distance / f_stop / shutter_angle / exposure_ev, all four port-shadowed scalar params. Pure CPU pass-through — takes a Camera in, rewrites ONLY its lens block, passes every other field (position, basis vectors, near/far, projection mode, cached view matrix) through unchanged. Insert once between a camera source (node.orbit_camera / node.free_camera / node.look_at_camera) and its consumers so depth-of-field, motion blur, and node.render_scene's exposure all read the same lens instead of duplicating params. focus_distance is world units along the camera's fwd axis (<= 0 = hyperfocal/neutral); f_stop is the aperture N (large = shallow-DoF off); shutter_angle is degrees 0..=360 (0 = no motion blur); exposure_ev is stops (0 = neutral, render_scene multiplies its final straight rgb by 2^exposure_ev). Defaults reproduce a neutral lens, so an unwired, untouched camera_lens node changes nothing downstream.
nodes.summary: The physical camera: focus distance, aperture, shutter angle, and exposure — one lens any camera source can feed, and every 3D consumer reads.

nodes.label: Canvas Area Scale

params.label: Width
params.name: width
params.tooltip: The canvas width in pixels. Wire this from the generator's output width so it tracks the real resolution.

params.label: Height
params.name: height
params.tooltip: The canvas height in pixels. Wire this from the generator's output height so it tracks the real resolution.

params.label: Reference Area
params.name: reference_area
params.tooltip: The baseline pixel count the ratio is measured against. Default is 1920 by 1080. The output reads 1 at that size and rises as the canvas gets bigger. Set to 0 and it falls back to 1 so nothing breaks.

params.label: Min Width
params.name: min_width
params.tooltip: Floors the input width before the area is worked out, so a tiny window does not dim the output below something usable. 0 means no floor. Set to 640 for FluidSim2D behaviour.

params.label: Min Height
params.name: min_height
params.tooltip: Floors the input height before the area is worked out, so a tiny window does not dim the output below something usable. 0 means no floor. Set to 360 for FluidSim2D behaviour.

nodes.purpose: Emit (width * height) / reference_area as a scalar. The brightness compensation atom for splat-based density displays — wire `width` from `system.generator_input.output_width` and `height` from `system.generator_input.output_height`, then multiply the result into a tone-map's `intensity` so the canvas stays equally bright across output resolutions.
nodes.summary: Outputs how big the canvas is compared to a reference size, used to keep particle brightness steady when the resolution changes.

nodes.label: Cel Material

params.label: Colour R
params.name: color_r
params.tooltip: The red amount of the base colour, from 0 to 1. This is the colour the bands are tinted before lighting snaps them into steps.

params.label: Colour G
params.name: color_g
params.tooltip: The green amount of the base colour, from 0 to 1.

params.label: Colour B
params.name: color_b
params.tooltip: The blue amount of the base colour, from 0 to 1.

params.label: Opacity
params.name: color_a
params.tooltip: How opaque the material is, from 0 for see-through up to 1 for solid.

params.label: Cel Bands
params.name: cel_bands
params.tooltip: How many flat lighting steps the surface snaps into. 2 gives a stark lit and shadow split, and higher counts toward 16 ease back into smooth shading.

params.label: Band Low
params.name: band_low
params.tooltip: How dark the shadow side gets, as a fraction of full brightness. 0.08 leaves the shadows nearly black, higher lifts them.

params.label: Band High
params.name: band_high
params.tooltip: How bright the lit side gets, as a fraction of full brightness. 1 is full brightness on the lit face.

params.label: Emission R
params.name: emission_r
params.tooltip: The red amount of light the surface gives off on its own, from 0 to 1, independent of any scene light.

params.label: Emission G
params.name: emission_g
params.tooltip: The green amount of light the surface gives off on its own, from 0 to 1.

params.label: Emission B
params.name: emission_b
params.tooltip: The blue amount of light the surface gives off on its own, from 0 to 1.

params.label: Emission Intensity
params.name: emission_intensity
params.tooltip: How strongly the emission colour glows, from 0 for off up to 10. Leave it at 0 unless you want the material to light itself.

nodes.purpose: Cel-shaded material — Lambert N·L quantized into `cel_bands` discrete bands. Stylised look; the DigitalPlants aesthetic. The bundled 3D mesh renderers compute per-fragment N·L with the wired light, snap into one of N bands between `band_low` (shadow side) and `band_high` (lit side), and multiply by base_color. Outputs one Material on `out`. Requires a `light` input wired to the renderer.
nodes.summary: A toon material that snaps the lighting into a few flat bands for a cartoon or cel-shaded look.

nodes.label: Centered UV

params.label: Center X
params.name: cx
params.tooltip: Horizontal point the coordinates measure from, where 0.5 is the middle of the canvas. Move it to slide a radial or zoom effect off centre.

params.label: Center Y
params.name: cy
params.tooltip: Vertical point the coordinates measure from, where 0.5 is the middle of the canvas.

params.label: Scale X
params.name: scale_x
params.tooltip: Stretches the horizontal coordinates. 1 leaves them as is, higher spreads the pattern wider, and a negative value flips it left to right.

params.label: Scale Y
params.name: scale_y
params.tooltip: Stretches the vertical coordinates. 1 leaves them as is, and a negative value flips it top to bottom.

nodes.purpose: UV recentered around (cx, cy) with per-axis scale. out.r = (uv.x - cx) * scale_x, out.g = (uv.y - cy) * scale_y. The canonical centered/aspect-corrected coordinate space for procedural patterns — replaces the explicit (uv - center) * (aspect, 1) * inverse_scale chain a centered field would otherwise need. Defaults cx = cy = 0.5 preserve screen-centered behaviour.
nodes.summary: Outputs each pixel's position measured from a centre point, so the middle reads zero and the edges spread out. The base for radial and zoom effects.

nodes.label: Channel Mixer

params.label: Row 0 (R)
params.name: row0
params.tooltip: How much red, green, blue, and alpha each feed into the new red channel. The default takes red straight through.

params.label: Row 1 (G)
params.name: row1
params.tooltip: How much red, green, blue, and alpha each feed into the new green channel. The default takes green straight through.

params.label: Row 2 (B)
params.name: row2
params.tooltip: How much red, green, blue, and alpha each feed into the new blue channel. The default takes blue straight through.

params.label: Row 3 (A)
params.name: row3
params.tooltip: How much red, green, blue, and alpha each feed into the new alpha channel. The default takes alpha straight through.

nodes.purpose: Per-pixel 4x4 RGBA matrix transform: out = M . in, where M's rows are the four Vec4 params (row0=R, row1=G, row2=B, row3=A). Identity matrix is the param default — output = input.
nodes.summary: Remaps RGBA channels through a 4x4 matrix — swap, isolate, or blend channels into each other.

nodes.label: Checkerboard

params.label: Scale
params.name: scale
params.tooltip: How many squares fill the frame. Higher means smaller squares.

params.label: Offset X
params.name: offset_x
params.tooltip: Slides the grid sideways.

params.label: Offset Y
params.name: offset_y
params.tooltip: Slides the grid up and down.

nodes.purpose: Pure generator. Alternating black/white squares at configurable scale. Output is binary {0, 1} broadcast to RGB (A = 1). Useful as a debug/diagnostic pattern, a mask for node.compose, or a base for grid-aligned effects.
nodes.summary: Lays down an alternating black and white checker grid at any scale. Handy as a test pattern, a mask, or a base for tiled looks.

nodes.label: Chroma Key

params.label: Key Colour
params.name: key_color
params.tooltip: The colour to key on. Pixels close to this become the mask, the classic move is the green or blue of a screen behind your subject.

params.label: Tolerance
params.name: tolerance
params.tooltip: How far a pixel's colour can sit from the key and still count as a match. Higher pulls in a wider range, and above about 0.5 it starts grabbing most of the image.

params.label: Softness
params.name: softness
params.tooltip: Fades the edge of the mask. 0 gives a clean hard edge, higher values blur the transition between keyed and untouched.

params.label: Mode
params.name: mode
params.tooltip: Select outputs white where the colour matches, good for applying an effect to that colour. Reject flips it for a traditional green screen knockout.

nodes.purpose: Produce a per-pixel mask describing how close each pixel is to a target colour (RGB Euclidean distance, soft falloff at the tolerance edge). Pairs with `masked_mix` to make any effect operate selectively on a chosen colour range.
nodes.summary: Outputs a mask showing how close each pixel is to a chosen colour, the green-screen key. Feed it into a mask mix to knock out a background.

nodes.label: Circle Mask

params.label: Center X
params.name: cx
params.tooltip: Horizontal centre of the circle across the frame, where 0 is the left edge and 1 is the right.

params.label: Center Y
params.name: cy
params.tooltip: Vertical centre of the circle up the frame, where 0 is the bottom edge and 1 is the top.

params.label: Radius X
params.name: radius_x
params.tooltip: Half the width of the shape. Make it different from Radius Y to stretch the circle into an oval. At 0 the shape collapses to a line along that axis.

params.label: Radius Y
params.name: radius_y
params.tooltip: Half the height of the shape. Make it different from Radius X to stretch the circle into an oval. At 0 the shape collapses to a line along that axis.

params.label: Rotation
params.name: rotation
params.tooltip: Turns the oval around its centre, measured in degrees. Has no visible effect on a perfect circle.

params.label: Softness
params.name: softness
params.tooltip: Feathers the edge of the mask. 0 gives a clean hard edge, and higher values fade it out, measured in radius units so 1 spreads the falloff a full radius past the edge.

nodes.purpose: Rotated elliptical SDF mask. Output: RGB = mask value (inside=1, outside=0, smoothstep falloff of width `softness`), A = 1. Pure UV-space; no canvas-aspect correction. Industry-standard masking convention — pairs with masked_mix downstream, and with node.invert when the polarity needs flipping (e.g. DoF wants outside=1).
nodes.summary: Draws a soft-edged circle to limit an effect to a round region. It can stretch into an oval and rotate.

nodes.label: Clamp

params.label: Min
params.name: min
params.tooltip: The lowest value any colour is allowed to reach. Nothing goes darker than this.

params.label: Max
params.name: max
params.tooltip: The highest value any colour is allowed to reach. Nothing goes brighter than this.

nodes.purpose: Per-pixel clamp on RGB: out.rgb = clamp(in.rgb, min, max). Alpha passes through unchanged. The saturate() atom: pair after scale_offset_texture / power_texture / trig_texture / any chain that produces unbounded output, before LUT lookups / pow with fractional exponent / displacement scales that need a defined input range. Defaults to [0, 1].
nodes.summary: Holds every colour between a low and high limit so nothing goes darker or brighter than you set. The tidy-up step after a math node.

nodes.label: Clip Trigger Cycle

params.label: Modulus
params.name: modulus
params.tooltip: How many values to cycle through, so the output lands in 0 up to this minus 1. Default 7 matches the fluid seed patterns. Set it to your own row count.

nodes.purpose: Defense-in-depth `trigger_count % modulus` cycle: emits a value in [0, modulus) on each new trigger_count, advancing past would-be repeats so consecutive emissions never duplicate. Pair with `node.fluid_seed.pattern` for the legacy 7-pattern re-seed cycle, or any other discrete selector that must never fire the same row twice in a row. Pass RAW `trigger_count` (never pre-wrapped) — the cycle handles the modulus internally and pre-wrapping breaks idempotence.
nodes.summary: Steps through a range on each clip trigger, never landing on the same value twice in a row. Drives never-repeat preset cycling.

nodes.label: Clip Trigger Index

params.label: Modulus
params.name: modulus
params.tooltip: How many slots to cycle through before wrapping back to the start. Set it to 3 for a three-way swap or 8 for an eight-step bank.

nodes.purpose: Emit `trigger_count % modulus` as a scalar via the idempotence-safe ClipTriggerCycle gate. The graph-level counterpart to a primitive's internal clip-trigger cycling (Plasma does this inline). Use as `mux_texture.selector` to swap between N upstream variants on each clip retrigger; pair with `mux_scalar` and a static-axis param to support both manual and trigger-driven modes from one outer-card toggle.
nodes.summary: Counts how many times a clip has been triggered and wraps it to a range, so each retrigger steps to the next slot. Drives preset cycling.

nodes.label: CoC Dilate
nodes.purpose: Fixed 3x3 neighborhood-max dilation of a single-channel mask-shaped texture (R == G == B convention, e.g. node.coc_from_depth's output): out.r = max over the 3x3 neighborhood (self + 8 neighbors) of in.r, broadcast to RGB, alpha = 1.0. Fixes BUG-137 (docs/BUG_BACKLOG.md): node.variable_blur reads its per-pixel gather radius from only the center pixel's own CoC, so a heavily-blurred pixel never borrows a wider radius from a neighboring high-CoC pixel — this atom spreads the max CoC outward before the gather consumes it, softening the hard seam at depth discontinuities. Wire coc_from_depth.out -> coc_dilate.in -> variable_blur(H/V).width. No params: the 3x3 radius is fixed, not a performer knob. A flat (uniform) input passes through unchanged (max of identical values is that value).
nodes.summary: Spreads the maximum blur amount from a depth-of-field mask into its neighboring pixels, so the transition from sharp to blurry looks soft instead of having a hard visible edge.

nodes.label: CoC From Depth
nodes.purpose: Physically-based circle-of-confusion from scene depth + a Camera (thin-lens model, docs/CINEMATIC_POST_DESIGN.md D1): f_mm = 24mm / (2*tan(fov_y/2)); A_mm = f_mm/f_stop; D_mm = linearize_depth(raw_depth, near, far) * 1000; S_mm = focus_distance * 1000; coc_mm = A_mm*f_mm*|D_mm-S_mm| / (D_mm*max(S_mm-f_mm, 1.0)); coc_px = clamp(coc_mm/24mm * viewport_h, 0, max_radius). Output is coc_px / max_radius (a [0,1] fraction) — wire straight into node.variable_blur's `width` input with max_radius matched between the two nodes. f_stop = infinity (pinhole) makes the whole buffer zero. Reads fov_y/near/far and the Camera's lens (focus_distance/f_stop, written by node.camera_lens) entirely via derived uniforms — the Camera wire is never a GPU binding.
nodes.summary: Computes how out-of-focus each pixel should be from scene depth and a physical camera lens — the depth-of-field math, before any blurring happens.

nodes.label: Color LUT

params.label: Amount
params.name: amount
params.tooltip: Crossfades between the original image and the palette-mapped version. 0 leaves the source untouched and 1 is the full lookup.

params.label: Contrast
params.name: contrast
params.tooltip: Pushes brightness around the midpoint before the lookup, which spreads the image across more of the palette. 1 leaves it unchanged, higher is punchier.

nodes.purpose: 1D LUT remap: sample a W×1 LUT texture indexed by BT.601 luminance (with contrast adjust), then crossfade against the source. The Infrared preset graph supplies the LUT.
nodes.summary: Remaps the image through a lookup-table strip indexed by brightness, the engine behind heat-map and infrared palettes.

nodes.label: Color Sample

params.label: UV
params.name: uv
params.tooltip: Where in the image to read the colour, as a point from 0 to 1 across width and height. The centre is 0.5, 0.5.

params.label: Region Radius (px)
params.name: radius_px
params.tooltip: Averages a square window of this many pixels around the sample point. 0 reads a single pixel, higher values smooth out noise so the reading reflects the region.

nodes.purpose: Read a single pixel from the input texture at the configured `uv`. Emits `out` (Vec3 RGB) and `luma` (Rec.709-weighted brightness scalar). Bridge for pulling representative colours or per-region brightness out of an image. One frame of latency.
nodes.summary: Reads the colour at a single point in the image and outputs its RGB and brightness. An eyedropper you can drive an effect from.

nodes.label: Colorize

params.label: Amount
params.name: amount
params.tooltip: How strongly to tint the image toward the chosen colour.

params.label: Tint Hue
params.name: hue
params.tooltip: The colour to tint toward, as a position on the colour wheel in degrees.

params.label: Tint Saturation
params.name: saturation
params.tooltip: How saturated the tint colour is.

params.label: Focus
params.name: focus
params.tooltip: How tightly the tint sticks to the bright neutral areas rather than spreading everywhere.

nodes.purpose: Tint an image toward a hue, masked per-pixel by (brightness × neutrality × focus): a selective colorize/duotone toward highlights. Bright neutral pixels take the tint; dark or already-saturated pixels resist it. `amount` is colorize strength [0,1], `hue` the tint hue (deg), `saturation` the tint saturation, `focus` how tightly the mask favours highlights/neutrals (0 = tint everything). All four port-shadow their params for live modulation.
nodes.summary: Tints the image toward a single colour, strongest on the bright neutral areas. Good for duotones and washes.

nodes.label: Combine XY (curve)

params.label: Scale
params.name: scale
params.tooltip: Rescales the whole curve. At 1 it fills the inner half of the screen, and 4 cancels the built-in screen-fit so the X and Y values land at their raw screen-fractional size.

nodes.purpose: Combine two Array<f32> (x channel, y channel) into one Array<CurvePoint>. The curve-pipeline counterpart to node.split_xy; the standard way to assemble a curve from independently-built axis chains (generate_range → array_math sweep → pack_curve_xy → render_lines). `scale` is port-shadows-param so an outer slider can rescale the whole curve at performance time. An internal PROJ_SCALE = 0.25 screen-fit constant is folded into the output: at scale = 1.0 the curve fills the inner 50% of the screen — matches the legacy generator_math::PROJ_SCALE convention so existing line-renderer presets stay visually identical. CPU-only — runs on the content thread so downstream CPU consumers see same-frame writes.
nodes.summary: Zips two number lists, X and Y, into one list of points ready to draw as a line or curve.

nodes.label: Combine XYZW
nodes.purpose: Combine four Array<f32> (x, y, z, w channels) into one Array<Vec4Vertex>. The 4D analogue of node.combine_xy: zips axis-separated channels (built by generate_grid_uv → array_math chains) into the typed wire that node.rotate_4d / project_4d / render_lines consume. Pure structural transformation — no scale bake; per-shape magnitude normalisation is applied upstream via array_math(ScaleOffset) since the constant is shape-specific (0.125 for tesseract, 0.176776695 for duocylinder, …). Pair with generate_grid_uv + array_math(Cos|Sin) + edges_from_grid_uv to author any (u, v)-parametric 4D surface in pure JSON. CPU-only — runs on the content thread so downstream CPU consumers see same-frame writes.
nodes.summary: Zips four separate number lists into one list of 4D points. The 4D counterpart to combining X and Y into a curve.

nodes.label: Compressor Envelope

params.label: Ratio
params.name: ratio
params.tooltip: How hard to duck the gain when the input is loud. Higher squeezes more.

params.label: Sensitivity
params.name: sensitivity
params.tooltip: How quickly the envelope reacts to changes in the input level.

params.label: Target
params.name: target
params.tooltip: The level the compressor aims to hold the signal at.

nodes.purpose: Audio-compressor envelope path applied to a scalar signal level. Log-domain, program-dependent attack/release with ratio compression toward a target. Out is a gain multiplier in [0.1, 10.0]. Stateful.
nodes.summary: Takes a signal level and produces a gain that ducks when the input is loud, the way an audio compressor rides the volume. Use it for auto-gain on brightness.

nodes.label: Connect Nearest

params.label: Max Distance
params.name: max_distance
params.tooltip: How close two items must be before a line connects them, in the input's own coordinate space which is 0 to 1 for blob detections. Lower values only link items that are nearly touching.

params.label: Max Edges
params.name: max_edges
params.tooltip: Caps how many connecting lines can be drawn. Once this many edges exist the rest are skipped, which keeps a busy scene from filling with lines.

nodes.purpose: For each item in a Channels[X, Y, WIDTH, HEIGHT] array, find its nearest neighbour within max_distance and emit an EdgePair (A_INDEX, B_INDEX). Sparse nearest-neighbour graph generation. Wire detection regions, particle positions, or any sparse-position array; output connects to render_lines edges port for connection-line visualisation.
nodes.summary: For each item in a list, finds its nearest neighbour and emits a connecting line. Used to draw constellations between tracked blobs.

nodes.label: Contrast

params.label: Contrast
params.name: contrast
params.tooltip: How hard to push the lights and darks apart. 1 leaves it unchanged, higher is punchier, lower is flatter.

nodes.purpose: Pivot-around-0.5 contrast: out = (c - 0.5) * contrast + 0.5. 1.0 = unchanged, >1 = punchier, <1 = flatter. Alpha passes through. The `contrast` input port shadows the param for live modulation. HDR-safe affine — use this rather than node.levels for contrast (levels' gamma pow NaNs on the negatives a contrast push produces).
nodes.summary: Pushes the lights and darks apart for a punchier image, or pulls them together for a flatter one. It pivots around mid grey.

nodes.label: Cube Mesh

params.label: Max Capacity
params.name: max_capacity
params.tooltip: How much room to reserve in the vertex buffer, set when the effect is built. 36 holds exactly one cube, and larger values only help if a downstream node expects a bigger buffer.

params.label: Size
params.name: size
params.tooltip: Scales the cube. 1 is the default unit cube, smaller shrinks it, larger grows it.

nodes.purpose: Emit a unit cube as 36 triangle-list MeshVertex entries (6 faces × 2 triangles × 3 vertices) with per-face outward normals. The cube-shape building block for NestedCubes / DigitalPlants and any instanced-cube graph: pair with node.arrange_copies + node.render_copies to draw a field of cubes.
nodes.summary: Builds a unit cube as a 3D mesh ready to rotate, light, and render. The starting block for box-based geometry.

nodes.label: Custom Convolution

params.label: Kernel[0]
params.name: k0
params.tooltip: The top-left weight of the 3x3 kernel. The nine weights read in reading order, top row first.

params.label: Kernel[1]
params.name: k1
params.tooltip: The top-centre weight of the 3x3 kernel.

params.label: Kernel[2]
params.name: k2
params.tooltip: The top-right weight of the 3x3 kernel.

params.label: Kernel[3]
params.name: k3
params.tooltip: The middle-left weight of the 3x3 kernel.

params.label: Kernel[4] (center)
params.name: k4
params.tooltip: The centre weight of the 3x3 kernel, applied to the pixel itself. Defaults to 1 so an untouched node passes the image straight through.

params.label: Kernel[5]
params.name: k5
params.tooltip: The middle-right weight of the 3x3 kernel.

params.label: Kernel[6]
params.name: k6
params.tooltip: The bottom-left weight of the 3x3 kernel.

params.label: Kernel[7]
params.name: k7
params.tooltip: The bottom-centre weight of the 3x3 kernel.

params.label: Kernel[8]
params.name: k8
params.tooltip: The bottom-right weight of the 3x3 kernel.

params.label: Bias
params.name: bias
params.tooltip: A constant added to every pixel after the kernel runs. Useful for lifting emboss and edge-detect results out of black.

params.label: Normalise
params.name: normalise
params.tooltip: Divides the result by the sum of the weights so brightness is preserved. Turn it on for blurs and off for sharpen or edge-detect kernels.

nodes.purpose: General 3×3 non-separable convolution with a user-supplied kernel (9 float weights k0..k8 in row-major order, k4 = center). Optional bias and sum-normalisation. Useful for Sobel, Laplacian, emboss, sharpen, diffusion, custom edge detectors. The kernel is data — composable, modulatable, and discoverable for AI agents (vs hardcoded-weight specific primitives).
nodes.summary: Runs a custom 3x3 kernel over the image, so you can build your own blur, sharpen, edge-detect, or emboss from nine weights. For when the preset filters don't do quite what you want.

nodes.label: Cycle Table Row

params.label: Table
params.name: table
params.tooltip: The built-in table of number rows it steps through. Each clip trigger advances to the next row and emits it.

nodes.purpose: Cycle through a curated `Table` of f32 rows on each clip trigger, emitting the selected row as `Array<f32>`. row_idx = ClipTriggerCycle::step(trigger_count, table.row_count()): a repeated trigger_count returns the cached row (idempotent — same-frame callers don't re-roll); a new trigger_count emits trigger_count % row_count, unless that would repeat the previous emission (and row_count > 1), in which case it advances by +1 mod row_count instead — never fires the same row twice in a row. The generic preset-cycler primitive — pair with any consumer that takes a variable-length float buffer (per-instance angles, channel triplets, rhythm steps) plus a trigger source from generator_input. Output capacity = table.col_count(); rows are dimensionally consistent (enforced at JSON load). Unwired trigger_count stays on row 0.
nodes.summary: Steps through the rows of a small built-in table on each clip trigger, emitting one row of numbers at a time. A way to sequence preset values.

nodes.label: Cylinder Wrap Field

params.label: Base Radius
params.name: base_radius
params.tooltip: How wide the cylinder is at its base. Larger values push the copies further out from the centre axis.

params.label: Height
params.name: height_scale
params.tooltip: How tall the wrapped shape stands. Larger values stretch the copies further up and down the axis.

params.label: Taper
params.name: taper
params.tooltip: How sharply the radius narrows toward the top, giving cone and conifer shapes. 0 is a straight cylinder, higher makes a pointier top.

params.label: Instance Scale
params.name: instance_scale
params.tooltip: Scales every copy placed on the surface. Feed the same value into a paired torus wrap so copies stay the same size across a morph.

nodes.purpose: Lift an Array<vec2<f32>> of UVs onto a cylindrical surface and emit Array<InstanceTransform>. For each UV: theta = uv.x * TAU, r = base_radius * pow(max(1 - uv.y, 0), taper) + radius_disp, y = (uv.y - 0.5) * height_scale, pos = (r·cos θ, y, r·sin θ). The taper curve narrows the radius toward uv.y=1 — produces stem / vase / cone / conifer shapes. Optional radius_disp Array<f32> adds per-instance radial noise (typically driven by node.simplex_noise_per_copy + shaping). All scalar params are port-shadow so the cylinder can be animated by control wires.
nodes.summary: Wraps a flat grid of points around a cylinder, placing copies on a curved surface. Part of the digital-plants geometry.

nodes.label: Depth Map

params.label: Analysis Max Dim
params.name: analysis_max_dim
params.tooltip: The longest side, in pixels, the frame is shrunk to before the AI looks at it. Lower runs faster but reads depth coarsely, higher is sharper but slower.

params.label: Update Interval (frames)
params.name: update_interval
params.tooltip: How many frames to wait between depth estimates. 1 runs every frame for the freshest result, higher values ease the CPU load but the depth lags the picture a little.

nodes.purpose: MiDaS monocular depth estimation via FFI native plugin, wrapped as a primitive. Input: any Texture2D frame. Output: depth map (R = G = B = depth ∈ [0, 1], near = 1, far = 0; A = 1). Inference runs on a background worker thread with ~2-3 frame latency; output is bilinear-upsampled from an analysis-resolution staging texture into the runtime-allocated output. Until first inference completes, the output is black.
nodes.summary: Estimates a depth map from any flat image with an AI model, so nearer things read bright and far things dark. Feed it into a blur or displace to fake 3D from 2D footage.

nodes.label: Digital Plants Render

params.label: Instance Count
params.name: instance_count
params.tooltip: How many cubes to draw, counted from the start of the field. Drop it to thin out the look or lighten the load.

params.label: Light X
params.name: light_x
params.tooltip: Horizontal position of the light in the scene. Move it to swing the shadows side to side.

params.label: Light Y
params.name: light_y
params.tooltip: Height of the light above the scene. Higher places it overhead for short shadows, lower for long raking ones.

params.label: Light Z
params.name: light_z
params.tooltip: Depth position of the light, toward or away from the camera.

params.label: Light Intensity
params.name: light_intensity
params.tooltip: Brightness of the light. 0 leaves the cubes in shadow, and higher values blow out the lit faces.

nodes.purpose: Fused two-pass DigitalPlants renderer: shadow pass (depth-only from light POV) into an internal shadow map, then main pass with instanced cel-shaded cubes + 5-tap PCF shadow sampling. Hardcoded 36-vert cube geometry (no Array<MeshVertex> input). Pair upstream with node.arrange_copies (or any procedural compute that produces InstanceTransforms — DigitalPlants's procedural compute is one such producer).
nodes.summary: Renders a field of cubes lit with shadows, the core of the Digital Plants look. A fused renderer still to be decomposed.

nodes.label: Distance to Point

params.label: Center X
params.name: cx
params.tooltip: Horizontal position of the point to measure from.

params.label: Center Y
params.name: cy
params.tooltip: Vertical position of the point.

params.label: Scale
params.name: scale
params.tooltip: Multiplies the distance, controlling how fast it brightens with range.

params.label: Scale X (anisotropic)
params.name: scale_x
params.tooltip: Stretches the distance horizontally, for an oval falloff.

params.label: Scale Y (anisotropic)
params.name: scale_y
params.tooltip: Stretches the distance vertically.

nodes.purpose: Pure generator. Per-pixel Euclidean distance from a configurable center in UV space, broadcast to R/G/B (A=1). Building block for radial fields, circle fields, and tunnel-like compositions.
nodes.summary: Outputs how far each pixel is from a chosen point, bright far away and dark near it. A radial gradient you build circle masks and ripples from.

nodes.label: Dither

params.label: Amount
params.name: amount
params.tooltip: How few brightness levels to crush the image into. Lower gives a harder, more banded look.

nodes.purpose: Luminance-preserving dither quantize driven by an external threshold pattern. Quantizes Rec.709 luma to 8->2 levels (by `amount`), dither-biased by the `pattern` input's R channel, preserves hue, and crossfades against the source. Pair with node.dither_pattern for the classic six patterns, or feed any threshold texture.
nodes.summary: Reduces the image to a few brightness levels and hides the banding with a fine noise pattern. The classic low-bit look.

nodes.label: Dither Pattern

params.label: Algorithm
params.name: algorithm
params.tooltip: The threshold pattern used to decide where pixels flip. Choose from Bayer, Halftone, Lines, X-Hatch, Noise and Diamond.

nodes.purpose: Pure generator. Emits a per-pixel ordered-dither / halftone threshold in [0,1] (R=G=B=T, A=1) for one of six algorithms (Bayer 8x8, Halftone, Lines, CrossHatch, Blue Noise, Diamond), in screen space. The reusable pattern half of the dither effect — pair with node.dither (consumes a pattern) for the full effect, or feed any halftone / threshold consumer. The quantizer accepts ANY threshold texture, so you can also drive it from hash noise, a custom ramp, or a voronoi field.
nodes.summary: Generates the threshold grid that the Dither node uses to decide where pixels flip, with a choice of Bayer, halftone, and other patterns. Feed its output into Dither.

nodes.label: Downsample

params.label: Factor
params.name: factor
params.tooltip: How much to shrink the image. 2 is half size, 4 is a quarter, and so on.

nodes.purpose: Integer-factor (2x / 4x / 8x) box-filter downsample of a Texture2D. Output dims = input dims / factor; the executor allocates the downstream slot at the smaller size so subsequent passes (e.g. a gaussian blur) run at reduced bandwidth. Used as the front of multi-resolution pipelines — quarter-res velocity blur in oily fluid, bloom mip starts, CoC pyramids, etc.
nodes.summary: Shrinks the image by a whole-number factor with a box filter, trading detail for speed. Good before a heavy effect or for a blocky look.

nodes.label: Draw Connections
nodes.purpose: Draw dashed lines between paired detections, additively over the source. Pairs arrive as Channels[A_INDEX, B_INDEX] (wire node.connect_nearest) indexing the Channels[X, Y, WIDTH, HEIGHT] detections array; each line runs centre to centre. midpoint_radius_px adds a soft dot at each pair's midpoint (0 turns it off). Pixel params are 1080p-referenced. The relationship layer of a tracking HUD.
nodes.summary: Draws dashed lines linking tracked objects that are near each other, with an optional dot at the middle of each link.

nodes.label: Draw Dots
nodes.purpose: Draw a soft dot at the centre of every detection in a Channels[X, Y, WIDTH, HEIGHT] array, additively over the source image. Coverage fades linearly to the edge of radius_px (1080p-reference pixels), so dots read as small glows at any resolution. The centre-point layer of a tracking HUD.
nodes.summary: Draws a small glowing dot at the centre of every tracked object.

nodes.label: Draw Gauge
nodes.purpose: Draw an outlined readout bar below every detection in a Channels[X, Y, WIDTH, HEIGHT] array, additively over the source. The bar fills in proportion to the detection's area (fill_scale maps area to the 0..1 fill), so bigger objects read as fuller bars. Pixel params are 1080p-referenced. The size-readout layer of a tracking HUD.
nodes.summary: Draws a small readout bar under every tracked object that fills up as the object gets bigger.

nodes.label: Draw Lines

params.label: Edge Thickness
params.name: edge_thickness
params.tooltip: How thick each line is drawn, as a fraction of the frame height. Around 0.002 is roughly one pixel at 1080p.

params.label: Closed Loop
params.name: closed_loop
params.tooltip: Joins the last point back to the first so the path closes into a loop. Turn it off to leave an open strip.

params.label: Show Vertices
params.name: show_verts
params.tooltip: Draws a dot at each point as well as the connecting lines.

params.label: Vertex Size
params.name: vert_size
params.tooltip: How large the vertex dots are drawn, where 1 matches the classic generator size.

params.label: Animate
params.name: animate
params.tooltip: Reveals the lines as a scrolling window that travels along the path rather than drawing them all at once.

params.label: Speed
params.name: speed
params.tooltip: How fast the reveal window scrolls when Animate is on.

params.label: Window
params.name: window
params.tooltip: The fraction of the path shown at once when Animate is on. Small values draw a short travelling segment, larger values show more of the line.

params.label: Beat Flash
params.name: beat_flash_amount
params.tooltip: How hard the lines pulse brighter on each beat. Set it to 0 to hold a steady brightness.

params.label: Color R
params.name: color_r
params.tooltip: The red amount of the line colour. Values above 1 push into HDR for a brighter additive glow.

params.label: Color G
params.name: color_g
params.tooltip: The green amount of the line colour. Values above 1 push into HDR for a brighter additive glow.

params.label: Color B
params.name: color_b
params.tooltip: The blue amount of the line colour. Values above 1 push into HDR for a brighter additive glow.

params.label: Color A
params.name: color_a
params.tooltip: The opacity of the lines, from invisible at 0 to fully solid at 1.

nodes.purpose: Draw an Array<CurvePoint> as anti-aliased capsule line segments with 4x MSAA and additive blending. Input points are in pre-aspect curve space centred at the origin; this node applies aspect correction + centre offset on its way to the framebuffer. `animate=true` enables a scrolling-window reveal that matches the legacy line-generator helper; `show_verts=true` draws a dot at each (visible) vertex. `beat_flash_amount` pulses luminance per beat to match the legacy generator_lines.wgsl flash. Pair with node.combine_xy (for parametric curve graphs built from generate_range + array_math chains) or other curve-source primitives upstream.
nodes.summary: Draws a set of smooth anti-aliased lines onto the image from a list of points. Used for wireframes, paths, and curve overlays.

nodes.label: Draw Markers
nodes.purpose: Stamp a marker symbol at every detection in a Channels[X, Y, WIDTH, HEIGHT] array, drawn additively over the source image. Symbol picks the look: Corner Brackets traces the four corners of each detection's bounding box, Crosshair draws a cross at its centre. Arm length follows the detection's size via size_fraction; thickness_px keeps line weight constant across resolutions. The marker layer of a tracking HUD.
nodes.summary: Draws a marker on every tracked object: corner brackets around it or a crosshair at its centre. The building block for tracking overlays.

nodes.label: Draw Particles (scatter)

params.label: Active Count
params.name: active_count
params.tooltip: How many particles to splat, counted from the start of the buffer. Match it to your live particle count so empty slots add nothing.

params.label: Energy per Particle
params.name: scaled_energy
params.tooltip: How much brightness each particle deposits where it lands. Higher values build the image up faster, so dense clouds bloom sooner.

params.label: Boundary
params.name: boundary
params.tooltip: What happens to particles that land outside the canvas. Wrap tiles them back around so fluids flow without a seam, Discard drops them to avoid a hard edge when projecting from 3D.

nodes.purpose: Atomic-add splat of particles into a u32 fixed-point accumulator buffer sized to the host's canvas. Each live particle contributes `scaled_energy` to its nearest texel; the buffer is cleared at the start of each dispatch. `boundary` selects the out-of-bounds policy: Wrap (toroidal — seamless tiling, FluidSim style) or Discard (drop the particle — avoids the edge seam when projecting from 3D where particles legitimately fall outside [0,1]², StrangeAttractor style). `active_count` and `scaled_energy` are port-shadows-param so they can be driven by runtime wires (e.g. a `node.math` chain for brightness normalisation by particle count). `width` and `height` are required wired inputs — the convention is to drive them from `system.generator_input.output_width / output_height` so the dispatch tracks the host's canvas (the buffer itself is also auto-sized to the canvas via `canvas_sized_array_outputs()`, so allocation and dispatch never disagree). Pair with `node.resolve_scatter` to read the result as a float texture.
nodes.summary: Splats a cloud of particles onto a buffer, building up an image from where they land. Pair it with Resolve Scatter to read the result back.

nodes.label: Draw Particles (3D scatter)

params.label: Active Count
params.name: active_count
params.tooltip: How many particles from the front of the buffer to splat into the volume. Set this to match the live particle count.

params.label: Volume Resolution
params.name: vol_res
params.tooltip: The width and height of the volume grid in cells. Higher gives a finer density field at the cost of memory and speed.

params.label: Volume Depth
params.name: vol_depth
params.tooltip: How many cells deep the volume grid is. Together with the resolution this sets how much detail the density field holds.

params.label: Energy per Particle
params.name: scaled_energy
params.tooltip: How much density each particle adds to its cell. The default 4096 reads back as roughly 1.0 in float density downstream.

nodes.purpose: Atomic-add splat of an Array<Particle> into a u32 3D accumulator buffer sized vol_res × vol_res × vol_depth. Each live particle's nearest-voxel cell receives `scaled_energy` via atomicAdd. Pair with node.resolve_scatter_3d to lift the u32 grid into a float Texture3D for downstream volumetric primitives like blur_3d, gradient_curl_3d, project_particles_3d.
nodes.summary: Splats 3D particles into a volume buffer, building up a 3D density field from where they land. The 3D version of Draw Particles.

nodes.label: Draw Particles (camera)

params.label: Active Count
params.name: active_count
params.tooltip: How many particles get drawn, counted from the front of the buffer. It is capped to the real particle count.

params.label: Display Width
params.name: disp_w
params.tooltip: Width of the image the particles splat onto, in pixels. Wire this from the canvas size so the splat matches your output resolution.

params.label: Display Height
params.name: disp_h
params.tooltip: Height of the image the particles splat onto, in pixels. Wire this from the canvas size so the splat matches your output resolution.

params.label: Projection
params.name: mode
params.tooltip: Perspective gives true depth and hides particles behind the camera. Orthographic keeps everything flat and wraps the edges so the field tiles without a visible seam.

params.label: Energy per Particle
params.name: scaled_energy
params.tooltip: How much brightness each particle deposits where it lands. Higher makes a denser, more glowing splat.

nodes.purpose: Fused 3D→2D camera projection + atomic-add scatter. Takes 3D particles and a Camera; projects each particle through orthographic (with toroidal wrap) or perspective camera math; atomic-adds scaled_energy into a 2D u32 accumulator. Pair downstream with node.resolve_scatter → texture for display. Sibling to node.draw_particles (2D in/2D out) and node.draw_particles_3d (3D in/3D out) — this one bridges 3D in to 2D-grid out via a camera. Used by FluidSim3D's display path.
nodes.summary: Projects 3D particles through a camera and splats them onto a 2D image in one step. The display path for a 3D particle sim.

nodes.label: Draw Rectangles

params.label: Color
params.name: color
params.tooltip: The colour of every rectangle drawn on top of the image.

params.label: Alpha
params.name: alpha
params.tooltip: How opaque the rectangles are. The blend is additive so even at full alpha the rectangles add their colour to whatever is underneath rather than fully covering it.

params.label: Rect Count
params.name: rect_count
params.tooltip: How many rectangles from the list to draw. It is safe to leave high since empty zero size entries are skipped automatically.

nodes.purpose: Instanced filled-rectangle overlay composited onto a source texture. Each item in the input Channels[X, Y, WIDTH, HEIGHT] array draws one axis-aligned filled rectangle with the configured color and alpha. Additive blend. Zero-size items are skipped. For gauge fills, center dots, debug regions, VU meters, status bars, selection boxes.
nodes.summary: Draws a batch of filled rectangles onto the image from a list of positions and sizes. Good for bars, blocks, and data overlays.

nodes.label: Draw Scanlines
nodes.purpose: Composite a subtle repeating horizontal scanline pattern over the whole image, additively. period_px sets the line spacing in output pixels; intensity sets how much brightness each line adds. The monitor-glass screen treatment that finishes a HUD look.
nodes.summary: Adds faint monitor-style scanlines across the whole image.

nodes.label: Draw Ticks
nodes.purpose: Draw four alternating long/short tick marks down the right side of every detection in a Channels[X, Y, WIDTH, HEIGHT] array, additively over the source at half intensity. Tick spacing follows the detection's height; lengths and offset are in 1080p-reference pixels. The scale-readout garnish of a tracking HUD.
nodes.summary: Draws small measurement-style tick marks beside every tracked object.

nodes.label: Edge Detect

params.label: Amount
params.name: amount
params.tooltip: Crossfades the outline back over the source. 0 leaves the image untouched and 1 shows the bare edges on black.

params.label: Threshold
params.name: threshold
params.tooltip: How strong an edge has to be before it draws. Raise it to keep only the boldest outlines and drop the fine texture.

nodes.purpose: Sobel 3×3 edge detection with smoothstep threshold, crossfaded against the source by amount. Brightness-based; no glow — chain with Bloom or Halation for glow.
nodes.summary: Finds the edges in the image and draws them as bright lines on dark, a Sobel outline. Crossfade it back over the source for a sketch look.

nodes.label: Edge Pairs

params.label: Vertex Count
params.name: count
params.tooltip: How many points to connect in order. Wire it from a variable point source to grow or shrink the shape live.

params.label: Closed
params.name: closed
params.tooltip: When on, joins the last point back to the first to close the loop, giving a full polygon. Turn it off for an open line that leaves the ends apart.

params.label: Edge Buffer Capacity
params.name: max_capacity
params.tooltip: The most points this can ever connect, which sizes the buffer. Set it once to cover your largest shape and leave it alone.

nodes.purpose: Generate consecutive-pair edge topology [(0,1), (1,2), …, (N-2, N-1)] from a vertex count, optionally closed via (N-1, 0). The polyline-topology atom: pair with a points source (generate_range + array_math + pack_curve_xy, or any custom CurvePoint producer) and node.draw_lines to draw a closed regular polygon, an open polyline, or any topology where edges connect consecutive vertex indices. `count` is port-shadow-param so an upstream variable-N source (e.g. a mux driving the active polygon side count) drives the active edge count. Output capacity is `max_capacity`; slots beyond the active count are EdgePair::SENTINEL so downstream draw_lines filters them out — drawing exactly the active topology, never garbage from the inactive tail.
nodes.summary: Connects a list of points in order into a single line, pairing each point with the next. Can close the loop back to the start.

nodes.label: Edge Slope

params.label: Channel
params.name: channel
params.tooltip: Which colour channel the slope is measured from, R, G, B, or A. Use R for a greyscale height field.

params.label: Scale Mode
params.name: scale_mode
params.tooltip: How the slope is scaled. Texel measures the raw pixel-to-pixel difference and suits normal maps and heightfields. UV scales by the image size so the result is in per-UV-unit space, which fluid-sim flow needs.

params.label: Wrap Mode
params.name: wrap_mode
params.tooltip: How edge pixels read their neighbours. Clamp holds the edge for normal images. Repeat wraps around to the far side, which you want for a tiling or cyclic fluid field.

nodes.purpose: Per-pixel central-difference gradient of a single input channel. Output: (dx, dy, 0, 1) in RGBA. `scale_mode` selects Texel-space (`(R - L) * 0.5` — default, matches oily-fluid / heightmap-to-normal usage) or UV-space (`(R - L) * W * 0.5` per-axis — multiplies by the dimension halves so output is in per-UV-unit space, what fluid-sim gradient-rotate needs). `wrap_mode` selects Clamp (default, bilinear-clamp sampler) or Repeat (toroidal sampler for cyclic fluid sims). The standard vec2 gradient atom: feeds Sobel edge detectors, fluid-sim curl-from-color extraction, heightmap→normal pipelines, reaction-diffusion flow seeding.
nodes.summary: Measures how fast a value changes across the image, giving the direction and steepness of edges. The base for normal maps and edge effects.

nodes.label: Edge Slope (3D)

params.label: Volume Resolution
params.name: vol_res
params.tooltip: The width and height of the volume in voxels, from 16 to 512. Match it to the density volume you are feeding in.

params.label: Volume Depth
params.name: vol_depth
params.tooltip: How many slices deep the volume is, from 16 to 512. Match it to the density volume you are feeding in.

nodes.purpose: 6-tap central-difference gradient of a scalar density Texture3D, written as a vec3 Texture3D. Toroidal wrap (XY use vol_res, Z uses vol_depth); gradient = float3(dx, dy, dz) * 0.5 in integer voxel space. 3D sibling of node.edge_slope. Decomposed from the gradient half of the legacy fused node.fluid_gradient_curl_3d; pair with node.swirl_force_3d for the FluidSim3D force field.
nodes.summary: Measures how fast a value changes through a 3D volume, giving a direction at every point. Used to find flow and forces inside a fluid sim.

nodes.label: Edge Stretch

params.label: Width
params.name: width
params.tooltip: How wide the centre strip is before it gets smeared to the edges. Smaller values pin a thinner strip and stretch it further.

params.label: Direction
params.name: direction
params.tooltip: Which way to smear, with Horiz stretching the middle row outward, Vert the middle column, and Both at once.
