nodes.purpose: Edge-stretch coordinate generator: clamps the per-pixel UV to a center strip of width `width` on the selected axis (Horiz / Vert / Both) and emits it (R = clamped_u, G = clamped_v). Resampling at these coordinates stretches the edge row/column outward. Pair with node.remap (Clamp) + node.mix (Lerp) — the TD coordinate → remap → blend shape replacing the fused node.edge_stretch kernel.
nodes.summary: Grabs a thin strip across the middle of the frame and smears it out to the edges, the classic slit-scan stretch. It outputs coordinates, so pair it with Remap.

nodes.label: Envelope Decay

params.label: Decay Rate
params.name: decay_rate
params.tooltip: How fast the envelope fades after each trigger. Higher decays quicker.

nodes.purpose: Exponential one-shot decay. Snaps to 1.0 on each integer-edge change of `trigger`, then decays frame-rate-independently (env *= exp(-decay_rate · dt)). Drives clip-trigger envelope modes.
nodes.summary: Snaps to full on each trigger then fades back to zero at a rate you set. The classic one-shot envelope for hits and flashes.

nodes.label: Envelope Follower (A/R)

params.label: Attack (s)
params.name: attack
params.tooltip: How fast the envelope rises when the input gets louder, in seconds.

params.label: Release (s)
params.name: release
params.tooltip: How fast it falls when the input gets quieter, in seconds.

nodes.purpose: Asymmetric attack/release envelope follower on a scalar: tau = attack if in > previous else release; alpha = 1 - exp(-dt / tau) (dt = frame delta seconds; alpha = 1 when tau < 1e-6); out = previous + (in - previous) * alpha. Same EMA shape as node.smoothing but tau switches by direction each frame. attack ranges [0, 5] s (default 0.005), release [0, 10] s (default 0.5). Optional reset_trigger drops the stored `previous` to 0 on an integer-edge change.
nodes.summary: Follows the level of a signal, rising fast on the attack and falling slow on the release, or however you set the two times. The asymmetric version of a smooth.

nodes.label: Explosion Force

params.label: Point X
params.name: point_x
params.tooltip: Horizontal centre of the blast, from 0 at the left edge to 1 at the right.

params.label: Point Y
params.name: point_y
params.tooltip: Vertical centre of the blast, from 0 at the top to 1 at the bottom.

params.label: Amplitude
params.name: amplitude
params.tooltip: How hard the burst pushes. At 0 the field is empty and the node costs nothing, raise it to throw particles harder.

params.label: Envelope
params.name: envelope
params.tooltip: The current strength of the burst from 0 to 1, usually driven by a decay so the impulse fades after it fires. At 0 the field is empty.

params.label: Radius
params.name: radius
params.tooltip: How far the push reaches from the centre point, in fractions of the frame. The force falls off smoothly to nothing at the edge of this radius.

params.label: Time
params.name: time
params.tooltip: Feeds the noise that breaks up the push so it looks organic rather than perfectly radial. Wire your time source in to keep it animating.

nodes.purpose: Produces a per-pixel vec2 force texture for a radial impulse burst around (point_x, point_y) within `radius`. Combines radial outward push, tangent curl, noise-perturbed radial direction, and a `(1-t²)²` falloff envelope, multiplied by `amplitude * envelope`. Sum into a velocity field via node.mix(Add) and let the downstream particle integrator pick up the impulse. Reusable for any 'impulse around a point' — snaps, beat shoves, audio splashes, fluid clip-trigger inject.
nodes.summary: Makes a force field that pushes outward from a point, the field you feed into a particle move to drive an explosion.

nodes.label: Exposure

params.label: Gain
params.name: gain
params.tooltip: Multiplies the brightness. Above 1 brightens, below 1 darkens, and 0 is black.

nodes.purpose: Multiply the input texture's RGB by a scalar gain. Alpha passes through unchanged. The `gain` input port is the standard control-wire shadow of the `gain` param — wire any scalar source (LFO, BeatGate, Luminance, …) to make the gain react in real time.
nodes.summary: Brightens or darkens the whole image by multiplying every colour. Above 1 brightens, below 1 darkens, and 0 is black.

nodes.label: Extrude Curve
nodes.purpose: Extrude a 2D outline curve (Array<CurvePoint>) along +Z into a (steps+1) x cols positions+uv grid, cols = the outline's point count (or +1 when `close` duplicates the first point as the last column for a closed loop). pos(i,j) = (x_j, y_j, depth * i/steps). Normals are left zero — wire node.make_triangles downstream (src_cols=cols, src_rows=steps+1). No end caps in v1 (the extruded solid is open at both ends).
nodes.summary: Pushes a flat 2D shape straight through space to build a 3D extrusion — like a cookie cutter dragged through dough. Turns outlines into solid ribbons or beveled panels.

nodes.label: Facet Normals
nodes.purpose: Recompute exact per-triangle flat normals for an Array<MeshVertex> flat triangle list. One thread PER VERTEX: thread idx reads its triangle's 3 verts (base = 3*(idx/3)) via a buffer gather, computes n = normalize(cross(v1.pos - v0.pos, v2.pos - v0.pos)), and writes vertex idx with that normal. Positions and uv pass through unchanged. A trailing partial triangle (base+2 >= vertex_count) passes through with its existing normal unchanged. The v1 normal-policy reset (D4): wire downstream of a heavy push_along_normals or morph_mesh to trade their approximate/unchanged normals for an exact faceted look.
nodes.summary: Recomputes a mesh's normals from its own triangle geometry, giving flat, faceted shading — the exact fix for a mesh whose normals went stale after a heavy deformation.

nodes.purpose: Legacy type-ID alias of node.noise (Perlin, Detail 4). fBM is octave-summed Perlin, so it's a Detail value on the unified node rather than a separate type; constructs the unified primitive pre-set to 4 octaves, retained so older presets load unchanged.
nodes.summary: Octave-summed Perlin noise for rich fractal texture. The 4-octave Perlin branch of the unified Noise node.

nodes.label: Feedback
nodes.purpose: 1-frame texture delay. Last frame's `in` becomes this frame's `out`. Closes per-frame feedback loops without introducing graph cycles — the loop runs through the StateStore, not through wires. Compose with affine_transform + gain + mix + vignette for stylized-feedback chains, or with custom compute steps for fluid / reaction-diffusion sims. Optional `reset_trigger` zeroes the persistent state texture on integer-edge changes (scene cut + state clear pattern).
nodes.summary: Holds the previous frame and hands it back this frame, which lets you build feedback loops like trails and echoes. Wire its output back into the chain through a blend.

nodes.label: Field Combine

params.label: R Coefficient
params.name: a
params.tooltip: How much of the input's red channel feeds the output value. At 1 the red passes through, 0 ignores it, negative flips its sign.

params.label: G Coefficient
params.name: b
params.tooltip: How much of the input's green channel feeds the output value. At 0 the green is ignored, higher mixes more of it in.

params.label: Constant Offset
params.name: c
params.tooltip: A constant added to the result, useful for shifting the whole field up or down to centre it where you want.

nodes.purpose: Per-pixel scalar field: out.rgb = a * in.r + b * in.g + c, alpha = 1. Projects a 2D coordinate texture (typically uv_field output) onto any linear combination of its channels, with an optional constant offset. All three coefficients (a, b, c) accept scalar wires for per-frame-derived transforms (rotation, aspect, scale).
nodes.summary: Mixes the channels of a coordinate field into one value with weights and an offset. The math step that turns coordinates into a custom gradient.

nodes.label: Film Grain

params.label: Amount
params.name: amount
params.tooltip: How heavy the grain is. Low adds subtle texture, high adds strong noise.

nodes.purpose: Multiplicative white-noise grain: out.rgb = src.rgb * (1 - amount * (1 - white_noise(pixel))). Black stays black, bright areas pick up paper-like texture — simulates paper absorbing paint unevenly. Alpha passes through. The grain pass of Watercolor as a reusable atom; `amount` port-shadows the param.
nodes.summary: Lays fine film-style grain over the image, heavier in the bright areas like real photographic stock. Dial the amount for a subtle texture or heavy noise.

nodes.label: Flash

params.label: Amount
params.name: amount
params.tooltip: How strong the flash is. Wire a beat gate or envelope here for strobes and hits.

params.label: Mode
params.name: mode
params.tooltip: Which way it flashes, toward black, toward white, or a brightness boost.

nodes.purpose: Modulate image brightness by a scalar `amount` in one of three modes: Opacity (col*(1-amount), toward black), White (mix toward white), Gain (col*mix(1,3,amount), brighten 3x). The brightness-apply half of Strobe with the gate factored out — wire node.beat_gate into `amount` for a beat-synced strobe, or an LFO/audio/MIDI for any pulsing flash. `amount` port-shadows the param for live modulation.
nodes.summary: Pulses the whole image brighter, toward white, or toward black from a single amount. Wire a beat gate or envelope into the amount for strobes and hits.

nodes.label: Flatten 3D → 2D

params.label: Projection
params.name: mode
params.tooltip: How the 3D points flatten to 2D. Orthographic keeps a flat scale and ignores depth, while Perspective shrinks far points toward a vanishing point.

params.label: Projection Scale
params.name: proj_scale
params.tooltip: How much the flattened shape is zoomed. Higher values fill more of the frame.

params.label: Projection Distance
params.name: proj_dist
params.tooltip: The camera distance in Perspective mode. Closer distances exaggerate the depth foreshortening, farther ones flatten it out. Orthographic mode ignores it.

nodes.purpose: Project an Array<MeshVertex> (3D positions) to an Array<CurvePoint> (2D pre-aspect curve space) with either orthographic or perspective projection. Output is centred at the origin — node.draw_lines applies the center offset itself, so the convention matches every other Array<CurvePoint> producer (generate_lissajous, etc.). For Wireframe-shaped decompositions: polytope_vertices → Rotate3D → Project3D → render_lines. An optional camera: Camera port overrides both legacy modes (port-shadows-param, docs/CAMERA_AND_LENS_DESIGN.md D3): when wired, every point projects through the same right-handed camera convention node.render_scene uses, so the wireframe path agrees pixel-for-pixel with the scene-renderer family. Unwired, the legacy mode/proj_scale/proj_dist math is bit-identical to before — no migration for existing presets.
nodes.summary: Flattens a 3D mesh down to 2D points using a camera, so you can draw it as lines. The projection step for wireframe rendering.

nodes.label: Flatten 4D → 3D

params.label: Projection Scale
params.name: proj_scale
params.tooltip: How large the flattened shape comes out, default 0.25. Higher fills more of the frame.

params.label: Projection Distance
params.name: proj_dist
params.tooltip: How far the 4D camera sits from the shape, default 3. Small values exaggerate the four-dimensional warp, large values flatten it out.

nodes.purpose: Project an Array<Vec4Vertex> to Array<CurvePoint> via two-stage perspective (4D → 3D collapse with f = proj_dist / (proj_dist - w), then 3D → 2D with s = proj_dist / (proj_dist + p3z)). Bit-exact port of generator_math::project_4d. The 4D-equivalent of node.flatten_3d for Tesseract / Duocylinder decomposition.
nodes.summary: Flattens 4D geometry like a tesseract down toward 3D, the first step in drawing a four-dimensional shape.

nodes.label: Flatten to Camera Plane

params.label: Flatten
params.name: flatten
params.tooltip: How far to squash the particle cloud flat toward the camera. 0 leaves the full volume, 1 presses it into a sheet facing the screen.

params.label: Active Count
params.name: active_count
params.tooltip: How many particles to process, capped at the buffer size. Match it to the live particle count from the rest of the chain.

nodes.purpose: Compress particles toward the camera viewing plane. For each live particle: depth = dot(position - 0.5, cam.fwd); position -= cam.fwd * depth * flatten * 0.1. Collapses a 3D particle volume toward a flat sheet facing the camera (FluidSim3D's flatten control). Takes a Camera input and reads cam.fwd. Decomposed from the flatten step of the fused node.fluid_simulate_3d.
nodes.summary: Squashes a cloud of 3D particles flat toward the camera by a dial-able amount, from a full volume down to a pancake facing the screen.

nodes.label: Flip

params.label: Angle
params.name: angle
params.tooltip: Rotates the mirror line. Set it for a horizontal, vertical, or diagonal flip.

nodes.purpose: Sample input at UVs mirrored across a line through center at `angle` radians. Single-axis 2-fold symmetry (one half visible, other half is mirror). Distinct from `node.transform` fold modes (axis-aligned, both halves visible mirrored) and `node.kaleidoscope` (N-segment radial). Use for tilted symmetry overlays, asymmetric kaleidoscope variants, height-map symmetry in shading chains.
nodes.summary: Mirrors the image across a line through the centre at any angle, so one half becomes a reflection of the other. Set the angle for a horizontal, vertical, or diagonal flip.

nodes.label: Flow Field Noise

params.label: Time Scale
params.name: z_scale
params.tooltip: How fast the flow evolves over time. The default 0.01 drifts slowly, raise it for quicker swirling motion.

params.label: Domain Warp
params.name: warp_scale
params.tooltip: How much the field folds back on itself for swirlier flow. 0 gives a plain direct field and higher values get more turbulent.

params.label: Resolution
params.name: resolution
params.tooltip: What size to generate the field at. Full is sharpest, while half and quarter are 4x and 16x cheaper and upscale cleanly since the flow is low detail.

nodes.purpose: Generate a 2D flow vector field from domain-warped fBM Perlin noise. Zero inputs. Output: Rgba16Float texture with flow_x in R, flow_y in B (G=0, A=1.0) — matches the Watercolor flow-map convention so it composes with UV displacement primitives that read .rb as offset.
nodes.summary: Generates a swirling 2D flow field from layered noise, the velocity field you feed into advect or displace for fluid-like motion.

nodes.label: Flow Lines (LIC)

params.label: Steps
params.name: steps
params.tooltip: How many samples to walk along the flow in each direction. More steps draw longer smears, up to a limit of 64.

params.label: Δt (pixels/step)
params.name: dt
params.tooltip: How far each step travels, in pixels. Steps times this sets the total streak length.

nodes.purpose: Line Integral Convolution. For each pixel, walks N steps forward and N steps backward along the normalised velocity field, weighted-accumulating `source.r` along the path. Output = weighted_sum / total_weight in R; GBA = (0, 0, 1). Steps capped at 64. The classic flow-visualisation atom: pair `source` with a hash-noise texture for streamlines (oily-fluid Lines), or with a derived heightmap for flow-aligned intensity (oily-fluid Flow Field).
nodes.summary: Smears noise along a flow field to reveal its streamlines, turning a vector field into a visible flow texture.

nodes.label: Fractal Noise (per copy)

params.label: Scale
params.name: scale
params.tooltip: How zoomed in the noise is. Higher values pack more detail into the same space so neighbouring copies vary more.

params.label: Z
params.name: z
params.tooltip: Slides through the noise along a third axis. Animate this from time to make the value on each copy drift smoothly.

params.label: Offset X
params.name: offset_x
params.tooltip: Shifts the noise sideways. Move it to pan the whole field or to reach a fresh patch of randomness.

params.label: Offset Y
params.name: offset_y
params.tooltip: Shifts the noise vertically. Move it to pan the field or sample a different part of the pattern.

params.label: Octaves
params.name: octaves
params.tooltip: How many layers of detail to stack. More octaves add finer grain at the cost of more work, and 5 matches the classic fractal noise.

params.label: Lacunarity
params.name: lacunarity
params.tooltip: How much finer each stacked layer gets. Larger values open the cells up and spread the detail apart.

params.label: Gain
params.name: gain
params.tooltip: How much each finer layer adds, from 0 to 1. Lower values keep the result smooth while higher values make it rougher and busier.

nodes.purpose: Sample fractal Brownian motion (multi-octave 3D simplex) at each UV in an Array<vec2<f32>>, emit Array<f32>. Per-instance counterpart to node.noise. For each idx: out[idx] = fbm(vec3(uv * scale + offset, z), octaves, lacunarity, gain). The internal loop matches noise_common.wgsl::fbm byte-for-byte; with the defaults (octaves=5, lacunarity=1.5, gain=0.8) the output is bit-identical to the legacy fbm — DigitalPlants's petal-noise pass relies on this. Port-shadow on scale / z / offset_* so the noise field can be animated from time and LFO wires.
nodes.summary: Gives every copy its own fractal-noise value, a smooth random number per copy you can drive size, colour, or motion with.

nodes.label: Free Camera
nodes.purpose: Free-look perspective camera source. Emits one Camera on `out` from world-space position (pos_x/pos_y/pos_z) and Euler angles (yaw about world up Y, pitch about the camera's right axis, roll about fwd) — the gizmo- and import-friendly authoring mode, as opposed to node.orbit_camera's target-orbit style. At yaw=pitch=roll=0 the camera looks down -Z (right-handed convention). yaw/pitch/roll/fov_y are radians (Angle params; editor displays degrees). All seven spatial inputs are port-shadowed scalar inputs, so any of them can be driven by a beat_ramp or LFO — camera moves are beat-addressable. CPU-only, no GPU dispatch. Pair downstream with any 3D consumer (render_3d_mesh, render_instanced_3d_mesh, render_scene) that takes a `camera: Camera` input.
nodes.summary: A free-look camera positioned and aimed directly with Euler angles, instead of orbiting a target. Gizmo- and import-friendly.

nodes.label: Frequency Ratio

params.label: Index
params.name: index
params.tooltip: Picks a ratio from the table of musical intervals. Each step is a different X-to-Y relationship.

nodes.purpose: Emit two scalars from a curated table of small-integer harmonic ratios. `a:b` maps to a musical interval (1:2 octave, 2:3 fifth, 3:4 fourth, …). Indexing the 10-row table is the clip-trigger primitive for shape generators (Lissajous-style curves) that want clean musically-meaningful closed shapes instead of non-closing scribbles. `index` is port-shadows-param so a counter / trigger source can drive the variety per retrigger.
nodes.summary: Emits a pair of small whole-number ratios from a musical-interval table. Use it for Lissajous curves and similar shapes where the X and Y rates set the form.

nodes.label: Gaussian Blur

params.label: Kernel Size
params.name: kernel_size
params.tooltip: How many samples the blur uses. More is smoother but slower.

params.label: Axis
params.name: axis
params.tooltip: Which direction this pass blurs, horizontal or vertical. Pair the two for a full blur.

params.label: Radius (px)
params.name: radius
params.tooltip: How far the blur reaches, in pixels.

params.label: Address Mode
params.name: address_mode
params.tooltip: What the blur does at the edges of the frame, clamp or repeat.

nodes.purpose: Single-axis Gaussian blur. Pair an H pass with a V pass for an isotropic blur. Two algorithms behind one primitive: Fixed (default) uses precomputed 9/17/25-tap kernels at σ≈2/4/6 with `step` controlling per-tap UV stride — cheap, deterministic, used by Halation / DoF / Bloom / OilyFluid. Dynamic uses the legacy fluid-sim algorithm — sigma = max(radius/3, 1), bilinear tap-pair loop, `radius` is in pixels — required for bit-exact FluidSim2D parity (the perceived stroke width depends on the dynamic curve specifically). Set `radius_mode = Dynamic` and wire `radius` to switch algorithms; `kernel_size` and `step` are ignored in Dynamic mode. Dynamic with radius=0 collapses to a single-tap nearest-neighbor sample — the legacy downsample trick. Linear is the exact port of the classic Blur node's per-axis pass (sigma = radius/2, one tap per pixel offset up to 32, normalized): pair an H and a V pass to replace a Blur node with zero look change.
nodes.summary: A single-axis Gaussian blur. Pair a horizontal pass with a vertical one for an even, soft blur in all directions.

nodes.label: glTF Mesh
nodes.purpose: Read a glTF/.glb file from disk and emit its geometry as a triangle-list Array(MeshVertex) wire. mesh_index=-1 (the default) world-combines the whole default scene — drop a model in and it renders. mesh_index >= 0 selects one mesh (optionally one primitive of it via primitive_index) in LOCAL space, undisplaced by node transforms, for callers that place it themselves via node.render_scene's per-object transform.
nodes.summary: Loads a glTF/.glb model file from disk as mesh geometry, so imported 3D assets flow into the render pipeline like any other shape primitive.

nodes.label: glTF Texture
nodes.purpose: Read one embedded image out of a glTF/.glb file and emit it as a Texture2D wire, so an imported mesh's baked-in albedo/alpha map can feed node.render_scene's base_color_map_N. texture_index selects among document.textures(); color_space picks sRGB (albedo/base-color — the default) vs Linear (normal/metallic/roughness maps) so the hardware linearizes correctly on sample. width/height set the output resolution: the glTF importer sets these to the source image's exact dimensions (a 1:1 stretch), while manual drops resample to the default 1024² until resized.
nodes.summary: Loads an embedded image from a glTF/.glb file as a texture, so an imported model's baked-in albedo/alpha map flows into the render pipeline like any other texture source.

nodes.label: Gradient

params.label: Stops
params.name: stops
params.tooltip: The list of colour stops that make up the gradient, each set at a position along the strip. Add as many as you need up to 16.

params.label: Domain
params.name: domain
params.tooltip: How far along the value range the strip covers. 1 is the normal range, and 2 reaches past the last stop to catch bright overshoots for a thermal look.

nodes.purpose: General N-stop gradient / LUT generator. Emits a 1D piecewise-linear gradient as a texture — texel x → t = x/(width-1) * domain (endpoint-inclusive: texel 0 is exactly the first stop, the last texel is exactly t=domain), evaluated over a Table of `[position, r, g, b]` stops (up to 16). Constant in y, so it's a luminance LUT for node.color_lut, and reusable as a gradient texture anywhere (false-colour, duotone, thermal palettes, gradient-map, UI ramps). Evaluation matches the classic gradient(): clamp below the first stop, lerp between stops, and EXTRAPOLATE the last segment past the last stop — the overshoot that paints HDR blowout highlights when domain > 1.
nodes.summary: Builds a colour gradient as a strip you can use as a lookup table or feed into Gradient Map. Add as many colour stops as you like.

nodes.label: Gradient Map

params.label: Color A
params.name: color_a
params.tooltip: The colour mapped to the dark end of the image. Black areas pick up this colour.

params.label: Color B
params.name: color_b
params.tooltip: The colour mapped to the bright end of the image. White areas pick up this colour.

nodes.purpose: Maps input luminance to a two-stop gradient (color_a at luma 0 -> color_b at luma 1). The gradient-map atom (Blender ColorRamp / TD Lookup with two stops). For richer multi-stop palettes (thermal, etc.) use node.lut1d with a supplied LUT texture.
nodes.summary: Recolours an image by mapping its brightness onto a two-colour gradient — dark areas become one colour, bright areas another.

nodes.label: Grid Edges

params.label: Grid Size
params.name: grid_size
params.tooltip: How many points across each side of the grid. Drive this from the same value as the matching grid points node so the layout and the edges agree.

nodes.purpose: Emit the u-wrap + v-wrap wireframe edge topology for an n × n parametric grid as Array<EdgePair>. Pairs with node.grid_points + node.combine_xyzw to author any (u, v)-parametric surface in the graph (Duocylinder, torus, Klein bottle, geodesic sphere, terrain mesh). Each (iu, iv) cell emits two edges: one toward the next u (with modular wrap) and one toward the next v (with modular wrap). Total edge count = n² × 2. Vertex indexing matches generate_grid_uv's row-major convention (idx = iu * n + iv) so the same grid_size scalar should drive both atoms. CPU-write — sentinel-padded inactive tail, same pattern as node.platonic_solid_edges.
nodes.summary: Outputs the wireframe edges that connect a grid of points, so you can draw the grid as a mesh of lines.

nodes.label: Grid Mesh

params.label: Max Capacity
params.name: max_capacity
params.tooltip: The most vertices the grid can ever hold. Set it once when building the effect since changing it rebuilds the graph, and keep it at or above resolution X times resolution Y.

params.label: Resolution X
params.name: resolution_x
params.tooltip: How many points across the grid. More points give finer detail at the cost of memory.

params.label: Resolution Y
params.name: resolution_y
params.tooltip: How many points down the grid. More points give finer detail at the cost of memory.

params.label: Size X
params.name: size_x
params.tooltip: The width of the grid in world units.

params.label: Size Y
params.name: size_y
params.tooltip: The depth of the grid in world units.

nodes.purpose: Emit a regular NxM grid of MeshVertex items in the XZ plane, sized in world units. Pair with a displacement primitive that perturbs Y from a Texture2D, then route to node.render_mesh. The unlock for MetallicGlass-shaped graphs where the displacement source is wire-controlled.
nodes.summary: Builds a flat grid of points as a 3D mesh, the base for terrain, cloth, and displacement looks. Pair it with Surface Bumps or Push Mesh.

nodes.label: Grid Points (UV)

params.label: Grid Size
params.name: grid_size
params.tooltip: How many steps to sample along each axis, giving you that many points squared. Changing it rebuilds the chain, so set it while authoring rather than driving it live.

params.label: U Max
params.name: u_max
params.tooltip: The end of the U range that gets sampled. Defaults to a full turn, which is the right shape for closed surfaces like a torus, or set it to 1 for flat UV meshes.

params.label: V Max
params.name: v_max
params.tooltip: The end of the V range that gets sampled. Defaults to a full turn, or set it to 1 for flat UV meshes.

nodes.purpose: Emit two Array<f32> outputs (u_values, v_values) sampling a 2D parameter domain [0, u_max) × [0, v_max) at grid_size steps along each axis, flattened to grid_size² entries in row-major order (idx = iu * grid_size + iv). The parametric-surface authoring atom: pair with array_math (Cos / Sin / ScaleOffset) + pack_vec4 + edges_from_grid_uv to author any (u, v)-parametric surface in the graph — Duocylinder, torus, Klein bottle, geodesic sphere, terrain mesh — without a per-surface Rust atom. End-exclusive sampling matches periodic-surface conventions where the wrap edge is supplied by edges_from_grid_uv. CPU-only — runs on the content thread so downstream CPU readers see same-frame writes.
nodes.summary: Outputs a grid of U and V values sampling a parametric surface, the input for building curved meshes and wireframes.

nodes.label: Grid UV Field

params.label: Grid Size
params.name: grid_size
params.tooltip: How many sample points across and down, so the total is this number squared. Changing it rebuilds the chain since it sets the buffer size.

nodes.purpose: Emit an Array<vec2<f32>> of UV positions on an N×N grid in [0,1]² space, sampling each cell at its centre: for idx = row*N + col, uv = ((col+0.5)/N, (row+0.5)/N). Foundational producer for per-instance noise samplers (simplex/fbm per_instance) and topology-wrap primitives (cylinder/torus wrap field). Output capacity equals grid_size² and is pre-allocated at chain-build time from the `grid_size` param.
nodes.summary: Outputs a grid of sample points across the frame as a list, used to drive instanced shapes or sample a field at regular spots.

nodes.label: Hash Field by Seed

params.label: Seed
params.name: seed
params.tooltip: Picks which random layout you get. The same seed always gives the same result, so wire a beat or trigger here to reshuffle the pattern on cue.

params.label: Seed X
params.name: seed_x
params.tooltip: How strongly the seed shifts the field along its first axis before hashing. Changing it gives a different family of random offsets.

params.label: Seed Y
params.name: seed_y
params.tooltip: How strongly the seed shifts the field along its second axis before hashing. Changing it gives a different family of random offsets.

params.label: Mode
params.name: mode
params.tooltip: Hash2 returns a random pair in the red and green channels, good for 2D offsets. Hash1 returns a single random value across red, green, and blue.

nodes.purpose: Hash an input value-field's RG channels with an added scalar seed: seeded = field.rg + seed·(seed_x, seed_y); Hash2 (mode 0) → out.rg = hash2(seeded) in [0,1]^2, Hash1 (mode 1) → out.rgb = hash1(seeded) in [0,1]. The 're-hash a value field by a seed' atom — feed node.voronoi_2d's cell_id output (RG) + a beat_floor seed to get per-cell randoms that re-roll each beat (Voronoi Prism's per-beat content shuffle / visibility). General: any value field, any seed.
nodes.summary: Scrambles a coordinate field by a seed so the same input gives a different but stable random offset per seed. Used to re-randomise a pattern on a trigger.

nodes.purpose: Legacy type-ID alias of node.noise (Random). Constructs the unified noise primitive pre-set to the per-pixel hash branch (R-only output, no octaves), retained so older presets load unchanged.
nodes.summary: Uncorrelated per-pixel white noise, grain and dust and LIC ink. The Random branch of the unified Noise node.

nodes.label: HDR Mix

params.label: HDR Retention
params.name: retention
params.tooltip: How much of the reference image's above-white highlights to keep. At 1 the bright detail stays anchored to the reference, and at 0 the compressed image passes through untouched.

nodes.purpose: Preserve a reference texture's above-1.0 highlight energy through a compressed texture's gain adjustment. Per-pixel: SDR body from `compressed`, HDR portion lerps between compressed's HDR and reference's HDR by `retention`. retention=1 keeps the HDR ceiling anchored to reference; retention=0 passes compressed through unchanged.
nodes.summary: Blends two images while keeping the bright above-white highlights from a reference, so a gain or grade doesn't crush the HDR detail. Reach for it when a process is flattening your highlights.

nodes.label: HDRI Source
nodes.purpose: Read a linear-HDR equirectangular environment map (.exr) off disk and emit it as a Texture2D wire, so node.render_scene's envmap input can be lit by a real-world HDRI capture instead of the procedural node.bake_environment studio. No color_space param — EXR is always linear light, full stop. width/height set the output resolution (default 2048x1024): the decoded source is stretch-blit into that slot every frame, so a lower output resolution trades reflection sharpness for prefilter-convolution cost on node.render_scene's envmap-sampling passes.
nodes.summary: Loads a linear-HDR .exr environment map from disk as a texture, so a real-world HDRI capture flows into node.render_scene's envmap input like any other texture source.

nodes.label: Hue / Saturation

params.label: Hue (deg)
params.name: hue
params.tooltip: Rotates every colour around the colour wheel, measured in degrees.

params.label: Saturation
params.name: saturation
params.tooltip: How vivid the colours are. 0 is grey, 1 is unchanged, higher is more saturated.

params.label: Value
params.name: value
params.tooltip: Overall brightness. 1 leaves it unchanged.

nodes.purpose: HSV colour adjust: rotate hue (degrees), scale saturation, scale value. RGB→HSV→adjust→RGB (TD HSV Adjust / Blender Hue-Saturation-Value). The standalone colour-rotation atom — saturation/value are multipliers (1.0 = unchanged). All three are port-shadowed scalars: wire an LFO / MIDI / driver to sweep hue live. Color Grade composes from this.
nodes.summary: Spins the hue around the colour wheel and adjusts how vivid and bright the image is. The HSV way to recolour.

nodes.label: Hypercube Edges (4D)
nodes.purpose: Emit the wireframe edge topology of a 4D hypercube as Array<EdgePair> — the constant 32-edge bit-flip table. The 4D counterpart of node.platonic_solid_edges. Pair with node.hypercube_points and feed both into node.draw_lines (vertices → points, edges → edges) for a 4D wireframe. No params: a hypercube has one topology; the dimension-morph lives in the vertex positions.
nodes.summary: Builds the wireframe edges of a hypercube — which corners connect. Feed it with the matching hypercube points to draw the 4D cube.

nodes.label: Hypercube Points (4D)
nodes.purpose: Emit the 16 corner vertices of a 4D hypercube as Array<Vec4Vertex>, with a continuous `dimension` control (1..4) that collapses the higher axes toward zero so the shape morphs point → line → square → cube → tesseract. At dimension=4 it is the full tesseract. Pair with node.hypercube_edges and feed both into node.rotate_4d → node.flatten_4d → node.draw_lines (with the `edges` input wired). The 4D counterpart of node.platonic_solid_points.
nodes.summary: Builds the corner points of a hypercube. The Dimension knob morphs it from a flat square up through a cube to a full 4D tesseract — wire it to an LFO to animate the reveal.

nodes.label: Image Folder

params.label: Folder
params.name: folder
params.tooltip: The folder of images to play through. Files are sorted alphabetically and laid out as a strip you scrub with the position knob.

params.label: Position
params.name: position
params.tooltip: Where you are in the folder, from the first image at 0 to the last at 1. Drive it with an LFO to sweep through the sequence.

params.label: Zoom
params.name: uv_scale
params.tooltip: Zooms into the current image. 1 fits it to frame and higher values crop in closer.

params.label: Next
params.name: next
params.tooltip: Steps forward one image each time it fires. Wire a MIDI button or clip retrigger to it.

params.label: Prev
params.name: prev
params.tooltip: Steps back one image each time it fires. Wire a MIDI button or clip retrigger to it.

nodes.purpose: Scrub through a folder of images via a position scalar (0..1). The host sets the folder path on the outer-card String binding; the primitive scans it, sorts alphabetically, and loads slices on demand in a background thread. Built-in aspect-fit + uv_scale matches the legacy MRI volume display so downstream primitives don't have to reinvent it.
nodes.summary: Plays through a folder of images with a single position knob, so you can scrub or sequence stills. Point it at a folder and drive the position.

nodes.label: Inject Burst
nodes.purpose: Fixed-duration burst state machine. On each new `trigger` (when enabled) runs a burst for `duration` seconds emitting active=1, a 0 to 1 phase ramp, and a stable hashed pick point. Drives FluidSim2D's inject mode.
nodes.summary: On each trigger it runs a short timed burst, giving an active flag, a 0-to-1 ramp, and a random spot to inject at. Built for fluid sims that puff in new material on a hit.

nodes.label: Invert

params.label: Intensity
params.name: intensity
params.tooltip: How far to blend toward the inverted image, from 0 for the original up to 1 for the full negative. Values in between give a partial invert.

nodes.purpose: Inverts RGB channels and blends against the source by intensity. Alpha is preserved.
nodes.summary: Flips every colour to its opposite, turning a negative of the image. Blend it part-way for a partial invert.

nodes.label: Kaleidoscope

params.label: Segments
params.name: segments
params.tooltip: How many mirrored wedges to fold the image into. More wedges give a finer pattern.

params.label: Center X
params.name: cx
params.tooltip: Horizontal center of the fold. 0 is the left edge, 1 is the right.

params.label: Center Y
params.name: cy
params.tooltip: Vertical center of the fold. 0 is the top, 1 is the bottom.

nodes.purpose: Kaleidoscope coordinate generator: folds the plane into `segments` mirrored wedges around (cx, cy) and emits the per-pixel sample UV (R = folded_u, G = folded_v). Pair with node.remap (Clamp) to resample a source at the folded coordinates, then node.mix (Lerp) to crossfade — the TD coordinate → remap → blend shape that replaces the fused node.kaleidoscope kernel. Reusable for any radial-symmetry warp.
nodes.summary: Folds the image into a ring of mirrored wedges around a centre point. More segments give finer slices. It outputs warped coordinates, so pair it with Remap to apply them.

nodes.label: Keep In Box (3D)

params.label: Container
params.name: container
params.tooltip: The shape that holds the particles in. None wraps them around the edges, while Cube, Sphere, and Torus bounce them back inside the surface.

params.label: Container Scale
params.name: ctr_scale
params.tooltip: How big the container is inside the unit cube, from 0.2 to 1. Smaller values pull the walls in tighter. This has no effect when the container is set to None.

params.label: Active Count
params.name: active_count
params.tooltip: How many particles from the front of the buffer to contain. Set this to match the live particle count so idle slots are left alone.

nodes.purpose: Post-integration hard containment for 3D particles: toroidal wrap (container = None) or SDF reflect + clamp (Cube/Sphere/Torus). For None: position = fract(position + 1). For an SDF container: when a particle escapes (d > 0) it's pushed back inside along the surface normal, then clamped to [0.001, 0.999]. The 3D sibling of node.wrap_around (torus-only); decomposed from the containment step of the fused node.fluid_simulate_3d. Particle velocity is not touched (the legacy velocity-bounce write was dead state).
nodes.summary: Holds 3D particles inside their container, either wrapping them around or bouncing them back at the edges. The hard boundary after a move.

nodes.label: Levels

params.label: Scale
params.name: scale
params.tooltip: Multiplies the brightness before everything else. The main contrast control.

params.label: Offset
params.name: offset
params.tooltip: Adds or subtracts brightness, lifting or lowering the whole image.

params.label: Lo
params.name: lo
params.tooltip: The black point. Anything below this is clipped to black.

params.label: Hi
params.name: hi
params.tooltip: The white point. Anything above this is clipped to white.

params.label: Gamma
params.name: gamma
params.tooltip: Bends the midtones. Below 1 brightens them, above 1 darkens them.

nodes.purpose: Fused tone-shape atom: out.rgb = pow(clamp(in.rgb * scale + offset, lo, hi), gamma). Alpha pass-through. Collapses the scale_offset_texture → clamp_texture → power_texture trio (and its bandwidth) into one dispatch. Reach for this wherever a per-channel affine remap, clamp, and gamma all appear together — MetallicGlass height/metallic chains, halation bloom shaping, hue ramps.
nodes.summary: Reshapes brightness in one step with scale, offset, a clamp, and gamma. A compact way to lift shadows, crush highlights, or set black and white points.

nodes.label: LFO

params.label: Rate Mode
params.name: rate_mode
params.tooltip: Locks the cycle to the song tempo, or lets it run free in Hz.

params.label: Rate
params.name: rate
params.tooltip: How fast it cycles. When synced you pick a note value like 1/4 or 1/8, otherwise it is measured in cycles per second.

params.label: Speed
params.name: angular_rate
params.tooltip: The free-running speed in Hz, used only when Sync is off.

params.label: Shape
params.name: shape
params.tooltip: The waveform, anything from a smooth sine to a hard square or a random sample and hold.

params.label: Phase
params.name: phase
params.tooltip: Shifts the starting point of the cycle. Range 0 to 1.

params.label: Min
params.name: min
params.tooltip: The value at the bottom of the cycle. Set Min above Max to flip the output upside down.

params.label: Max
params.name: max
params.tooltip: The value at the top of the cycle.

nodes.purpose: Low-frequency oscillator. Emits a scalar on `out`, shaped sine / triangle / saw / square. `rate_mode=Musical` locks the cycle to a musical note rate (1/4, 1/8, etc.); `rate_mode=Free` runs at a continuous `Speed` set in Hz in the editor (stored internally as rad/s — the underlying sine is `sin(seconds * angular_rate)`, matching the legacy generator convention). Output maps the internal `[0, 1]` shape onto `[min, max]` so a single LFO can drive bipolar, biased, or amplitude-scaled targets without a downstream `node.math`. Stateless and seek-safe.
nodes.summary: A smoothly cycling value you wire into any knob to make it move on its own. Pick a waveform like sine or saw, and lock it to the tempo or let it run free.

nodes.label: Light

params.label: Mode
params.name: mode
params.tooltip: The kind of light. Sun throws parallel rays from far away like daylight, Point glows outward from a single spot in the scene.

params.label: Position X
params.name: pos_x
params.tooltip: Where the light sits along X, in world units. For a Sun this anchors the shadow frame rather than the brightness direction.

params.label: Position Y
params.name: pos_y
params.tooltip: Where the light sits along Y, in world units, default 30 so it starts overhead.

params.label: Position Z
params.name: pos_z
params.tooltip: Where the light sits along Z, in world units.

params.label: Aim X
params.name: aim_x
params.tooltip: The X of the point the light looks at. For a Sun this sets the direction the rays travel, for a Point it aims the shadow.

params.label: Aim Y
params.name: aim_y
params.tooltip: The Y of the point the light looks at, setting which way it shines.

params.label: Aim Z
params.name: aim_z
params.tooltip: The Z of the point the light looks at, setting which way it shines.

params.label: Colour R
params.name: color_r
params.tooltip: The red part of the light colour, from 0 to 1. The colour is multiplied by intensity before it reaches the surface.

params.label: Colour G
params.name: color_g
params.tooltip: The green part of the light colour, from 0 to 1. The colour is multiplied by intensity before it reaches the surface.

params.label: Colour B
params.name: color_b
params.tooltip: The blue part of the light colour, from 0 to 1. The colour is multiplied by intensity before it reaches the surface.

params.label: Intensity
params.name: intensity
params.tooltip: How bright the light is, default 1. 0 turns it off, higher pushes more light onto the scene.

params.label: Range
params.name: range
params.tooltip: How far the light reaches, default 30. For a Sun it sizes the shadow area, for a Point it is the distance where brightness halves. Set it to your scene scale.

params.label: Cast Shadows
params.name: cast_shadows
params.tooltip: Whether this light drops shadows. Above 0.5 turns them on, below skips the shadow pass entirely for speed.

params.label: Shadow Softness
params.name: shadow_softness
params.tooltip: How fuzzy the shadow edges are, Hard, Soft, or VerySoft. Contact gives real contact-hardening shadows, sharp where things touch, soft further away. Softer edges look nicer but cost more to render.

params.label: Shadow Bias
params.name: shadow_bias
params.tooltip: Nudges the shadow off the surface to stop a surface shadowing itself, default 0.003. Raise it if you see speckled self-shadowing, lower it if shadows float away from contact points.

params.label: Shadow Resolution
params.name: shadow_resolution
params.tooltip: The detail of the shadow map, default 2048. Higher gives sharper shadows on big scenes, lower runs faster. Rarely needs changing during a show.

params.label: Light Size
params.name: light_size
params.tooltip: Only used in Contact shadow softness. How big the light source is, in world units, default 1. Bigger sizes give softer, wider penumbras away from contact points.

nodes.purpose: Single light source for 3D lighting pipelines. Mode enum picks Sun (parallel rays, ortho shadow frustum) or Point (omnidirectional, perspective shadow frustum). Outputs a Light wire consumed by shading atoms (lambert_directional, blinn_specular, etc.) and shadow-aware mesh renderers (the PBR path lives inside node.render_mesh's material). All scalar params are port-shadow so the light can be animated by LFOs, MIDI, or other control sources. Colour is premultiplied with intensity at emission. Industry-standard Blender / TouchDesigner shape — one node per light, shadow-mapping is a property of the light not a separate pipeline stage. shadow_softness's Contact tier (REALTIME_3D_DESIGN §11 D12) trades the fixed PCF kernel for PCSS contact-hardening: shadows go sharp where the caster touches the receiver and soften with distance, driven by the port-shadowed light_size (world-units light diameter).
nodes.summary: A single light source for 3D scenes, set to a sun for parallel rays or a point for a local glow. Wire it into a material or a mesh renderer.

nodes.label: Linear Gradient

params.label: Center X
params.name: cx
params.tooltip: Horizontal point the gradient pivots around.

params.label: Center Y
params.name: cy
params.tooltip: Vertical pivot point.

params.label: Rotation
params.name: rotation
params.tooltip: The direction the gradient runs, in degrees.

params.label: Softness
params.name: softness
params.tooltip: How sharp or soft the transition is.

nodes.purpose: Directional 0→1 ramp in UV space. Output: RGB = ramp value (0 on the negative side of the rotated axis, 1 on the positive side, smoothstep transition of width `softness` centred at (cx, cy)), A = 1. The straight-line gradient — pairs with masked_mix for fades / wipes, with node.invert to flip direction, or with a 1D LUT (node.lut1d) to remap the ramp into arbitrary value curves.
nodes.summary: A straight light-to-dark ramp across the frame at any angle. The simplest gradient, good for fades, masks, and ramps to drive other effects.

nodes.label: Look-At Camera
nodes.purpose: Look-at perspective camera source. Emits one Camera on `out` from a world-space position (pos_x/pos_y/pos_z) and a world-space target point (target_x/target_y/target_z) — fwd = normalize(target - pos), right/up orthonormalized against fixed world up (0,1,0). The third standard camera authoring mode alongside node.orbit_camera's target-orbit style and node.free_camera's Euler pos+angles. fov_y is radians (Angle param; editor displays degrees). All seven spatial inputs are port-shadowed scalar inputs, so any of them can be driven by a beat_ramp or LFO — camera moves are beat-addressable. CPU-only, no GPU dispatch. Pair downstream with any 3D consumer (render_3d_mesh, render_instanced_3d_mesh, render_scene) that takes a `camera: Camera` input.
nodes.summary: A camera positioned directly and aimed at a target point, instead of orbiting or using Euler angles.

nodes.label: Luminance
nodes.purpose: Average Rec. 709 luminance of the input texture, emitted as a scalar on `out` (range [0, 1]). Bridge from image domain to control domain — wire this into any scalar input to make the effect respond to its own brightness. One frame of latency on the measurement.
nodes.summary: Measures the average brightness of the image and outputs it as a single number. Wire it into a knob to make an effect react to how bright the picture is.

nodes.label: Make Triangles
nodes.purpose: Convert a positions-only NxM Array<MeshVertex> grid into a triangle-list (N-1)*(M-1)*6 vertex stream with finite-difference normals. The adapter primitive between node.grid_mesh (positions) and node.render_mesh (triangle list). For MetallicGlass-shaped graphs: GenerateGridMesh → DisplaceMesh → TriangulateGrid → Render3DMesh.
nodes.summary: Turns a grid of points into a solid mesh of triangles, so a flat field of points becomes a surface you can render.

nodes.label: Masked Mix

params.label: Amount
params.name: amount
params.tooltip: Scales the whole mask up or down so you can crossfade the effect in and out from one knob. At 0 the output is always A regardless of the mask.

nodes.purpose: Per-pixel blend of two textures, weighted by a third texture's red channel. The 'apply X only where Y' compositor: any threshold-style mask wired into `mask` selects where `b` overrides `a`. With no mask wired this behaves like `mix`.
nodes.summary: Blends two images using a third as a mask, applying one only where the mask is bright. The apply-only-where node.

nodes.label: Matcap Two-Tone

params.label: Y Low (shadow)
params.name: color_y_low
params.tooltip: The shadow colour along the vertical axis, used where the surface faces down.

params.label: Y High (highlight)
params.name: color_y_high
params.tooltip: The highlight colour along the vertical axis, used where the surface faces up.

params.label: X Low (left)
params.name: color_x_low
params.tooltip: The colour along the left side of the surface. Set it equal to the right colour to drop the horizontal tint.

params.label: X High (right)
params.name: color_x_high
params.tooltip: The colour along the right side of the surface.

nodes.purpose: Cross-axis 4-colour matcap from a tangent-space normal map. Per pixel: mc=n.xy*0.5+0.5, base=mix(y_low, y_high, mc.y), side=mix(x_low, x_high, mc.x), out=(base+side)*0.5. Two 2-tone gradients per axis combined for a 4-corner matcap look. Defaults reproduce oily-fluid's PBR base palette (deep purple → pale blue Y axis, magenta → teal X axis).
nodes.summary: Shades a surface by mapping its normals into a two-tone sphere lookup, a fast stylised material that needs no real lights.

nodes.label: Math

params.label: A
params.name: a
params.tooltip: The first value in.

params.label: B
params.name: b
params.tooltip: The second value in.

params.label: Operation
params.name: op
params.tooltip: What to do with the two values, like add, multiply, min, or max.

nodes.purpose: Scalar arithmetic. Combines two control signals into one with the selected op (add / subtract / multiply / divide / min / max / atan2 / sin / cos / reciprocal / floor / ceil / modulo / exp2 / sqrt). Composition glue for control wires. `b` is unused for unary ops (sin, cos, reciprocal, floor, ceil, exp2, sqrt). Both `a` and `b` are port-shadows-param: when an input wire isn't connected the inline param value is used, so constants can be set on the node without dragging a Value node in.
nodes.summary: Combines two control signals into one with a chosen op, like add, multiply, min, or max. The basic calculator for modulation.

nodes.label: Mesh Edges
nodes.purpose: Emit the per-triangle wireframe edge topology of a flat triangle-list Array<MeshVertex> as Array<EdgePair>: triangle t contributes (3t, 3t+1), (3t+1, 3t+2), (3t+2, 3t). Pure index arithmetic — glTF meshes are unindexed flat triangle lists (gltf_load expands indices), so no vertex data is read and edge count equals vertex count. Pair with node.flatten_3d (same mesh) into node.draw_lines for a true mesh wireframe of any imported or procedural triangle mesh. Interior edges shared by two triangles are emitted twice (flat verts carry no adjacency) — under draw_lines' additive blend shared edges draw ~2x brighter; dedup would need adjacency reconstruction (future work if renders demand it). `active_count` (input-only) overrides the vertex count used for topology when the source buffer's capacity exceeds the asset's real loaded vertex count — when unwired, edge count tracks the BUFFER capacity, which produces degenerate (0,0)-style dot edges from the zero-filled tail if max_capacity was sized larger than the asset.
nodes.summary: Outputs the wireframe edges of a triangle mesh, so any imported model can be drawn as lines. The mesh counterpart of Grid Edges.

nodes.label: Mesh Ramp
nodes.purpose: Compute a per-vertex growth-mask weight from a spatial axis sweep over an Array<MeshVertex>. Source of weights, not a deformer: m = measure(pos - origin) along `axis` (X/Y/Z signed coordinate, Radial XZ cylindrical radius, or full Distance magnitude), t = clamp((m - bound_min) / (bound_max - bound_min), 0, 1), w = 1 - smoothstep(phase, phase + feather, t), optionally inverted. Wire the `weights` output into any deformer's optional weights port and sweep `phase` (e.g. from a beat ramp) so the effect grows progressively across the mesh instead of applying uniformly everywhere.
nodes.summary: Turns a mesh's own positions into a growth mask — a value from 0 to 1 per vertex that sweeps across the mesh along an axis. Feeds any deformer's weight input to make effects grow progressively.

nodes.label: Mirror

params.label: Mode
params.name: mode
params.tooltip: Which fold to apply, from a simple Mirror or FlipY to a four-way QuadMirror or a FoldBoth kaleidoscope. This node outputs the folded coordinates, so send it into Remap to fold a picture.

nodes.purpose: Mirror/fold coordinate generator: rewrites the per-pixel UV via an axis flip or kaleidoscope-style fold (Identity / Mirror / MirrorX / MirrorY / FlipY / QuadMirror / FoldX / FoldY / FoldBoth) and emits it (R = folded_u, G = folded_v). Resampling at these coordinates produces the mirrored / folded image. Pair with node.remap (Clamp) + node.mix (Lerp) — the TD coordinate → remap → blend shape replacing the fused TransformFX mirror modes. The affine half (translate/scale/rotate) stays in node.transform.
nodes.summary: Folds the image back on itself for mirror reflections, from a simple flip to a four-way quad mirror. It produces the folded coordinates, so feed it into Remap to apply the fold to a picture.

nodes.label: Mix

params.label: Amount
params.name: amount
params.tooltip: How much to crossfade from the first image to the second.

params.label: Blend Mode
params.name: mode
params.tooltip: How the two images combine, like Add, Screen, Multiply, or Overlay.

nodes.purpose: Combine two textures with one of 8 blend modes (Lerp, Screen, Add, Max, Multiply, Difference, Overlay, Divide), crossfaded back against A by `amount`. At amount=0 returns A unchanged; at amount=1 returns the full blended result. Lerp mode is a pure linear crossfade. Divide mode is per-channel `a / b`, guarded against divide-by-near-zero (returns 0 when `b` is below epsilon — same convention as `node.array_math` Divide).
nodes.summary: Blends two images together with a choice of modes like Add, Screen, Multiply, and Overlay, plus a crossfade amount. The core layer-blend node.

nodes.label: Morph Mesh
nodes.purpose: Static two-mesh lerp between two Array<MeshVertex>s, by index. n = min(count_a, count_b); pos = mix(a, b, t * w), normal = normalize(mix(a.normal, b.normal, t * w)), uv from `a`. `w` is the optional per-vertex `weights` input (a short or unwired weights buffer degrades to 1.0, never silent 0). Correspondence is by index — meaningful between variants of one mesh (a low-poly and a scanned-detail version of the same object) or as a deliberate scramble-morph between unrelated meshes of similar vertex count; both are stage-valid. Normals are approximate (lerp + renormalize), correct-looking for moderate blends. This is the static two-mesh lerp only — glTF morph-target playback is a separate future design, not an extension of this atom.
nodes.summary: Blends smoothly between two meshes vertex-by-vertex, so one shape dissolves into another. Works best when both meshes share the same vertex count and layout.

nodes.label: Motion Blur
nodes.purpose: Velocity-directed gather motion blur (thin-shutter model, docs/CINEMATIC_POST_DESIGN.md D4): smear_px = velocity_ndc * 0.5 * viewport * (shutter_angle/360), clamped to +/- max_blur_px; output is the average of 8 equal-weight taps of `in`, evenly spaced from uv - smear_uv/2 to uv + smear_uv/2. shutter_angle = 0 (pinhole default) collapses every tap onto the same texel, an exact pass-through. `velocity` expects render_scene's Rg16Float NDC-delta `velocity` output (own-texel read, never filtered). `camera` reads only the wired Camera's lens.shutter_angle (written by node.camera_lens) entirely via derived uniforms — the Camera wire is never a GPU binding, and this atom declares no port-shadowed scalar of its own (wire an LFO/beat envelope into node.camera_lens's own shutter_angle port for live control).
nodes.summary: Smears each pixel along its own screen-space motion, scaled by the camera's shutter angle — the classic filmic motion-blur look, driven by real per-object movement instead of a post-process approximation.

nodes.label: Move Particles (Euler step)

params.label: Active Count
params.name: active_count
params.tooltip: How many particles are alive and being moved.

params.label: Speed
params.name: speed
params.tooltip: How fast the particles move each step.

nodes.purpose: Apply one Euler integration step to each live particle's position.xy by a per-particle 2D force. `position.xy += forces[i] * speed * (delta * 60)`. Frame-rate-normalised via the `* 60` scale so the same `speed` value gives consistent motion across frame rates (matches the legacy fluid_simulate's `dt_scale = dt * 60`). Dead particles (life <= 0) pass through unchanged. No boundary handling — pair with `node.wrap_around` for the toroidal policy.
nodes.summary: Moves every particle one step along its velocity each frame. The basic integrator that makes a particle system actually move.

nodes.label: Move Particles (3D, Euler step)

params.label: Active Count
params.name: active_count
params.tooltip: How many particles to move each frame, counted from the start of the buffer. Match it to your live particle count so dead slots stay put.

params.label: Speed
params.name: speed
params.tooltip: Scales how far every particle travels per step. 1 moves them at the force's natural pace, higher is faster. Wire an LFO or audio band in to drive the energy live.

nodes.purpose: Apply one Euler integration step to each live particle's position.xyz by a per-particle 3D force. `position.xyz += forces[i] * speed * (delta * 60)`. Frame-rate-normalised via the `* 60` scale (matches the legacy fluid_simulate_3d's dt_scale = dt * 60). Dead particles (life <= 0) pass through unchanged. No boundary handling — pair with node.keep_in_box_3d. The 3D sibling of node.move_particles; decomposed from the integration step of the fused node.fluid_simulate_3d.
nodes.summary: Moves every 3D particle one step along its velocity each frame. The integrator for a 3D particle system.

nodes.label: Multi Blend
nodes.purpose: Dynamic N-input per-pixel weighted sum: out = (in_0 + in_1 + … + in_{N-1}) / divisor, all channels. `num_inputs` reconfigures how many Texture2D ports the node exposes; unwired inputs drop out of the sum. Supersedes the fixed five-input node.texture_sum_5.
nodes.summary: Adds together any number of images and divides by a shared amount, collapsing a long chain of Mix(Add) nodes into one. Divisor 1 sums, divisor N averages.

nodes.label: Smooth (neighbors)

params.label: Grid Size
params.name: grid_size
params.tooltip: The width of the square grid the points are laid out on. This has to match the producer feeding it, since that is how each point finds its four neighbours.

params.label: Center Weight
params.name: center_weight
params.tooltip: How much each point keeps its own value versus averaging in its neighbours. 1 leaves it untouched and lower values smooth harder, with 0.2 being heavy smoothing.

nodes.purpose: 5-point cross-neighborhood smoothing of an Array<InstanceTransform> arranged as an NxN grid. Smooths the xyz position; scale and rotation pass through. Border instances fall back to self. Drives plant-stalk-style smoothed motion in instanced renderers — pair upstream with a procedural compute that emits noisy positions, then this primitive cleans them.
nodes.summary: Averages each point with its neighbours on a grid, smoothing out a bumpy field of values or positions.

nodes.label: Nested Cubes Geometry

params.label: Filter
params.name: filter
params.tooltip: How quickly the cubes ease toward their target rotation. Higher values snap faster, lower values glide more slowly.

params.label: Scale
params.name: scale
params.tooltip: Overall size of the cube field. 1 is the default framing, higher fills more of the frame.

params.label: Scatter
params.name: scatter
params.tooltip: How far the faces break apart from each cube. 0 keeps the cubes solid, higher pulls the faces outward.

params.label: Kick Decay
params.name: decay_rate
params.tooltip: How fast the rotation kick fades after each trigger, per second. Higher values make a quick snap, lower values let the kick linger.

nodes.purpose: Render a 5-instance gap-face cube field with EMA-smoothed per-instance Y rotation, per-face scatter, and a per-face envelope-driven kick on each trigger. Isometric orthographic camera. Target angles arrive on a port (Array<f32> length 5) so an upstream cycler or accumulator chooses the rotation behaviour; the kick envelope is internal state.
nodes.summary: Renders a field of nested, rotating cubes with per-face scatter and a beat-driven kick. A self-contained generator, still to be broken into atoms.

nodes.label: Noise

params.label: Type
params.name: type
params.tooltip: Which kind of noise. Perlin and Simplex are both smooth and organic, with Simplex showing fewer grid-aligned streaks. Random is uncorrelated per-pixel grain for film and dither.

params.label: Scale
params.name: scale
params.tooltip: How zoomed in the noise pattern is. Low values give big soft blobs, higher values pack in finer detail.

params.label: Offset X
params.name: offset_x
params.tooltip: Slides the noise field sideways. Wire an LFO in to drift or animate the pattern over time.

params.label: Offset Y
params.name: offset_y
params.tooltip: Slides the noise field up and down. Wire an LFO in to drift or animate the pattern over time.

params.label: Detail (octaves)
params.name: octaves
params.tooltip: How many layers of finer noise stack on top of each other for fractal detail. 1 is a single smooth layer, raise it toward 8 for richer texture. Ignored when Type is Random.

params.label: Lacunarity
params.name: lacunarity
params.tooltip: How much smaller each stacked layer gets than the one before. 2 is the classic setting. Only matters when Detail is above 1.

params.label: Persistence
params.name: persistence
params.tooltip: How much quieter each stacked layer is than the one before. 0.5 gives the familiar natural look, lower makes the fine detail fainter. Only matters when Detail is above 1.

nodes.purpose: Pure generator. Unified 2D procedural noise: `type` selects Perlin (gradient noise, square-grid lobes), Simplex (cleaner gradient noise, fewer directional artifacts), Random (per-pixel wang_hash white noise), or Value (smooth interpolated hash-grid noise — soft, slightly blobby; the classic `fract(sin)`-free value-noise with the 123.34/456.21/45.32 hash, matching the Latent Space website mosh field). `octaves` (Detail) stacks frequencies into fBM for Perlin/Simplex/Value (octaves=1 is single-octave; >1 sums lacunarity/persistence-scaled octaves). Output remapped to [0, 1]. Perlin/Simplex/Value broadcast to RGB (A=1); Random writes R only (G=B=0), matching the legacy hash field. Merges and replaces node.perlin_noise_2d / node.simplex_noise_2d / node.fbm_2d / node.hash_noise_field_2d (those type-IDs alias here).
nodes.summary: Procedural noise in one node. Pick the Type, set the Scale, and raise Detail to stack octaves into rich fractal noise. Perlin, Simplex, and Value are smooth and organic for clouds, terrain, and slow fields; Random is per-pixel grain for film and dither.

nodes.label: Normalize
nodes.purpose: Per-pixel safe-normalize of the input's RG channels treated as a vec2. Writes (v/length(v), 0, 1) when length ≥ 1e-6, else (0, 0, 0, 1). Direction-only — restores magnitude downstream with `node.exposure`. The building block for curl-force extraction (normalize gradients before summing in fluid-sim velocity steps) and any flow-field that wants directional uniformity regardless of source magnitude.
nodes.summary: Scales the red and green channels read as a 2D vector down to length 1, keeping the direction and dropping the magnitude.

nodes.label: One Euro Filter

params.label: Min Cutoff (Hz)
params.name: min_cutoff
params.tooltip: How much to smooth when the signal is still. Lower is smoother but laggier at rest.

params.label: Beta (speed coeff)
params.name: beta
params.tooltip: How much to ease off the smoothing as the signal moves faster. Higher keeps fast moves crisp.

params.label: Derivative Cutoff (Hz)
params.name: d_cutoff
params.tooltip: Smoothing on the speed estimate itself. Usually left alone.

nodes.purpose: Adaptive temporal low-pass (1€ filter) on a Channels array. Low cutoff when the signal is still (heavy smoothing, eliminates jitter); raises cutoff when the signal moves fast (responsive tracking, no perceptible lag). Per-channel per-sample independent filter. Wire detection regions, DNN depth scalars, audio bins, or any noisy signal that needs temporal stabilisation.
nodes.summary: Smooths a jittery signal but lets fast moves through cleanly, so it removes noise without the laggy feel of a plain smooth. Great for hand-tracked or sensor input.

nodes.label: Optical Flow

params.label: Analysis Max Dim
params.name: analysis_max_dim
params.tooltip: The longest side the image is shrunk to before flow is measured, in pixels. Smaller is faster and softer while larger catches finer motion at more cost.

params.label: Update Interval (frames)
params.name: update_interval
params.tooltip: How many frames between each flow measurement. Higher values save work but the motion lags further behind the picture.

nodes.purpose: Dense optical flow (Farneback + global motion compensation) via the MiDaS native plugin. Wraps FfiDepthEstimator::compute_flow on a background worker that holds the previous frame internally and pairs it with the current. Input: any Texture2D. Outputs: (a) Rgba16Float flow map with R=flow_x, G=confidence, B=flow_y, A=valid_mask (R/B layout matches node.flow_field_noise and node.uv_displace_by_flow); (b) scalar cut_score — global-motion-compensated frame-difference, crosses ~0.28 on hard scene cuts, near zero on continuous motion.
nodes.summary: Measures how the image is moving between frames and outputs that motion as a flow field. Drive a displace or advect with it to push pixels along the motion.

nodes.label: Orbit Camera

params.label: Orbit
params.name: orbit
params.tooltip: Swings the camera left and right around the target, in degrees. This is the horizontal angle of the orbit.

params.label: Tilt
params.name: tilt
params.tooltip: Raises and lowers the camera over the target, in degrees. Positive looks down from above, negative looks up from below.

params.label: Distance
params.name: distance
params.tooltip: How far the camera sits from the target. Larger values pull back for a wider view, smaller values move in close.

params.label: FOV Y
params.name: fov_y
params.tooltip: The vertical field of view in degrees. Wider angles take in more of the scene and exaggerate perspective, narrower angles flatten it.

params.label: Look Y
params.name: look_y
params.tooltip: Raises or lowers the point the camera looks at, shifting the whole framing up or down in the scene.

params.label: Roll
params.name: roll
params.tooltip: Tilts the camera sideways around its own view direction, in degrees, so the horizon leans. 0 keeps it level.

params.label: Near
params.name: near
params.tooltip: Closest distance the camera can see. Anything nearer is clipped away. Leave it small unless the scene has extreme depth.

params.label: Far
params.name: far
params.tooltip: Furthest distance the camera can see. Anything beyond is clipped away. Leave the default unless the scene is very deep.

nodes.purpose: Orbit-style perspective camera source. Emits one Camera on `out` from five port-shadowed scalar inputs (orbit/tilt/distance/fov_y/look_y): pos = (distance*cos(orbit)*cos(tilt), distance*sin(tilt) + look_y, distance*sin(orbit)*cos(tilt)); target = (0, look_y, 0); world up = (0, 1, 0) — the bit-exact formula every legacy 3D renderer used pre-Camera-port. orbit/tilt/fov_y/roll are radians (Angle params; editor displays degrees), distance ∈ (0.01, 100], fov_y ∈ [0.05, 2.5] rad, look_y ∈ [-10, 10]. Also exposes pos_x/pos_y/pos_z scalar outputs for PBR shading atoms that need the camera world position per pixel. CPU-only, no GPU dispatch. Pair downstream with any 3D consumer (render_3d_mesh, render_instanced_3d_mesh, digital_plants_render, scatter_particles_camera) that takes a `camera: Camera` input — replaces N separate camera scalar params per primitive with one wire.
nodes.summary: A camera that orbits around a target point, with controls for distance, height, and angle. The viewpoint for 3D mesh rendering.

nodes.label: Pack RGBA

params.label: Default R
params.name: default_r
params.tooltip: The value the red channel falls back to when nothing is wired into the red input.

params.label: Default G
params.name: default_g
params.tooltip: The value the green channel falls back to when nothing is wired into the green input.

params.label: Default B
params.name: default_b
params.tooltip: The value the blue channel falls back to when nothing is wired into the blue input.

params.label: Default A
params.name: default_a
params.tooltip: The value the alpha channel falls back to when nothing is wired into the alpha input. Defaults to 1 so the result stays fully opaque.

nodes.purpose: Pack four single-channel textures into one RGBA output by reading the R channel of each input into the matching output channel. Optional inputs default to the constant `default_a` (0.0 for r/g/b, 1.0 for a). Use when an atomic decomposition has computed each channel separately and downstream consumers expect packed data.
nodes.summary: Combines four single-channel images into one RGBA image, one image per colour channel. The opposite of pulling an image apart.

nodes.label: PBR Material

params.label: Base Colour R
params.name: color_r
params.tooltip: The red part of the base colour, from 0 to 1. On metal this also tints the reflections.

params.label: Base Colour G
params.name: color_g
params.tooltip: The green part of the base colour, from 0 to 1. On metal this also tints the reflections.

params.label: Base Colour B
params.name: color_b
params.tooltip: The blue part of the base colour, from 0 to 1. On metal this also tints the reflections.

params.label: Opacity
params.name: color_a
params.tooltip: How opaque the surface is, from fully see-through at 0 to solid at 1.

params.label: Ambient
params.name: ambient
params.tooltip: A flat fill light so shadowed faces are not pure black, default 0.05. Raise it to lift the darks, drop it for more contrast.

params.label: Metallic
params.name: metallic
params.tooltip: How metal the surface reads. 0 is a dielectric like plastic or wood, 1 is bare metal like chrome or gold.

params.label: Roughness
params.name: roughness
params.tooltip: How rough the surface is, default 0.5. Near 0 gives a sharp mirror finish, 1 is fully matte. It never reaches true zero to keep the highlight stable.

params.label: Emission R
params.name: emission_r
params.tooltip: The red part of the colour the surface glows on its own, before intensity scales it.

params.label: Emission G
params.name: emission_g
params.tooltip: The green part of the colour the surface glows on its own, before intensity scales it.

params.label: Emission B
params.name: emission_b
params.tooltip: The blue part of the colour the surface glows on its own, before intensity scales it.

params.label: Emission Intensity
params.name: emission_intensity
params.tooltip: How strongly the surface glows on its own, default 0 for no glow. Raise it to make the material light up regardless of the scene lights.

nodes.purpose: Cook-Torrance microfacet PBR (D_GGX × G_Smith × F_Schlick) + IBL reflection material. The workhorse for realistic 3D surfaces. The bundled 3D mesh renderers evaluate the BRDF per fragment, blend with envmap-sampled IBL, and combine with diffuse + emission. `metallic` blends F0 from dielectric (≈4%) to metal (= base_color); `roughness` controls the microfacet spread (sharp 0.01 to fully rough 1.0). `clearcoat` (0..1) layers a second, always-dielectric (F0=0.04) GGX specular lobe on top — a car-paint/lacquer coat — with its own `clearcoat_roughness`; the base layer is energy-compensated by the coat's Fresnel (KHR_materials_clearcoat, factor-only v1). Outputs one Material on `out`. Requires BOTH a `light` input AND an `envmap` Texture2D wired to the renderer (the conditional-requirement table enforces this at preset-load).
nodes.summary: A physically based material with roughness, metalness, and environment reflections. The realistic workhorse for 3D surfaces.

nodes.label: Peak
nodes.purpose: Peak (max) Rec. 709 luminance of the input texture, emitted as a scalar on `out` (range [0, 1]). Bridge from image domain to control domain — drives knobs that should respond to the brightest spot rather than overall brightness. One frame of latency on the measurement.
nodes.summary: Measures the brightest point in the image and outputs it as a single number. Reacts to the highlights rather than the overall brightness.

nodes.purpose: Legacy type-ID alias of node.noise (Perlin, Detail 1). Constructs the unified noise primitive pre-set to the Perlin branch at single-octave, retained so older presets load unchanged.
nodes.summary: Classic Perlin gradient noise. The single-octave Perlin branch of the unified Noise node.

nodes.label: Person Mask

params.label: Analysis Max Dim
params.name: analysis_max_dim
params.tooltip: The resolution the AI runs at. Lower is faster but rougher.

params.label: Update Interval (frames)
params.name: update_interval
params.tooltip: How often to re-run the detection, in frames. Higher saves performance.

params.label: Temporal Smoothing
params.name: smoothing
params.tooltip: How much to smooth the mask over time to reduce flicker.

nodes.purpose: Person / human segmentation via the native plugin's process_subject_mask API. Detects PEOPLE specifically (selfie / human / person model variants), not generic salient objects. Input: any Texture2D frame. Output: Rgba16Float mask where R = G = B = person probability ∈ [0, 1] (0 = background, 1 = person); A = 0 until the first inference completes, 1 afterwards (availability gate). Inference runs on a background worker with ~2-3 frame latency. Temporal blending (α = 0.55 default) reduces noise worker-side before upload — matches the legacy WireframeDepth contract. Same channel pack as depth_estimate_midas so they compose interchangeably as mask inputs.
nodes.summary: Finds people in the image with an AI model and outputs a mask that is white on the person and black elsewhere. Use it to cut someone out or key effects to them.

nodes.label: Phong Material

params.label: Colour R
params.name: color_r
params.tooltip: Red component of the surface colour, from 0 to 1.

params.label: Colour G
params.name: color_g
params.tooltip: Green component of the surface colour, from 0 to 1.

params.label: Colour B
params.name: color_b
params.tooltip: Blue component of the surface colour, from 0 to 1.

params.label: Opacity
params.name: color_a
params.tooltip: How opaque the surface is. 1 is solid, lower values let what is behind show through.

params.label: Ambient
params.name: ambient
params.tooltip: How lit the shadowed side stays. 0 leaves unlit faces fully dark for hard contrast, and higher values raise the floor so the whole object reads. Around 0.3 gives a half-lit look.

params.label: Specular R
params.name: specular_color_r
params.tooltip: Red component of the highlight colour, from 0 to 1. Tint it to colour the shine.

params.label: Specular G
params.name: specular_color_g
params.tooltip: Green component of the highlight colour, from 0 to 1. Tint it to colour the shine.

params.label: Specular B
params.name: specular_color_b
params.tooltip: Blue component of the highlight colour, from 0 to 1. Tint it to colour the shine.

params.label: Specular Power
params.name: specular_power
params.tooltip: How tight the shiny highlight is. Around 1 is very soft and almost matte, 32 is a normal glossy spot, and 256 is a tiny pinpoint glint.

params.label: Emission R
params.name: emission_r
params.tooltip: Red component of the glow colour the surface gives off on its own, from 0 to 1. Needs Emission Intensity above 0 to show.

params.label: Emission G
params.name: emission_g
params.tooltip: Green component of the glow colour the surface gives off on its own, from 0 to 1. Needs Emission Intensity above 0 to show.

params.label: Emission B
params.name: emission_b
params.tooltip: Blue component of the glow colour the surface gives off on its own, from 0 to 1. Needs Emission Intensity above 0 to show.

params.label: Emission Intensity
params.name: emission_intensity
params.tooltip: How brightly the surface glows on its own, independent of any light. 0 means no glow, and higher values make it self-lit.

nodes.purpose: Lambert diffuse + Blinn-Phong specular material. Cheap baseline for lit 3D surfaces. The bundled 3D mesh renderers compute per-fragment N·L diffuse and (N·H)^power specular, combine with the wired light's colour, and mix the ambient floor. Outputs one Material on `out`. Requires a `light` input wired to the renderer (Phong needs an illumination source; the conditional-requirement table enforces this at preset-load).
nodes.summary: A basic shiny material with soft diffuse shading and a sharp highlight. The cheap go-to for lit 3D surfaces.

nodes.label: Platonic Solid Edges

params.label: Shape
params.name: shape
params.tooltip: Which of the five Platonic solids to outline, from Tetra and Cube through to Icosa and Dodeca. Drive the matching points node from the same value so corners and edges agree.

nodes.purpose: Emit the wireframe edge topology of one of the five Platonic solids as Array<EdgePair>. Curated-enum atom — one CPU write of a static per-shape adjacency table, sentinel-padded for the inactive tail. Pair with node.platonic_solid_points (driving both from the same shape scalar) and feed both into node.draw_lines (vertices → points, edges → edges) for a 3D wireframe.
nodes.summary: Builds the wireframe edges of one of the five Platonic solids, pairing up which corners connect. Feed it with the matching points to draw the wireframe.

nodes.label: Platonic Solid Points

params.label: Shape
params.name: shape
params.tooltip: Which of the five Platonic solids to build, from Tetrahedron through Cube, Octahedron, Icosahedron, to Dodecahedron. Drive the same choice into the matching edges node so corners and lines agree.

nodes.purpose: Emit the vertex set of one of the five Platonic solids (Tetrahedron / Cube / Octahedron / Icosahedron / Dodecahedron) as Array<MeshVertex>. Curated-enum atom — one GPU dispatch with closed-form per-shape coordinates baked into WGSL, normalised to magnitude 0.25 (the legacy screen-friendly default).
