Pair with node.platonic_solid_edges (driving both from the same shape scalar) and feed both into node.rotate_3d → node.flatten_3d → node.draw_lines for a 3D wireframe.
nodes.summary: Builds the corner points of one of the five Platonic solids, from a tetrahedron to a dodecahedron. The vertex set for wireframe geometry.

nodes.label: Polar Field

params.label: Center X
params.name: cx
params.tooltip: The horizontal centre that angle and distance are measured from, where 0.5 is the middle of the frame.

params.label: Center Y
params.name: cy
params.tooltip: The vertical centre that angle and distance are measured from, where 0.5 is the middle of the frame.

nodes.purpose: Pure generator. Per-pixel polar coordinates around a configurable center: R = angle (atan2, normalized to [0,1]), G = radius (UV distance), B = 0, A = 1. Building block for spirals, rotations, kaleidoscopes, and any radial composition.
nodes.summary: Outputs each pixel's angle and distance from a centre instead of its X and Y. The base for spirals, tunnels, and kaleidoscopes.

nodes.label: Position Jitter

params.label: Frequency
params.name: frequency
params.tooltip: How tightly the noise varies across the copies. Higher values make neighbouring copies wander in different directions, lower values move whole regions together.

params.label: Amplitude
params.name: amplitude
params.tooltip: How far each copy is pushed off its spot. 0 leaves the grid perfect, higher values scatter the copies further.

params.label: Time UV.x Drift
params.name: time_uvx_drift
params.tooltip: Slides the noise pattern along over time so the scatter animates. Drive it from a time wire for slow drift.

params.label: Z Coord
params.name: z_coord
params.tooltip: Picks which slice of the noise field to sample. Animate it from time to make the whole pattern evolve in place rather than slide.

params.label: Axis Seed
params.name: axis_seed
params.tooltip: Separates the noise used for each axis so the three directions wobble independently. Move it to land in a different part of the noise.

nodes.purpose: Add 3-axis 3D-simplex position noise to each InstanceTransform's pos.xyz, leaving scale and rotation unchanged. base = (uv.x*freq + time_uvx_drift, uv.y*freq, z_coord); pos += amp · (simplex(base), simplex(base + (seed,0,0)), simplex(base + (0,seed,0))). Generic — any instanced field that wants organic per-instance position wobble. Reproduces both legacy DigitalPlants detail- and micro-noise patterns when parameterised.
nodes.summary: Adds a random offset to each copy's position with noise, so a perfect grid of copies looks more natural and scattered.

nodes.label: Posterize

params.label: Levels
params.name: levels
params.tooltip: How many brightness steps each colour gets crushed into. Fewer levels give a chunkier look.

nodes.purpose: Posterize: quantize each RGB channel to `levels` discrete steps (round to nearest, endpoints included). Alpha pass-through. The standalone quantize atom (TD / Blender posterize) — `levels` is a port-shadowed scalar so a beat/LFO can crush the palette rhythmically. Dither composes from this.
nodes.summary: Crushes each colour into a small number of steps for a banded, blocky look. Fewer levels give a chunkier result.

nodes.label: Power

params.label: Exponent
params.name: exponent
params.tooltip: Raises each value to this power. Above 1 pushes the midtones toward black for sharper points, and below 1 lifts the darks like a gamma brighten. Negative input is clamped to 0 first.

nodes.purpose: Per-pixel pow(max(input.rgb, 0), exponent). Alpha passes through. Sharpens or softens a [0, 1] field: exponent > 1 pushes mid-grays toward 0 (great for spiking voronoi F1 into star-points), exponent < 1 lifts darks (gamma-like brightening).
nodes.summary: Raises each value to a power, which sharpens or softens a 0-to-1 field. Above 1 pushes toward black, below 1 lifts the midtones.

nodes.label: Push Along Normals
nodes.purpose: Displace each vertex of an Array<MeshVertex> outward (or inward) along its own normal: pos += normal * amount * w * f. `w` is the optional per-vertex `weights` input (from node.mesh_ramp or any weights producer) — a short or unwired weights buffer degrades to 1.0 (full push), never to silent 0. `f` is an optional Texture2D `field` sampled bilinear at the vertex's own UV as (sample.r - field_bias), or 1.0 when unwired. Normals pass through unchanged — approximate at extremes, correct-looking for moderate organic-motion amounts; wire node.facet_normals downstream after a heavy push if the faceted look isn't wanted, or keep amount moderate to keep the source mesh's smooth normals.
nodes.summary: Pushes every point of a mesh outward or inward along its own surface direction — the 3D version of a bulge or breathe effect, optionally masked and driven by an image.

nodes.label: Push From Walls (3D)

params.label: Container
params.name: container
params.tooltip: The shape of the wall particles get pushed away from. None turns the push off entirely, the rest pick a Cube, Sphere, or Torus boundary.

params.label: Container Scale
params.name: ctr_scale
params.tooltip: How big the container is. Smaller packs the particles into a tighter volume, larger gives them more room before the walls start pushing back.

params.label: Active Count
params.name: active_count
params.tooltip: How many particles in the buffer feel the wall push, counted from the front. It is capped to the real particle count.

nodes.purpose: Soft container-boundary repulsion added in-place to an Array<[f32; 3]> force buffer. When a particle is within a 0.1 margin of the container SDF (Cube/Sphere/Torus), a gentle inward force `n * (t*t*0.15)` cushions it back along the outward normal, preventing wall pile-up. container = None disables it. The pre-integration cushion half of the FluidSim3D container behaviour (the post-integration hard wall is node.keep_in_box_3d). Decomposed from the fused node.fluid_simulate_3d.
nodes.summary: Pushes 3D particles gently away from the walls of their container as they get close, keeping them inside without a hard bounce.

nodes.label: Push Mesh

params.label: Columns
params.name: cols
params.tooltip: How many columns are in the source grid. This must match the grid the mesh came from or the height image will sample at the wrong spots.

params.label: Rows
params.name: rows
params.tooltip: How many rows are in the source grid. This must match the grid the mesh came from.

params.label: Displacement
params.name: displacement
params.tooltip: How far the height image pushes each point up or down. 0 leaves the mesh flat, and negative values push the other way.

params.label: Height Bias
params.name: height_bias
params.tooltip: The grey level that counts as no movement, from 0 to 1. At the default 0.5, mid grey stays put while brighter pixels rise and darker ones sink.

nodes.purpose: Perturb the Y component of an Array<MeshVertex> positions grid by sampling a height Texture2D at each vertex's UV. cols/rows describe the source grid topology; UV = (col / (cols-1), row / (rows-1)). For MetallicGlass-shaped graphs: GenerateGridMesh → DisplaceMesh → TriangulateGrid → Render3DMesh.
nodes.summary: Pushes a mesh's points up and down by reading a height image, turning a flat grid into bumpy terrain. The 3D version of a displacement.

nodes.label: Radial Offset Field

params.label: Mode
params.name: mode
params.tooltip: Which way the push points. Radial fans outward from the centre and fades toward the edge. Linear pushes everything the same way along the Angle you set.

params.label: Angle
params.name: angle
params.tooltip: The push direction for Linear mode, measured in degrees. Ignored in Radial mode.

params.label: Falloff
params.name: falloff
params.tooltip: How much the radial push fades from centre to edge. 1 keeps the full centre-to-edge ramp, and 0 makes the push the same strength everywhere. Only matters in Radial mode.

nodes.purpose: Directional displacement field generator. Radial mode: per-pixel direction points outward from the center, scaled by a center→edge falloff mask. Linear mode: a uniform direction at `angle` degrees. Output R = dir.x, G = dir.y (signed), B = 0, A = 1; |dir| ≤ 1. The reusable direction field behind the radial-warp family — feed it as the velocity field to node.rgb_split (chromatic aberration), node.uv_displace_by_flow (lens / zoom warp), node.texture_advect. The displacement magnitude and ± sign are applied by the consumer.
nodes.summary: Makes a push outward from a centre point that other nodes use to shift pixels. It has no look of its own, so wire it into a displace or remap node.

nodes.label: Range

params.label: Start
params.name: start
params.tooltip: The first number in the list.

params.label: End
params.name: end
params.tooltip: The last number in the list, or just short of it when End Inclusive is off.

params.label: Sample Count
params.name: max_capacity
params.tooltip: How many evenly spaced numbers to produce between the start and end.

params.label: End Inclusive
params.name: end_inclusive
params.tooltip: Whether the last value lands exactly on the end. On is right for closed curves that loop back, off is right for spacing points evenly around a circle.

nodes.purpose: Emit an Array<f32> of `count` samples linearly spaced over `[start, end]`. The TouchDesigner Pattern-CHOP analogue. Output is the t-parameter array that drives parametric curve graphs — pair with array_math (ScaleOffset / Sin / Cos) + pack_curve_xy for Lissajous-style curves, or with cycle_table_row + mux for stepped sequences. `start` and `end` are port-shadows-param so an LFO or driver wire can sweep the range dynamically. `active_count` (input-only) overrides the sample count for variable-N curves (regular polygons, variable-resolution sweeps) — when unwired the count is `max_capacity` for backward-compatible fixed-resolution sampling. Output capacity is `max_capacity`. CPU-only — the curve-math atom family runs on the content thread so downstream CPU readers (replicators, polyline stackers) see same-frame writes without a GPU→CPU fence.
nodes.summary: Builds a list of evenly spaced numbers between a start and an end. The starting point for laying out copies, rings, or steps.

nodes.label: Rectangle Mask

params.label: Center X
params.name: cx
params.tooltip: Horizontal center of the rectangle. 0 is the left edge, 1 is the right.

params.label: Center Y
params.name: cy
params.tooltip: Vertical center. 0 is the top, 1 is the bottom.

params.label: Half Width
params.name: half_width
params.tooltip: How far the rectangle reaches from its center to the left and right. At 0.5 it fills the frame.

params.label: Half Height
params.name: half_height
params.tooltip: How far it reaches up and down from the center.

params.label: Rotation
params.name: rotation
params.tooltip: Spins the rectangle around its center. Wire a knob or LFO to turn it over time.

params.label: Softness
params.name: softness
params.tooltip: Fades the edge of the mask. 0 gives a clean hard edge, higher values blur it out.

nodes.purpose: Rotated rectangular SDF mask (Chebyshev distance). Output: RGB = mask value (inside=1, outside=0, smoothstep falloff of width `softness`), A = 1. half_width / half_height are extents from the center (same convention as ellipse_mask's radii). For canvas-spanning bands, set the unbounded axis' half-extent ≥ 1.0; combined with rotation that gives rotated band masks for tilt-shift, scanlines, and letterboxes.
nodes.summary: Draws a soft-edged rectangle you can use to limit an effect to one region of the frame. Position it, size it, rotate it, and soften the edge.

nodes.label: Reinhard Tone Map

params.label: Intensity
params.name: intensity
params.tooltip: Brightens the image before the curve is applied, from 0 up to 16. 1 leaves it as is, higher pushes more of the picture toward the highlights.

params.label: Contrast
params.name: contrast
params.tooltip: A second multiplier on the signal before tone mapping, from 0 to 8. 1 leaves it unchanged.

params.label: Curve
params.name: curve
params.tooltip: Which Reinhard curve to use. Extended keeps more detail in bright highlights, while Simple is the textbook version that crushes them harder.

nodes.purpose: Reinhard tone mapping for HDR display in one of two curves: Extended (default — `x*(1+x/9)/(1+x)`, matches FluidSim bit-for-bit, preserves highlights) or Simple (`x/(x+1)`, the textbook Reinhard curve, matches the legacy MetallicGlass render terminal bit-for-bit). intensity + contrast are port-shadowed pre-multipliers — wire a `node.canvas_area_scale → node.math` chain into `intensity` for resolution-aware brightness compensation in particle-density pipelines. SDR-only — for HDR-aware (PQ / EDR) or alternate curves (ACES / AgX / Khronos PBR Neutral), use `node.tone_map`.
nodes.summary: A simpler HDR-to-display tone map using the Reinhard curve. Lighter weight than the full Tone Map node.

nodes.label: Remap

params.label: Wrap
params.name: wrap
params.tooltip: What happens when the map points outside the image. Clamp holds the edge pixel, Repeat tiles the image, and Mirror bounces it back.

params.label: Field Mode
params.name: mode
params.tooltip: How to read the coordinate map. Absolute treats it as the exact spot to sample, while Relative treats it as an offset added to each pixel's own position so displacement fields stack cleanly.

nodes.purpose: Resample `source` at the per-pixel UV coordinates in `uv_field`'s R/G channels (TouchDesigner's Remap TOP). `mode` = Absolute: out(p) = source(uv_field(p).rg); Relative: out(p) = source(p + uv_field(p).rg) — treat the field as a UV *offset* so it sums cleanly with other offset fields. `wrap` picks the out-of-[0,1] policy (Clamp / Repeat / Mirror). The generic UV-warp atom — pair with a coordinate-field producer (polar_field, centered_uv, scale_offset_texture, block_displace_field, optical flow) to build kaleidoscope / mirror / edge-stretch / chromatic split / glitch displace / lens distortion as a visible `coordinate-math → remap → blend` graph rather than a bespoke single-effect shader.
nodes.summary: Resamples the image through a coordinate map, reading each pixel from wherever the map points. This is the node that turns a Mirror, Kaleidoscope, or any coordinate field into an actual warped picture.

nodes.label: Remove Drift (3D)
nodes.purpose: Subtract the mean of an Array<[f32;3]> per-particle force field (over live particles) from every entry, so internal forces sum to zero and a particle fluid stops accumulating net momentum. Fixes the discrete-grid conservation residue that otherwise drifts a confined sim into a corner (BUG-066). amount 1 = full balance, 0 = passthrough; port-shadowed so it can be performed.
nodes.summary: Balances the forces on a particle system so it stops slowly sliding in one direction — a long-running fluid stays centered instead of silting into a corner.

nodes.label: Render Copies

params.label: Instance Count
params.name: instance_count
params.tooltip: How many copies of the mesh to draw. It is capped to the size of the transform list you wire in.

nodes.purpose: Bundled instanced 3D mesh renderer. Draws N copies of an Array<MeshVertex> base mesh, each transformed by an Array<InstanceTransform> entry. Takes a Camera + Material + optional Light + optional envmap + optional surface textures (normal_map / roughness_map), picks the matching per-MaterialKind fragment shader (Unlit / Phong / PBR / Cel), and emits a shaded `color` Texture2D. Surface textures sample at the base mesh's per-vertex UV (the same channel as render_3d_mesh) — the texture is shared across every instance and stays locked to the geometry as the camera moves. base_color_map (rgba) modulates base colour and supplies the cutout-alpha source; metallic_map.r replaces the material's scalar metallic when wired. Pair with node.arrange_copies to drive NestedCubes / DigitalPlants graphs. Per-kind requirements mirror render_3d_mesh: Unlit needs no light; Phong / Cel need light; PBR needs light + envmap.
nodes.summary: Draws many copies of one mesh in a single pass, each placed by a list of transforms. The fast way to render a field of repeated objects.

nodes.label: Render Mesh
nodes.purpose: Bundled 3D mesh renderer (TouchDesigner / Blender shape). Reads an Array<MeshVertex> as a triangle list, takes a Camera + Material + optional Light + optional envmap + optional surface textures (normal_map / roughness_map), and emits a shaded `color` Texture2D plus optional G-buffer outputs (`world_pos`, `world_normal`). The material's MaterialKind picks the fragment shader — Unlit / Phong / PBR / Cel. Per-kind requirements: Unlit needs no light. Phong / Cel need `light`. PBR needs `light` AND `envmap`. Surface textures sample at each fragment's mesh UV (the per-vertex `uv` channel interpolated through the rasterizer), so the texture sticks to the geometry as the camera moves — the industry-standard mesh-UV pattern. normal_map is interpreted as a world-space signed normal; roughness_map's red channel replaces the material's scalar roughness when wired; base_color_map (rgba) modulates the material's base colour and supplies the cutout-alpha source; metallic_map's red channel replaces the material's scalar metallic when wired.
nodes.summary: Draws a 3D mesh to the screen with a camera, a light, and a material. The final step that turns geometry into an image.

nodes.label: Render Scene
nodes.purpose: Multi-object 3D scene renderer: draws `objects` separate Array<MeshVertex> meshes into ONE shared depth buffer, so nearer objects correctly occlude farther ones. Neither object nor light count has a structural cap — objects generate `mesh_n/material_n` ports by name, and `lights` shared Light inputs ride a runtime-sized `@binding(8)` storage buffer (the `objects`/`lights` sliders are soft editor bounds, default max 64). Each object carries its own material_n: Material and pos/rot/scale transform params. Wired Light inputs accumulate in the Phong/PBR/Cel shading. ONE shared envmap input lights every PBR object in the scene. Reconfigures its port list (mesh_n/material_n pairs, light_0..N) and per-object transform params when `objects` or `lights` changes.
nodes.summary: Draws several 3D objects into one scene so the nearer ones correctly block the farther ones, each with its own position and material, lit by any number of shared lights.

nodes.label: Render Text

params.label: Size
params.name: size
params.tooltip: The text height as a fraction of the frame, default 0.25 for about a quarter of the screen.

params.label: Position X
params.name: pos_x
params.tooltip: Moves the text left and right across the frame, from -1 at the left edge to 1 at the right, 0 is centred.

params.label: Position Y
params.name: pos_y
params.tooltip: Moves the text up and down the frame, from -1 to 1, 0 is centred.

params.label: Scale
params.name: scale
params.tooltip: An extra size multiplier on top of the size setting. 1 leaves it as is, higher enlarges the text.

params.label: H Align
params.name: h_align
params.tooltip: How the lines line up sideways, Left, Center, or Right.

params.label: V Align
params.name: v_align
params.tooltip: How the block anchors vertically, Top, Center, or Bottom.

params.label: Letter Spacing
params.name: letter_spacing
params.tooltip: Extra gap between letters. 0 is the font's normal spacing, higher spreads them apart and negative packs them tighter.

params.label: Line Spacing
params.name: line_spacing
params.tooltip: The gap between lines, default 1.2. Lower tightens the lines, higher opens them up.

params.label: Stroke Width
params.name: stroke_width
params.tooltip: Outline thickness around the letters, as a fraction of the text size. 0 is no outline, higher draws a thicker border.

params.label: Fill Color
params.name: fill_color
params.tooltip: The colour of the letters themselves.

params.label: Stroke Color
params.name: stroke_color
params.tooltip: The colour of the outline drawn around the letters when stroke width is above 0.

params.label: Text
params.name: text
params.tooltip: The words to draw. Wire this through the card so you can change the message live during a show.

params.label: Font
params.name: fontFamily
params.tooltip: The font to use by name. Leave it blank to fall back to the built-in default.

nodes.purpose: Render a text string to the output texture. The host wires `text` and `fontFamily` through preset stringBindings; size/position/scale/alignment/spacing/stroke_width are port-shadows-param scalars; fill_color and stroke_color are editor-set Color params. CPU-rasterizes the glyphs via CoreText into internal R8Unorm coverage bitmaps — a fill mask always, plus an outline mask when stroke_width > 0 (both dirty-cached — only re-rasterized when text/font/size/style change). A compute kernel composites them as premultiplied alpha (fill over stroke over transparent) with aspect correction, so the text keys cleanly over the layer below. Single-node text generator: drop it between `system.generator_input` and `system.final_output`.
nodes.summary: Draws a text string onto the image with a chosen font, size, and position. Wire the text and font through the card so you can change them live.

nodes.label: Repeat Outline (rings)

params.label: Max Rings
params.name: max_rings
params.tooltip: The most copies this node will stack, from 1 up to 32. The actual count is whatever is smaller, this ceiling or the number of scales fed in.

nodes.purpose: Stack K transformed copies of a polyline (outline + edge topology) into one concatenated polyline. Per-ring uniform scale on the outline; per-ring index shift on the edges (sentinel-preserving). The K-fold replication atom for line-based generators: pair a single polygon / Lissajous / Rose curve outline + edges with a `scales` Array<f32> (from generate_range + array_math) to produce concentric, parallax, or stacked variations of the source polyline. The ring count is `min(scales.capacity, max_rings)`; output capacity is `input.capacity * max_rings` so the chain build pre-allocates the full stack at plan time.
nodes.summary: Stacks scaled copies of an outline into concentric rings, turning one shape into a set of nested rings.

nodes.label: Resolve Scatter

params.label: Fixed-Point Scale
params.name: fixed_point_scale
params.tooltip: The divisor that turns the raw counter values back into density. It must match the scale the scatter step wrote with, default 4096, or the brightness comes out wrong.

nodes.purpose: Read a u32 fixed-point accumulator buffer (produced by node.draw_particles), divide by `fixed_point_scale`, and write the result as a grayscale density texture. The bridge from Array(u32) back to Texture2D for downstream texture-domain primitives. Dimensions are taken from the output Texture2D — which the backend allocates at canvas size — so resolve always covers every pixel of the density texture, matching whatever scatter wrote (also canvas-sized via `canvas_sized_array_outputs()`).
nodes.summary: Reads back the buffer that Draw Particles wrote into and turns it into a normal image. The pickup step after a particle splat.

nodes.label: Resolve Scatter (3D)

params.label: Volume Resolution
params.name: vol_res
params.tooltip: Width and height of the volume in cells. This must match the particle scatter that filled the buffer.

params.label: Volume Depth
params.name: vol_depth
params.tooltip: Depth of the volume in cells. This must match the particle scatter that filled the buffer.

nodes.purpose: Read a u32 fixed-point 3D accumulator buffer (produced by node.draw_particles_3d), divide by fixed_point_scale (default 4096, FluidSim3D's legacy FIXED_POINT_MULTIPLIER), and write the result as a density Texture3D. Self-clears the accumulator to zero atomically as part of the same dispatch so the next frame starts fresh.
nodes.summary: Reads back the 3D buffer that a 3D particle scatter wrote into and turns it into a volume you can sample.

nodes.label: Revolve Curve
nodes.purpose: Revolve a 2D profile curve (Array<CurvePoint>, x=radius, y=height) around the Y axis into a profile_len x (segments+1) positions+uv grid. pos(i,j) = (x_i*cos(phi_j), y_i, x_i*sin(phi_j)), phi_j = sweep * j/segments. Normals are left zero — wire node.make_triangles downstream (src_cols=segments+1, src_rows=profile_len) for topology and finite-difference normals. `sweep` is UNBOUNDED (range None) — a saw LFO sweeping past 2*pi and beyond is a valid performer gesture (BUG-039 class); sin/cos absorb the wrap with no seam.
nodes.summary: Spins a 2D profile curve around a vertical axis to build a solid of revolution — a lathe. The classic way to build vases, columns, and bells from a cross-section.

nodes.label: RGB Split

params.label: Amount (pixels)
params.name: amount
params.tooltip: How far the red and blue channels pull apart, in pixels. Negative values swap which way they shift.

nodes.purpose: 3-tap RGB sample of `in` displaced by `velocity` (RG). R samples at `uv - velocity*amount/dims`, G at centre, B at `uv + …`. Alpha follows centre. Different from `node.chromatic_aberration` (radial split): this is FLOW-driven, the offset direction comes from a per-pixel velocity field. Used for normal-map chromatic splits in oily-fluid Oil Slick rendering, signed-field chromatic trails, anywhere displacement direction is data not symmetry.
nodes.summary: Pulls the red and blue channels apart along a direction you feed in, for a chromatic-aberration or glitchy colour-fringe look. The amount is in pixels and can go negative to swap which way they shift.

nodes.label: Rim Light (Fresnel)

params.label: View X
params.name: view_x
params.tooltip: The X part of the viewing direction the rim is measured against, from -1 to 1. The default looks straight down the Z axis at the surface.

params.label: View Y
params.name: view_y
params.tooltip: The Y part of the viewing direction the rim is measured against, from -1 to 1.

params.label: View Z
params.name: view_z
params.tooltip: The Z part of the viewing direction the rim is measured against, from -1 to 1. Default of 1 points the camera straight at the surface.

params.label: Power
params.name: power
params.tooltip: How tight the glowing edge is, from 0.5 for a wide soft rim up to 16 for a thin sharp line right at the silhouette.

params.label: Color
params.name: color
params.tooltip: The colour of the rim glow. This output is additive, so wire it onto your base shading with a Mix in Add mode.

nodes.purpose: Fresnel-based edge highlight from a tangent-space normal map: `f = pow(1 - max(dot(n, view), 0), power)`, output = color.rgb * f. ADDITIVE rim term — black at face-on, `color`-tinted at grazing. Sum with a base shading (matcap, lambert) via `node.compose` mode=Add to layer the rim onto the surface. Defaults match oily-fluid PBR (view=(0,0,1), power=3, color=iridescent magenta).
nodes.summary: Lights up the edges of a surface where it turns away from the camera, the glowing rim you see on backlit objects.

nodes.label: Rotate 3D

params.label: Angle X
params.name: angle_x
params.tooltip: Spin around the horizontal axis, in degrees. Wire an LFO or a beat in to keep it tumbling.

params.label: Angle Y
params.name: angle_y
params.tooltip: Spin around the vertical axis, in degrees.

params.label: Angle Z
params.name: angle_z
params.tooltip: Spin around the axis facing the camera, in degrees, which rolls the mesh in the plane of the screen.

nodes.purpose: Apply XYZ Euler rotation to an Array<MeshVertex>. Rotates position and normal of each vertex in X → Y → Z order (matches generator_math::rotate_3d bit-for-bit). The 3D-equivalent of node.rotate_4d, used in Wireframe-shaped graphs: polytope_vertices → Rotate3D → (project) → render.
nodes.summary: Spins a 3D mesh around the X, Y, and Z axes. Wire an LFO or a beat into the angles to keep it turning.

nodes.label: Rotate 4D

params.label: Angle XY
params.name: angle_xy
params.tooltip: Rotation in the XY plane, in degrees. Wire it through time for a continuous tumble.

params.label: Angle ZW
params.name: angle_zw
params.tooltip: Rotation in the ZW plane, in degrees. This plane reaches into the fourth dimension and is what makes a tesseract turn inside out.

params.label: Angle XW
params.name: angle_xw
params.tooltip: Rotation in the XW plane, in degrees. Combine it with the other two planes for a full 4D tumble.

nodes.purpose: Apply 4D rotation (XY, ZW, XW planes) to an Array<Vec4Vertex>. Matches generator_math::rotate_4d bit-for-bit. The transform stage of the 4D wireframe pipeline: producer → Rotate4D → renderer.
nodes.summary: Spins 4D geometry through its rotation planes, the move that makes a tesseract appear to turn inside out.

nodes.label: Rotate Coordinates

params.label: Angle
params.name: angle
params.tooltip: How far to spin the coordinate field around the centre, in degrees. A positive angle turns the +X direction toward +Y. This rotates the coordinates used for a warp, not the picture itself.

nodes.purpose: Rotate a 2D coordinate field around the origin by `angle` (radians). Reads (x, y) from input R/G, writes rotated (x', y') back to R/G. Collapses the `angle → cos / sin / neg_sin → field_combine(cos, -sin)` chain that any rotated-projection effect would otherwise need.
nodes.summary: Rotates a coordinate field around the centre. This spins the coordinates used to build a warp, not the image itself. For the picture, use Flip or a transform.

nodes.label: Rotate Vector

params.label: Angle
params.name: angle
params.tooltip: Turns every vector in the field by this amount, in degrees. The default 90 gives the perpendicular curl flow used for fluid sims.

nodes.purpose: Rotate the input's RG vec2 field by an arbitrary angle (radians) per pixel. `out.x = v.x*cos - v.y*sin`, `out.y = v.x*sin + v.y*cos`. The general curl-from-gradient atom — defaults to angle = PI/2 (+90° CCW, the divergence-free curl-flow case) but the angle is port-shadow-param so a control wire (LFO, driver, manual slider, clip-trigger envelope) can sweep it continuously. Sweeping the angle is how FluidSim2D's `rotation_angle` knob biases the flow off the pure-curl axis.
nodes.summary: Rotates a 2D vector field by an angle, turning every arrow in a flow or gradient field by the same amount.

nodes.label: Rotation Jitter

params.label: Amplitude
params.name: amplitude
params.tooltip: How much random twist to add to each copy. 0 leaves every copy aligned, and higher values scatter their angles more. The twist is added on top of any rotation the copies already had.

nodes.purpose: Add hash-driven per-instance Euler-rotation jitter to each InstanceTransform's rot_pad.xyz; positions and scale pass through. For each idx: rx/ry/rz = (hash_u32(idx*3+{0,1,2}) - 0.5) · amplitude. ADD semantics — pre-existing rotation from upstream is preserved and perturbed. Generic across any instanced field that wants visual density via non-uniform per-cube orientation. Reproduces the legacy DigitalPlants per-instance rotation hash bit-exactly when amplitude = 0.2.
nodes.summary: Adds a random twist to each copy's rotation, so a field of copies face slightly different ways instead of lining up.

nodes.label: Sample & Hold
nodes.purpose: Capture an input scalar on each trigger-edge and hold it until the next edge. Freezes the trigger-time value so mid-decay slider moves don't leak through.
nodes.summary: Grabs the value of a signal at each trigger and holds it steady until the next one. Freezes a moving value so later wiggles don't leak through.

nodes.label: Sample Image for Particles

params.label: Active Count
params.name: active_count
params.tooltip: How many particles to sample for, counted from the start of the list. Keep it matched to the particle count feeding the rest of the chain.

nodes.purpose: Per-particle bilinear sample of a Texture2D at each particle's position.xy. Output: Array<vec2<f32>> of the texture's RG channels per particle. The generic field-read atom for any particle pipeline — velocity fields, density samples, per-particle colour LUTs. Decomposed out of the legacy fused `integrate_particles` kernel.
nodes.summary: Reads the image colour underneath each particle, so the particles can pick up the look of whatever they fly over.

nodes.label: Sample Volume for Particles (3D)

params.label: Active Count
params.name: active_count
params.tooltip: How many particles read the volume this frame, capped to the size of the particle list. Entries beyond this count are left untouched.

nodes.purpose: Per-particle trilinear sample of a vec3 Texture3D at each particle's position.xyz. Output: Array<[f32; 3]> of the volume's RGB per particle (overwrite, not add — seeds the per-particle force buffer). The 3D sibling of node.sample_image_at_particles; the generic volumetric field-read atom for any 3D particle pipeline. Decomposed out of the fused node.fluid_simulate_3d.
nodes.summary: Reads a 3D volume at each particle's position, so particles can pick up a value from a density or flow field they pass through.

nodes.label: Saturation

params.label: Saturation
params.name: saturation
params.tooltip: How vivid the colours are. 0 is full greyscale, 1 leaves it unchanged, and above 1 oversaturates.

nodes.purpose: Luma-based saturation: out = mix(vec3(rec709_luma), c, saturation). 0 = grayscale, 1 = unchanged, >1 = oversaturated. Alpha passes through. The `saturation` input port shadows the param — wire any scalar source (LFO, audio bridge) to pump saturation live. Distinct from node.hue_saturation (HSV-space saturation); this pulls toward perceptual grey, the look Color Grade uses.
nodes.summary: Pulls colours toward grey or pushes them more vivid.

nodes.label: Scale + Offset (image)

params.label: Scale
params.name: scale
params.tooltip: Multiplies every colour before the offset is added. 1 leaves it unchanged, and a negative value inverts. Use 2 with offset -1 to turn a 0 to 1 field back into a signed -1 to 1 one.

params.label: Offset
params.name: offset
params.tooltip: Added to every colour after the scale. 0 leaves it unchanged. Use 0.5 with scale 0.5 to fit a signed -1 to 1 field into the 0 to 1 range.

nodes.purpose: Per-pixel affine remap `a * x + b` on RGB. Alpha pass-through. The general re-range primitive: use scale=2, offset=-1 to recover signed [-1, 1] noise from a [0, 1] generator; scale=0.5, offset=0.5 to compress signed sin/cos to [0, 1]; scale<0 to invert. Two-scalar version of node.exposure + node.brightness fused.
nodes.summary: Multiplies each colour by a scale and adds an offset, the image version of a basic value remap. Re-range a field before a clamp or a math step.

nodes.label: Scale + Offset (value)

params.label: A
params.name: a
params.tooltip: The value coming in to rescale.

params.label: Scale
params.name: scale
params.tooltip: Multiplies the value. Set it negative to flip the signal.

params.label: Offset
params.name: offset
params.tooltip: Adds to the value after scaling, shifting its range.

nodes.purpose: Scalar affine remap: out = a * scale + offset. The scalar counterpart of node.scale_offset_image — collapses Value+Math+Value+Math derivations like `3 + 5*x` or `0.3 - 0.28*x` into a single node. All three inputs are port-shadows-param: an inline param value drives the op when the wire is unwired.
nodes.summary: Multiplies a value by a scale and adds an offset, the everyday way to rescale a control signal into the range a knob wants. Set the scale negative to invert.

nodes.label: Scanline Jitter Field

params.label: Amount
params.name: amount
params.tooltip: How far the torn rows slide sideways and how many of them tear, from 0 for none up to 1. Feed the output into a Remap in Relative mode.

params.label: Scanline
params.name: scanline
params.tooltip: How many rows tear at once, from 0 for a clean image up to 1 for heavy tearing across the frame.

params.label: Speed
params.name: speed
params.tooltip: How fast the tearing pattern reshuffles over time, from 0.1 for a slow drift up to 10 for a fast jitter.

nodes.purpose: Generator for a per-row horizontal-offset field. `motion` picks the character: Tear (default) hashes each scanline row with a sine-hash on quantised time and gates by `scanline` so only a fraction of rows JOLT (the VHS / horizontal-tearing building block); Slide drives every band with smooth value noise on continuous time for an organic, ungated drift (the Latent Space website mosh slide). `bands` sets the band count: in Tear it has no effect (Tear is always per pixel row); in Slide, 0 = no rows (the field outputs zero, so a downstream flow/domain warp carries the motion without per-row slicing), and e.g. 36 = chunky strips. Emits `offset` (R = signed horizontal UV shift per row/band, G=B=0, A=1). Feed `offset` into node.remap (Relative mode) — alone or summed with node.block_displace_field's offset via node.mix(Add). `amount`/`speed` port-shadow their params; `time` is wired or read from FrameTime.seconds.
nodes.summary: Per-row horizontal offset for sideways glitch. Tear = gated VHS jolt; Slide = smooth organic per-band drift. Set Bands for chunky strips, feed it into Remap.

nodes.label: Scatter On Mesh
nodes.purpose: Scatter an Array<InstanceTransform> across a mesh's own surface (Array<MeshVertex>), area-weighted so instance density is uniform regardless of triangulation — the instance-producing sibling of node.spawn_from_mesh's surface mode, same 3-pass area/scan/place dispatch. Each instance gets a barycentric-sampled surface position, a uniform scale hashed into [scale_min, scale_max], and either a random upright yaw or (when align_to_normal is set) a rotation that additionally tilts the instance's local +Y onto the sampled triangle's flat face normal. Deterministic for a fixed (seed, mesh) — no true randomness. Pair with node.render_copies to draw the scattered instances: a field of scanned flowers on a terrain is terrain mesh -> node.scatter_on_mesh -> node.render_copies.
nodes.summary: Scatters copies of an object across a mesh's surface — a field of instances placed and sized randomly but deterministically, area-weighted so they don't clump on small triangles.

nodes.label: Sharpen

params.label: Amount
params.name: amount
params.tooltip: How much to sharpen. 0 passes through untouched, higher makes edges crisper.

nodes.purpose: Single-knob 4-neighbour Laplacian unsharp mask. `amount = 0` passes the source through unchanged; positive values add increasingly aggressive edge enhancement. Curated wrapper over the same math node.custom_convolution can express, factored out so one outer-card slider drives sharpening directly without needing five affine_scalar nodes to compute kernel weights.
nodes.summary: Sharpens the image by boosting the difference between each pixel and its neighbours. At 0 it passes through, higher values make edges crisper.

nodes.label: Shininess (Blinn)

params.label: Light X
params.name: light_x
params.tooltip: The X part of the light direction, from -1 to 1. Wiring a Light into the light port overrides this and the other two direction knobs.

params.label: Light Y
params.name: light_y
params.tooltip: The Y part of the light direction, from -1 to 1. The direction is normalised in the shader so only the ratio between X, Y, and Z matters.

params.label: Light Z
params.name: light_z
params.tooltip: The Z part of the light direction, from -1 to 1. Default 0.75 points the light mostly toward the camera for a forward-facing highlight.

params.label: View X
params.name: view_x
params.tooltip: The X part of the viewing direction, from -1 to 1. This is where the camera sits relative to the surface.

params.label: View Y
params.name: view_y
params.tooltip: The Y part of the viewing direction, from -1 to 1.

params.label: View Z
params.name: view_z
params.tooltip: The Z part of the viewing direction, from -1 to 1. Default 1 looks straight on, which puts the highlight where the light reflects back at the camera.

params.label: Power
params.name: power
params.tooltip: How tight the highlight is. Higher values give a small pinpoint glint while lower values spread it into a broad sheen.

params.label: Color
params.name: color
params.tooltip: The colour of the highlight. Alpha is ignored, and a wired light tints this further by multiplying its own colour in.

nodes.purpose: Blinn-Phong specular from a tangent-space normal map + directional light + view: `h = normalize(light + view); spec = pow(max(dot(n, h), 0), power)`. ADDITIVE — sum with a base shading via `node.compose` mode=Add. Defaults match oily-fluid PBR (light=(0.35,0.55,0.75), view=(0,0,1), power=48, near-white tint). Wire a `node.light` into `light` to drive direction + colour from one source instead of scattered scalars; the wired light's colour multiplies the `color` tint param.
nodes.summary: Adds a tight highlight where the surface catches the light, set by a shininess amount. The glossy hotspot on top of basic lighting.

nodes.label: Simplex Field 2D

params.label: Scale X
params.name: scale_x
params.tooltip: How many noise features fit across the width. Higher values make finer, busier horizontal detail.

params.label: Scale Y
params.name: scale_y
params.tooltip: How many noise features fit down the height. Higher values make finer, busier vertical detail.

params.label: Offset X
params.name: offset_x
params.tooltip: Pans the noise sideways through the frozen field. Animate it for directional horizontal flow.

params.label: Offset Y
params.name: offset_y
params.tooltip: Pans the noise up and down through the frozen field. Animate it for directional vertical flow.

params.label: Z
params.name: z
params.tooltip: Picks which slice of the noise to sample. Animate it over time for shimmer in place, and use a large gap between nodes for uncorrelated fields.

params.label: Output Channel
params.name: output_channel
params.tooltip: Which channel the noise is written to, with options R, G, B, and A. The signed value lands here and the other channels stay flat.

nodes.purpose: Pure generator. 3D Perlin-style simplex noise sampled at `(uv * scale + offset, z)`. Outputs the SIGNED noise value in approximately [-1, +1] to the R channel (GBA = 0, 0, 1). The Z axis is what makes a static node produce an evolving field — animate `z` for turbulent shimmer in place; pan `offset_x` / `offset_y` for directional flow through a frozen field. Use this when noise drives downstream math (displacement, color injection, fluid-sim seeding); use `node.noise` when you want a [0, 1] visual texture.
nodes.summary: Signed simplex noise output as a field, used to drive flows and displacements rather than shown directly.

nodes.purpose: Legacy type-ID alias of node.noise (Simplex, Detail 1). Constructs the unified noise primitive pre-set to the Simplex branch at single-octave, retained so older presets load unchanged.
nodes.summary: Cleaner gradient noise with fewer directional artifacts than Perlin. The single-octave Simplex branch of the unified Noise node.

nodes.label: Simplex Noise (per copy)

params.label: Scale
params.name: scale
params.tooltip: Frequency of the noise. Around 1 gives one broad swell across the field, higher values pack in fine grain.

params.label: Z
params.name: z
params.tooltip: Slices through the noise at a different depth. Wire a time value in to make the field drift and animate.

params.label: Offset X
params.name: offset_x
params.tooltip: Slides the noise field sideways. Wire an LFO in to scroll it across the copies.

params.label: Offset Y
params.name: offset_y
params.tooltip: Slides the noise field up and down.

nodes.purpose: Sample 3D Ashima simplex noise at each UV in an Array<vec2<f32>>, emit Array<f32>. Per-instance counterpart to node.noise (which samples per-pixel into a Texture2D). For each idx: out[idx] = simplex3d(vec3(uv[idx] * scale + offset, z)). All four shaping inputs (scale / z / offset_x / offset_y) are port-shadow-param so a time wire can drive `z` (animated noise field) or an LFO can pan `offset_*` (scrolling noise) without dragging extra Value nodes in.
nodes.summary: Gives every copy its own simplex-noise value, a smooth random number per copy for varying the look across a field.

nodes.label: Sine / Cosine

params.label: Frequency
params.name: freq
params.tooltip: Scales the input before the wave is applied, setting how many cycles fit across the range. The default of about 6.28 fits one full cycle across a 0 to 1 input.

params.label: Phase
params.name: phase
params.tooltip: Shifts the wave along, in degrees, so you can slide the ripples or animate them by sweeping this value.

params.label: Mode
params.name: mode
params.tooltip: Picks the wave shape from Sin, Cos, or Tan. Sin and Cos stay in the minus one to one range, Tan is clamped to keep the output stable.

nodes.purpose: Per-pixel trigonometric remap: out = trig_mode(input.rgb * freq + phase). Mode picks Sin / Cos / Tan; the rest of the wiring is identical so switching variants is one click. Tan output is clamped to ±32 to keep downstream shaders NaN/Inf-free. `freq` and `phase` can ALSO be driven per-pixel from optional texture inputs (`freq_tex` / `phase_tex` — R channel) — unlocks per-cell unique trig patterns (per-star twinkle, cellular flicker, etc.) when fed from a per-cell hash source like `node.voronoi_2d` (A channel) routed through `node.channel_mixer`.
nodes.summary: Runs each value through sine, cosine, or tangent after scaling it. The building block for ripples and wave patterns out of a gradient.

nodes.label: Sine Wave (projected)

params.label: X Coefficient
params.name: a
params.tooltip: How much the field's red channel feeds the wave, usually the X coordinate. Set a and b together to aim the wave along X, Y, or a diagonal.

params.label: Y Coefficient
params.name: b
params.tooltip: How much the field's green channel feeds the wave, usually the Y coordinate. Leave it at 0 to read only the red channel from a scalar field.

params.label: Constant Offset
params.name: c
params.tooltip: A constant added to the projection before the wave, which shifts its phase.

params.label: Frequency (base)
params.name: freq
params.tooltip: The base frequency of the wave, so higher values pack more cycles into the same space. Wire one shared value into every term to keep them in phase.

params.label: Frequency Scale
params.name: freq_scale
params.tooltip: A per-term multiplier on the base frequency. Give each term a different scale to layer waves at related rates.

params.label: Time (base)
params.name: time
params.tooltip: The base time that moves the wave along. Wire the generator time in so every term advances together.

params.label: Time Scale
params.name: time_scale
params.tooltip: A per-term multiplier on time, which sets how fast this wave drifts. Negative values send it the other way.

nodes.purpose: Fused linear-projection + sin term: out = sin((a*field.r + b*field.g + c) * freq * freq_scale + time * time_scale). One node per term of any sum-of-sines pattern (Plasma, moiré, standing waves). For a linear projection of UV channels set (a, b) to pick the projection; for a pre-computed scalar field (distance, noise) leave defaults (a=1, b=0, c=0) so it reads the broadcast R channel.
nodes.summary: Mixes a coordinate field into a moving sine wave in one step, the core ingredient of plasma and interference patterns.

nodes.label: Slice Volume

params.label: Slice Z
params.name: slice_z
params.tooltip: How deep into the volume to cut the slice, from the front at 0 to the back at 1. Sweep it smoothly to animate through the volume.

params.label: UV Scale
params.name: uv_scale
params.tooltip: Zooms the slice into the output frame. 1 fills it normally, higher crops in tighter, lower pulls back.

params.label: Center X Offset
params.name: center_x
params.tooltip: Shifts which part of the slice you see along X, from -1 to 1. Use it to pan across the volume when zoomed in.

params.label: Center Y Offset
params.name: center_y
params.tooltip: Shifts which part of the slice you see along Y, from -1 to 1. Use it to pan across the volume when zoomed in.

nodes.purpose: Sample a Texture3D at a fixed Z slice to produce a Texture2D. UV scale + center re-frame the slice into the output texture. Drives "peel a 2D plane out of a volume" use cases: MRI-style display, debug visualisation of volumetric fluid density, or any future Phase D volume rendering.
nodes.summary: Takes a flat slice through a 3D volume to get a normal 2D image. The way to look inside a fluid or density field.

nodes.label: Slope Displace

params.label: Strength
params.name: strength
params.tooltip: How much the embossed base layer drives the displacement. Higher pushes pixels further along the contrast contours for a more liquid pull.

params.label: Step (px)
params.name: step
params.tooltip: How far apart the gradient is sampled, in pixels. Larger reads a broader slope for a softer, wider warp.

params.label: Weight
params.name: weight
params.tooltip: Scales the final pixel shift, measured in UV units. The default is tiny on purpose since even small values move pixels noticeably.

nodes.purpose: Emboss-style displacement: soft-light-blend `base` over `image`, take the luminance Sobel gradient of the blend at a `step`-pixel offset, and displace `image` by gradient × `weight`. Output is `image` resampled at the displaced UV. Watercolor's slope pass as a reusable atom — the pigment-pooling edge-pull that follows contrast contours.
nodes.summary: Pushes pixels along the slope of an embossed version of the image, an emboss-driven warp for liquid and paint looks.

nodes.label: Smoothing

params.label: Time Constant (s)
params.name: time_constant
params.tooltip: How long the smoothing takes to settle, in seconds. Higher is smoother and slower to react.

nodes.purpose: Exponential one-pole low-pass on a scalar wire: alpha = 1 - exp(-dt / time_constant), out = previous + (in - previous) * alpha, where dt is the frame delta in seconds. At dt = time_constant the output has moved ~63% of the way to the input (1 - e^-1). Symmetric (single time constant, range (0.001, 2.0] s) — frame-rate-independent since alpha derives from dt, not frame count. Optional reset_trigger drops the stored `previous` to 0 on an integer-edge change.
nodes.summary: Smooths a jumpy control signal into a gentle glide, with the response time set in seconds. The same feel holds whatever the frame rate.

nodes.label: Smoothstep

params.label: Low
params.name: low
params.tooltip: The input value where the output starts rising from 0.

params.label: High
params.name: high
params.tooltip: The input value where the output reaches 1. Below low and above high are flat.

nodes.purpose: Per-pixel smoothstep contrast curve on RGB, alpha pass-through. Maps the input through `smoothstep(low, high, x)` per channel — anything below `low` clamps to 0, anything above `high` clamps to 1, and the Hermite polynomial smoothes the transition between them. Both edges are always live; for a symmetric-around-zero curve, wire `node.math(operation=Negate) → low` to mirror `high` into the low edge.
nodes.summary: Eases each value through a smooth S-curve between a low and high edge. Softens a hard threshold into a gentle ramp.

nodes.label: Spawn From Image

params.label: Max Capacity
params.name: max_capacity
params.tooltip: The most particles this node will ever hold, which sets the buffer size. Raise it only if you need more than the active count.

params.label: Active Count
params.name: active_count
params.tooltip: How many particles to spawn this frame. Every one lands on a bright part of the image, wrapping round if there are more particles than bright pixels.

params.label: Output Width
params.name: output_width
params.tooltip: The width of the space the image maps into, in pixels. Match it to your render size so the mask lands where you expect.

params.label: Output Height
params.name: output_height
params.tooltip: The height of the space the image maps into, in pixels. Match it to your render size so the mask lands where you expect.

params.label: Frame Seed
params.name: frame_seed
params.tooltip: Changes the random sub-pixel jitter on placement. Move it to reshuffle how particles sit within each bright pixel.

nodes.purpose: Exact-placement particle seeding from a Texture2D density mask. Two-pass dispatch: (1) compact — scan the mask, atomically append every bright texel's UV (R > 0.1) into a flat list; (2) place — assign each active particle a UV via round-robin (i mod bright_count) with sub-texel jitter. Guarantees every particle lands alive on the mask: zero dead particles regardless of mask sparsity. When active_count > bright_count, particles wrap-around the list (jittered so they don't stack). When the mask is empty every particle is parked dead at center.
nodes.summary: Creates particles placed by the bright areas of an image, so a picture or mask becomes a cloud of points. Spawn density follows the image.

nodes.label: Spawn From Mesh
nodes.purpose: Seed particles from a mesh's own geometry (Array<MeshVertex>) so an imported or procedural model can dissolve/explode into the existing 3D particle stack. `vertices` mode: one particle per vertex, exact silhouette. `surface` mode: area-weighted random triangle sampling for uniform surface density regardless of triangulation (three-pass dispatch: per-triangle area, prefix-sum scan, barycentric place). Positions are emitted in the mesh's LOCAL space, same convention as the mesh itself — an upstream transform applies later. Pair with node.apply_radial_burst_3d_to_particles + node.euler_step_particles_3d to blow the seeded cloud apart, crossfading the intact mesh render out as the particles render in.
nodes.summary: Creates particles from a mesh's own geometry — one per vertex for an exact silhouette, or scattered evenly across its surface. The way an imported model dissolves into particles.

nodes.label: Spawn Particles

params.label: Max Capacity
params.name: max_capacity
params.tooltip: The most particles this can ever hold, set when the chain is built.

params.label: Active Count
params.name: active_count
params.tooltip: How many particles to spawn and keep alive.

params.label: Seed
params.name: seed_offset
params.tooltip: Changes the random layout. Move it to get a different spread.

params.label: Seed Mode
params.name: seed_mode
params.tooltip: How the starting positions are chosen.

nodes.purpose: Emit a fresh Array<Particle> sized by `max_capacity` (chain-build-time ceiling). `active_count` particles initialise alive at Wang-hash uniform positions in [0,1]²; the remaining capacity sits dead at center. `seed_mode` picks when the seed kernel fires: EveryFrame (legacy — overwrite each tick; suited to advection 'rain' effects where the integrator never accumulates state) or OnceOnReset (seed once after a state-store reset; the buffer persists whatever the downstream sim writes into it — required for any sim where particle state must evolve across frames, e.g. StrangeAttractor + FluidSim2D).
nodes.summary: Creates a fresh batch of particles to start a simulation, with a count you set. The first node in a particle chain.

nodes.label: Split XY
nodes.purpose: Split an Array<vec2<f32>> into two Array<f32>s, one per component (`x`, `y`). Composition glue so Array<f32> math primitives can operate on individual axes of a vec2 wire — UV coordinates, 2D positions, 2D velocities — without each scalar-math primitive needing a vec2-input variant.
nodes.summary: Splits a list of 2D points into two separate number lists, one for X and one for Y. The inverse of combining them.

nodes.label: Spread Out (diffuse)

params.label: Diffusion
params.name: diffusion
params.tooltip: How hard the random kick hits each particle every frame. 0 leaves the cloud exactly as it is, and the useful range tops out around 0.05 before things scatter wildly.

params.label: Active Count
params.name: active_count
params.tooltip: How many particles in the buffer actually get kicked, counted from the front. It is capped to the real buffer size so a value above the particle count just affects all of them.

nodes.purpose: Apply a per-particle hash-based random kick to `Particle.velocity`. One GPU dispatch over Array<Particle> with aliased read+write. `diffusion` scales the kick magnitude (typical range 0..0.05); zero means no-op. `frame_count` reseeds the hash each frame so the kick is genuinely uncorrelated across frames. Generic Brownian-noise atom — pairs with any particle integrator (attractor ODE, fluid sim, advection) that wants additive jitter on its 3D state.
nodes.summary: Gives each particle a small random kick so a tight clump slowly spreads apart. Adds a bit of life and scatter.

nodes.label: Spread Out (3D diffuse)

params.label: Diffusion
params.name: diffusion
params.tooltip: How strong a random kick each particle gets, from 0 for none up to 0.5. Higher spreads tight clumps apart faster, and the push is stronger where particles are densest.

params.label: Active Count
params.name: active_count
params.tooltip: How many particles get the random kick. Keep it matched to the particle count feeding the sim.

nodes.purpose: Per-particle incoherent 3D random kick added in-place to an Array<[f32; 3]> force buffer, weighted by local density. forces[i] += (hash_float3(i, frame) - 0.5) * diffusion * capped(density). Reseeds the hash each frame (Brownian, not drift); the density weighting concentrates the kick where particles clump (anti-clumping diffusion). Decomposed from the diffusion step of the fused node.fluid_simulate_3d.
nodes.summary: Gives each 3D particle a small random kick so a tight clump slowly spreads apart in space.

nodes.label: SSAO (GTAO)
nodes.purpose: Ground Truth Ambient Occlusion (GTAO) from scene depth + a Camera (docs/CINEMATIC_POST_DESIGN.md D9), replacing node.ssao_from_depth. Reconstructs view-space center position + normal exactly as the retired atom (linearize_depth + inverse-projection xy; normal via explicit +/-1-texel finite differences). 2 slices per pixel at hash-derived angles (phi_i = hash_angle(px)*0.5 + i*(pi/2)); per slice, per side (+/- the slice's screen direction), 4 steps at screen radii derived from `radius` projected at the center pixel's depth; each step's sample is range-checked against `radius` in view space and folded into a per-side horizon cosine (max, floored at -1.0 for 'no occluder'); horizon angles converted via acos, clamped against the normal's signed in-plane angle, and integrated with the closed-form arc a(h) = 0.25*(-cos(2h-n)+cos(n)+2h*sin(n)); slice visibility = ||N_p||*(a(h1)+a(h2)); pixel visibility = mean of the 2 slices; out.r = clamp(1 - intensity*(1-visibility), 0, 1) (broadcast to RGB, alpha 1). 16 depth taps total, no temporal accumulation, no thickness heuristic — deterministic single-frame budget (D9(a)). Output is an AO map — wire it into a node.mix (Multiply mode) against the scene color; this atom does NOT modify the color image itself. Reads fov_y/near/far entirely via derived uniforms — the Camera wire is never a GPU binding.
nodes.summary: Computes contact shadows from scene depth and a physical camera lens using a horizon-angle integral (GTAO) — darkens crevices and touching surfaces the way ambient light naturally would, more accurately than the retired hemisphere-sample SSAO.

nodes.label: Sum Into Bins

params.label: Increment
params.name: increment
params.tooltip: How much is added to every slot on each trigger. Use 90 to step every value by a quarter turn per hit, negative to count down.

params.label: Capacity
params.name: capacity
params.tooltip: How many slots the running list holds. Set it to match the number of values the downstream node expects.

params.label: Initial
params.name: initial
params.tooltip: The starting values for the slots, taken from the first row of a table. Left unset, every slot starts at zero.

nodes.purpose: Add `increment` to every element of an internal Array<f32> accumulator on each clip trigger; emit the accumulator. Generic envelope-mode driver — pair with a trigger_count source to advance N parallel scalars synchronously. NestedCubes envelope mode is the first user.
nodes.summary: Adds an amount into each slot of a running list on every trigger, so you can build up a histogram or per-slot counter over time.

nodes.label: Surface Bumps

params.label: Z Scale
params.name: z_scale
params.tooltip: How pronounced the surface detail reads. Larger values flatten the bumps out, smaller values make them steeper and more dramatic.

params.label: Aspect
params.name: aspect
params.tooltip: Scales the vertical slope so non-square frames keep the right shape. Leave it at 1 unless your canvas is stretched, then wire the frame aspect in.

params.label: Coord Space
params.name: coord_space
params.tooltip: Which normal convention to output. TangentZ suits flat screen-space shading, while WorldYUp suits a 3D mesh lying flat with Y pointing up.

nodes.purpose: Scalar height field (read from `in.r`) → unit normal map (RGB) via central-difference gradient. Coord_space picks the output convention: TangentZ for flat-surface tangent-space shading (OilyFluid lambert/matcap/blinn), WorldYUp for 3D meshes laid out in the XZ plane with Y up (MetallicGlass full-resolution-reflection trick). Larger `z_scale` flattens the normal; smaller steepens it. `aspect` scales the Y-axis gradient for non-square world quads. Output is SIGNED (range [-1, 1] per channel).
nodes.summary: Turns a grayscale height image into a normal map, so light and dark become bumps and dents the lighting can catch. The way to add surface detail from a texture.

nodes.label: Swirl Force (3D, curl)

params.label: Volume Resolution
params.name: vol_res
params.tooltip: The width and height of the force volume in cells, default 128. Keep it matched to the gradient volume feeding in.

params.label: Volume Depth
params.name: vol_depth
params.tooltip: The depth of the force volume in cells, default 128. Keep it matched to the gradient volume feeding in.

params.label: Curl Strength
params.name: curl_strength
params.tooltip: How hard the swirl pulls particles into orbiting eddies. 0 is no swirl, higher spins them up. This is the dial that makes the flow read as smoke.

params.label: Slope Strength
params.name: slope_strength
params.tooltip: How hard particles push along the gradient. Negative pulls them toward dense spots, positive pushes them away, default -500.

params.label: Ref Axis X
params.name: ref_axis_x
params.tooltip: The X part of the axis the swirl rotates around. The three axis values get normalised together, so feed raw sine and cosine without worrying about length.

params.label: Ref Axis Y
params.name: ref_axis_y
params.tooltip: The Y part of the axis the swirl rotates around, default 1. The three axis values get normalised together before the swirl is built.

params.label: Ref Axis Z
params.name: ref_axis_z
params.tooltip: The Z part of the axis the swirl rotates around. The three axis values get normalised together, so a length of zero falls back to straight up.

nodes.purpose: Combine a vec3 gradient Texture3D into a force field: cross the gradient with a rotating reference axis for swirl (tangential orbit around density peaks) and add the gradient scaled by slope (radial push/pull). force = cross(gradient, ref_axis) * curl_strength + gradient * slope_strength; ref_axis is normalized CPU-side and applies to the whole volume (rotate it over time upstream so the swirl's quiet pole wanders). Writes a vec3 force Texture3D. The curl+slope half of the decomposed node.fluid_gradient_curl_3d; pair downstream of node.edge_slope_3d.
nodes.summary: Turns a 3D gradient field into a swirling, divergence-free force, the move that makes 3D particles curl into smoke-like eddies.

nodes.label: Switch (array)

params.label: Selector
params.name: selector
params.tooltip: Picks which input list to pass through, by number.

nodes.purpose: N-way Array<f32> selector. Routes one of in_0..in_7 (Array f32) to the output buffer based on the selector input (rounded, clamped). Output capacity = max of all wired input capacities at chain-build time. Completes the mux family alongside node.switch_value / node.switch_texture; primary use is mode-switching at the value-array level (e.g., NestedCubes Envelope vs Pose target_angles).
nodes.summary: Picks one of several incoming lists and passes it through, chosen by a selector number.

nodes.label: Switch (texture)

params.label: Selector
params.name: selector
params.tooltip: Picks which input image to pass through, by number.

params.label: Input Count
params.name: num_inputs
params.tooltip: How many inputs to expose. The node grows or shrinks to match.

nodes.purpose: Dynamic N-way Texture2D selector. `num_inputs` sets how many in_0..in_N ports exist and a rounded, clamped `selector` forwards the matching input. Reconfigures its port list when `num_inputs` changes.
nodes.summary: Picks one of several incoming images and passes it through, chosen by a selector number. The input count grows as you wire more in.

nodes.label: Switch (value)

params.label: Selector
params.name: selector
params.tooltip: Picks which input to pass through, by number.

nodes.purpose: N-way scalar selector. Routes one of in_0..in_7 (Scalar F32) to the output based on the selector input (rounded, clamped). Every input is port-shadows-param — wire dynamic sources into the ports, or set the inline `in_N` params for static option tables (curated frequency rates, side counts, gate constants). Useful for trigger-driven parameter switching.
nodes.summary: Picks one of several incoming values and passes it through, chosen by a selector number. Use it to flip between sources live.

nodes.label: Taper Mesh
nodes.purpose: Per-vertex taper of an Array<MeshVertex> along `axis`. s(v) = mix(1, taper, clamp((coord(v) - center) / length, 0, 1) * w), where coord(v) is the vertex's coordinate along `axis` and w is the optional per-vertex `weights` input (a short or unwired weights buffer degrades to 1.0, never silent 0). The two off-axis position components scale by s (taper=1 no change, taper=0 collapses to a point on the axis); normal off-axis components divide by s and the normal renormalizes — exact for this transform (D4). `t*w` is not re-clamped after the mix, so weights above 1.0 produce honest extrapolation (over-taper / flare) rather than a silent clamp.
nodes.summary: Narrows a mesh toward a point along one axis, like sharpening a pencil or a candle flame. The lighting normals scale with it so the taper still shades correctly.

nodes.label: Texture Advect

params.label: Δt (pixels/frame)
params.name: dt
params.tooltip: How far the flow drags the image each frame, in pixels, default 1. Negative runs the flow backwards for trail-erase tricks. Wire it to an LFO to pulse the motion.

params.label: Boundary
params.name: boundary
params.tooltip: What happens at the edges, Repeat for looping fluid sims or Clamp to fade off-frame samples into the edge colour.

nodes.purpose: Backward (semi-Lagrangian) advection of a texture by a 2D velocity field. For each output pixel: sample the source at `uv - velocity.rg * dt / dims`. The universal fluid op — used for color advection by velocity, self-advection of velocity by itself, smoke / dye / paint transport. `dt` is in pixels-per-frame and resolution-independent.
nodes.summary: Drags a texture along a velocity field, carrying the pixels with the flow. The transport step in a fluid simulation.

nodes.label: Texture Size
nodes.purpose: Read the input texture's pixel dimensions. Outputs `width`, `height`, and `aspect` (= width / height) as scalars. No GPU dispatch — values are CPU-accessible from the bound texture, so the read is zero-latency. Use to feed aspect-correction into a downstream effect-graph chain (e.g. wire `aspect` into `distance_to_point.scale_x` to make a radial mask circular on a non-square canvas).
nodes.summary: Reads the width, height, and aspect ratio of an image and hands them back as numbers. Wire the aspect into a mask to keep circles round on a wide canvas.

params.label: Divisor
params.name: divisor
params.tooltip: Divides the summed result. 1 keeps it a plain sum, 5 turns the five inputs into an average. A divisor of 0 falls back to black.

nodes.purpose: Per-pixel weighted-sum of five textures: out = (a+b+c+d+e) / divisor. divisor=1 keeps the result a plain sum (compose mode), divisor=5 turns it into an average. Collapses the four-deep Mix(Add) chain (+ optional scale_offset_texture for division) that a manual N-term composition would otherwise need.
nodes.summary: Legacy fixed five-input sum, superseded by node.multi_blend (dynamic N inputs). Hidden from the palette but still loads in saved graphs.

nodes.label: Threshold

params.label: Threshold
params.name: level
params.tooltip: The brightness cutoff. Pixels brighter than this are kept and darker ones are dropped.

params.label: Softness
params.name: softness
params.tooltip: Fades the edge of the cutoff. 0 gives a hard line and higher values blend the keep and drop regions together.

nodes.purpose: Pixel-local luma threshold with a smoothstep falloff of width `softness`. Isolates bright regions for bloom and highlight masks.
nodes.summary: Keeps only the bright parts of the image and drops the rest, with a soft edge you can widen. The way to pull out highlights for a bloom or a mask.

nodes.label: Tone Map

params.label: Exposure
params.name: exposure
params.tooltip: Brightens or dims the image before the curve is applied. 1 leaves it unchanged, above 1 brightens, below 1 darkens.

params.label: Curve
params.name: curve
params.tooltip: How the bright highlights roll off. Narkowicz and Hill ACES are the filmic standards, AgX gives a more natural look, and Khronos PBR Neutral holds onto saturation in very bright colours.

params.label: Output Mode
params.name: mode
params.tooltip: What kind of display you are mapping to. SDR for a normal screen or export, PQ for HDR10 export, EDR for a macOS HDR display, and EDR Passthrough to skip the curve.

params.label: Paper White (nits)
params.name: paper_white
params.tooltip: The brightness in nits that plain white should hit on an HDR display. Only used in the HDR output modes.

params.label: Max Nits
params.name: max_nits
params.tooltip: The peak brightness in nits your HDR display can reach, used to set where the brightest highlights land. Only used in the HDR output modes.

nodes.purpose: HDR → display tone mapping with selectable curve (Narkowicz ACES / Hill ACES / AgX / Khronos PBR Neutral) and output mode (SDR / PQ for HDR10 export / EDR for macOS / EDR passthrough). Per-channel curves, alpha pass-through. For a simple Reinhard-only path matching FluidSim's display bit-for-bit, use node.reinhard_tone_map.
nodes.summary: Fits HDR content, where colours can run far brighter than pure white, onto whatever display you are sending to. On a normal SDR screen or export it rolls the bright highlights down smoothly so they don't clip to flat white, and on an HDR display it can keep those highlights bright by mapping to an HDR output instead. The curve choice sets how that rolloff looks.

nodes.label: Torus Wrap Field

params.label: Tube Radius
params.name: base_radius
params.tooltip: The thickness of the tube, the fatness of the donut. Larger values make a chunkier ring.

params.label: Major Radius
params.name: torus_radius
params.tooltip: The overall radius of the ring from its centre to the middle of the tube. Larger values make a wider donut.

params.label: Fold Angle
params.name: fold_angle
params.tooltip: Rotates the whole ring around its X axis, in degrees. Wire a time source here to keep it folding continuously.

params.label: Instance Scale
params.name: instance_scale
params.tooltip: Scales every copy placed on the torus surface. When morphing between shapes, feed the same scale into the matching wrap node so the size stays continuous.

nodes.purpose: Lift an Array<vec2<f32>> of UVs onto a torus surface, emit Array<InstanceTransform>. For each UV: theta = uv.x * TAU, phi = uv.y * TAU, pos = ((R + r·cos φ)·cos θ, r·sin φ, (R + r·cos φ)·sin θ). Optional Array<f32> normal_disp pushes each instance along the outward surface normal — drive from node.fractal_noise_per_copy × petal-amplitude for flower-style petal displacement. `fold_angle` rotates the whole field around the X axis (port-shadow, drive from time for continuous animation). Generic across rings, halos, donuts, flower discs, gateways.
nodes.summary: Wraps a flat grid of points around a torus, a donut shape, placing copies on its surface.

nodes.label: Track Persist

params.label: Match Radius
params.name: match_radius
params.tooltip: How far a blob can move between frames and still count as the same one, in normalised canvas units. Raise it for fast movers, lower it for crowded scenes where IDs swap.

params.label: Grace Frames
params.name: grace_frames
params.tooltip: How many frames to keep holding a blob after it drops out, measured in frames. Raise it to ride through flickery detections, lower it to clear stale blobs fast.

nodes.purpose: Greedy nearest-neighbour identity tracking with grace-period retention. Matches incoming Channels[X, Y, WIDTH, HEIGHT] detections against a persistent tracked set using Euclidean distance on (X, Y). Output has stable identity across frames — prerequisite for temporal filters like one_euro_filter. Unmatched detections spawn new tracks (up to capacity); tracks missing for grace_frames cycles are removed.
nodes.summary: Keeps a stable identity on each tracked blob from frame to frame, holding onto one briefly even if it flickers out. Stops IDs from jumping around.

nodes.label: Transform

params.label: Translate X
params.name: translate_x
params.tooltip: Slides the image sideways. Negative moves it left, positive moves it right, and the range runs from -1 to 1 across the frame.

params.label: Translate Y
params.name: translate_y
params.tooltip: Slides the image up and down. The range runs from -1 to 1 across the frame and 0 leaves it centred.

params.label: Scale
params.name: scale
params.tooltip: Zooms the image around its centre. 1 is original size, below 1 shrinks it, above 1 enlarges it.

params.label: Rotation
params.name: rotation
params.tooltip: Spins the image around its centre in degrees. Positive turns it clockwise on screen and 0 leaves it level.

nodes.purpose: 2D UV affine: translate, scale, rotate around the center. Aspect-correct rotation; out-of-bounds samples return transparent black. Every affine param has a same-named scalar input port (port-shadows-param) — wire `translate_x`, `translate_y`, or `rotation` to drive the transform from a control producer (LFO, Color Compass, Math, …).
nodes.summary: Moves, scales, and rotates the whole image around its centre. The basic transform for repositioning a layer.

nodes.label: Transform 3D
nodes.purpose: TRS (position/rotation/scale) producer for scene objects. Nine scalar params — position X/Y/Z, rotation X/Y/Z (radians), scale X/Y/Z — each port-shadowed by a same-named optional scalar input port so the transform can be driven by an LFO, MIDI, a beat_ramp, or any other control-rate source. Outputs a single Transform struct (pos/rot_euler/scale) consumed by render_scene's transform_n ports (replacing nine per-object params) or any future TRS consumer. Matrices are composed by consumers — the wire carries plain TRS, never a matrix.
nodes.summary: Position, rotation, and scale for one scene object. Wire it into a render_scene transform slot, or drive an axis from an LFO or MIDI to animate it live.

nodes.label: Trigger Ease To
nodes.purpose: Beat-clocked snap-and-glide. On each trigger edge eases from the current value to the incoming `target` along a cubic ease-out over `window_beats` beats, then rests until the next trigger.
nodes.summary: On each trigger it eases smoothly from its current value to a new target over a number of beats, then rests. A beat-clocked glide between values.

nodes.label: Trigger Gate

params.label: Enable
params.name: enable
params.tooltip: Lets the trigger stream pass through while on. Triggers that arrive while it is off are dropped, so turning it back on does not replay the ones you missed.

nodes.purpose: Gate a trigger_count scalar stream. When `enable` is true, advances pass through (output += input - previous_input). When false, advances are absorbed — output stays frozen and re-enabling does NOT fire a backlog. Equivalent to the legacy `if triggered { advance }` gate pattern, expressed as a graph wire instead of consumer-internal state.
nodes.summary: Passes a trigger stream through only while it is enabled, so you can switch a clip-trigger source on and off.

nodes.label: Tube From Path
nodes.purpose: Sweep a circular ring around a centerline path (Array<CurvePoint>, XZ plane: x=world X, y=world Z) into a path_len x (sides+1) positions+uv tube grid. Optional `lift` (+Y per path point) and `radius_scale` (per path point, composable with a ramp for tapered vines) degrade to 0.0 / 1.0 past a short or unwired buffer, never to silent zero-radius. Frame per point: tangent from a central finite difference, reference-up=+Y — degenerates when the tangent is (near-)parallel to +Y (a vertical path segment); parallel-transport frames are deferred. Normals are left zero — wire node.make_triangles downstream (src_cols=sides+1, src_rows=path point count).
nodes.summary: Sweeps a tube of adjustable thickness along a path — the way you'd build a vine, cable, or ribbon from a center-line curve. Thickness and lift can vary per point for tapered, climbing shapes.

nodes.label: Turbulence (simplex)

params.label: Amplitude
params.name: amplitude
params.tooltip: How hard the noise field pushes each particle, from 0 for still up to 0.05. Small values already give plenty of swirl, so keep it low.

params.label: Modulator Gain
params.name: modulator_gain
params.tooltip: How much a wired modulator texture boosts the noise where it is bright, from 0 to 20. Only does anything when something is wired into the modulator input.

params.label: Z (Time)
params.name: z
params.tooltip: Slides the noise field through its third dimension, which animates the flow. Wire time times a rate here to keep the turbulence moving.

params.label: Noise Scale
params.name: noise_scale
params.tooltip: How zoomed in the noise pattern is, from 0 for huge soft swells up to 16 for fine busy detail.

params.label: Active Count
params.name: active_count
params.tooltip: How many particles the noise force is applied to. Keep it matched to the particle count feeding the sim.

nodes.purpose: Per-particle 2D simplex noise force added in-place to an Array<vec2<f32>> force buffer. Evaluates simplex_noise_2d at each particle's position (X/Y noise channels offset by 100 for decorrelation), scales by `amplitude`, optionally boosts by a scalar Texture2D sampled at the same UV (`amplitude * (1 + capped(m) * gain)`, capped = m/(1+m)), and adds to `forces[i]`. Aliased Array<vec2> in/out — one physical buffer, in-place mutation. Resolution-independent: work scales with particle count, not canvas area. Replaces a per-pixel texture noise chain (simplex_field × 2 + math + mix) for any per-particle noise consumer.
nodes.summary: Pushes particles around with a flowing noise field, giving organic, swirling motion. The classic turbulence force.

nodes.label: Turbulence (3D, simplex)

params.label: Turbulence
params.name: turbulence
params.tooltip: How strongly the 3D noise pushes the particles around. 0 leaves them on their existing path, and higher values stir up more swirl. The useful range is small, so keep it low.

params.label: Anti-Clump
params.name: anti_clump
params.tooltip: How much extra push the noise adds where particles are already crowded, which spreads dense clumps apart. 0 turns the boost off, and higher values separate clusters more aggressively.
