params.label: Active Count
params.name: active_count
params.tooltip: How many particles the noise force is applied to. It is clamped to whatever the particle and force buffers can hold.

nodes.purpose: Per-particle 3D simplex noise advection added in-place to an Array<[f32; 3]> force buffer. 3D noise from SimplexNoise2D on three orthogonal planes (yz/xz/xy), density-adaptive amplitude (turbulence * (1 + capped(density) * anti_clump), capped = d/(1+d)). Samples a density Texture3D at the particle's position. Aliased force in/out — one physical buffer, in-place add. The 3D sibling of node.turbulence, decomposed from the fused node.fluid_simulate_3d.
nodes.summary: Pushes 3D particles around with a flowing 3D noise field for organic, swirling motion through space.

nodes.label: Twist Mesh
nodes.purpose: Per-vertex twist of an Array<MeshVertex> about its own `axis`. theta(v) = angle * (coord(v) - center) * w, where coord(v) is the vertex's coordinate along `axis` and w is the optional per-vertex `weights` input (a short or unwired weights buffer degrades to 1.0, never silent 0). Position AND normal rotate about `axis` by theta — exact: axis=X rotates (y,z), axis=Y rotates (z,x), axis=Z rotates (x,y). `angle` is UNBOUNDED (range None) — a saw LFO doing full revolutions is a valid performer gesture (BUG-039 class): sin/cos absorb the wrap with no seam, never clamp it.
nodes.summary: Twists a mesh around its own length, like wringing out a cloth or spinning a vine. Position and lighting normals both rotate exactly, so continuous saw-LFO spin reads with no seam.

nodes.label: Unlit Material

params.label: Colour R
params.name: color_r
params.tooltip: The red amount of the flat base colour.

params.label: Colour G
params.name: color_g
params.tooltip: The green amount of the flat base colour.

params.label: Colour B
params.name: color_b
params.tooltip: The blue amount of the flat base colour.

params.label: Opacity
params.name: color_a
params.tooltip: The opacity of the material, from clear at 0 to solid at 1. Note that v1 renders opaque only, so this is informational for now.

params.label: Emission R
params.name: emission_r
params.tooltip: The red amount of the glow added on top of the base colour.

params.label: Emission G
params.name: emission_g
params.tooltip: The green amount of the glow added on top of the base colour.

params.label: Emission B
params.name: emission_b
params.tooltip: The blue amount of the glow added on top of the base colour.

params.label: Emission Intensity
params.name: emission_intensity
params.tooltip: How strongly the surface glows. At 0 there is no glow, and higher values push the emission colour brighter.

nodes.purpose: Flat-colour material — no lighting math, no shadow term. The renderer writes (base_color + emission) directly. Use for UI overlays, debug visualisation, neon, anything that shouldn't react to lights. The bundled 3D mesh renderers do NOT require a `light` input when this material is wired (the conditional-requirement table lets the light input stay truly optional). Outputs one Material on `out` consumed by render_3d_mesh / render_instanced_3d_mesh. Emission is premultiplied with `emission_intensity` at emission — downstream reads the final emissive directly.
nodes.summary: A flat-colour material with no lighting, so the surface shows its base colour straight. The simplest material, good for solid or glowing looks.

nodes.label: UV Displace by Flow

params.label: Weight
params.name: weight
params.tooltip: How far the flow field pushes the image, default 0.001 which is about one pixel at 1080p. Higher smears it more, negative pushes the other way.

params.label: Bias
params.name: bias
params.tooltip: The neutral midpoint of the flow channels, default 0.5 for standard 0 to 1 flow. Set it to 0 when the flow data is already signed around zero.

nodes.purpose: Sample a source texture at UVs displaced by a 2D flow vector field. offset = (flow.rb - bias) * weight. Pair with node.flow_field_noise upstream for procedural distortion (Watercolor-style), or with any other primitive that emits 2-channel offsets packed in R/B (e.g. an upstream node.custom_convolution configured as a gradient).
nodes.summary: Samples the image at positions pushed by a flow field, so the picture smears along the motion. The consumer for an optical-flow or noise flow field.

nodes.label: UV Field
nodes.purpose: Pure generator. Writes per-pixel UV coordinates as a texture: R = u (0..1 left-to-right), G = v (0..1 top-to-bottom), B = 0, A = 1. Foundation primitive of the procedural texture math family — compose with math/noise/distance/polar primitives to author novel procedural textures.
nodes.summary: Outputs the position of each pixel as a coordinate, red for left-to-right and green for top-to-bottom. The starting grid for most warps and patterns.

nodes.label: Value

params.label: Value
params.name: value
params.tooltip: The constant number this outputs. Set it by hand or expose it to drive from outside.

nodes.purpose: Emit a constant scalar value on the `out` port. Drive any scalar input by wiring this in; the consumer's same-named param becomes the fallback for when the wire is absent.
nodes.summary: Outputs a single fixed number you set by hand. Wire it into any knob as a constant, or expose it to drive from outside.

nodes.label: Value Overlay

params.label: Format
params.name: format
params.tooltip: What the labels show. Index is the array number, Hex is a hex ID, Coord prints the X and Y position, and Float3 shows a wired value.

params.label: Color
params.name: color
params.tooltip: The colour of the printed text.

params.label: Alpha
params.name: alpha
params.tooltip: Overall opacity of the labels. 1 is solid and lower values fade them into the image.

params.label: Font Scale
params.name: font_scale
params.tooltip: Size of the text. 1 is the base size, raise it for bigger labels.

params.label: Label Count
params.name: label_count
params.tooltip: The most labels to draw. Lower it to thin out a crowded readout.

params.label: Offset X
params.name: offset_x
params.tooltip: Nudges every label sideways from its anchor point, in fractions of the frame width.

params.label: Offset Y
params.name: offset_y
params.tooltip: Nudges every label up or down from its anchor point, in fractions of the frame height.

params.label: Anchor
params.name: anchor
params.tooltip: Which corner or centre of each region the label sits on, from Top Left through to Center.

nodes.purpose: Lightweight bitmap-font numeric labels at multiple positions, composited onto a source texture. Embedded 5×7 glyph atlas (0-9, A-F, hex, coords). Format enum selects display: Index (decimal), Hex (0Xnn), Coord (xxx,yyy from X/Y channels), Float3 (value×1000). For diagnostic HUDs, data-viz annotation layers, debug overlays.
nodes.summary: Prints small numeric labels onto the image at given spots using a built-in font. A quick readout for values flowing through a graph.

nodes.label: Variable Blur

params.label: Axis
params.name: axis
params.tooltip: Which direction this pass blurs, Horizontal or Vertical. Run one of each in sequence for a full blur in both directions.

params.label: Max Radius
params.name: max_radius
params.tooltip: The widest the blur can reach at full strength, in pixels. The control image scales each pixel between sharp and this maximum.

params.label: Quality
params.name: quality
params.tooltip: How many samples the blur takes, Low, Medium, or High. Higher is smoother but slower, and Medium matches the original.

params.label: Weighting Mode
params.name: weighting_mode
params.tooltip: None is a plain blur. ScatterAsGatherByCoC stops sharp areas from bleeding into blurry ones, which is what depth of field needs.

nodes.purpose: Separable Gaussian blur where the per-pixel kernel width is sampled from a `width` Texture2D's R channel. One dispatch handles one axis (horizontal or vertical); pair two with ping-pong textures for a 2D blur. Three quality levels (9-/17-/25-tap kernels at σ≈2/4/6) and an optional CoC-driven scatter-as-gather weighting that prevents sharp pixels bleeding into blurry regions — load-bearing for DoF-class effects.
nodes.summary: A Gaussian blur whose strength changes per pixel from a control image, so some areas blur more than others. Feed a mask or depth map into the width input for selective focus.

nodes.label: Vector Length
nodes.purpose: Per-pixel `length(in.rg)` as a scalar field in the R channel (GBA = 0, 0, 1). The vec2 magnitude atom — converts signed flow / displacement / gradient textures into positive scalar fields. Standard upstream step for heightmap-style ops that need a derived height from a vec2 source.
nodes.summary: Measures the length of the red and green channels read as a 2D vector, giving the strength of a flow or gradient field.

nodes.label: Vignette

params.label: Shape
params.name: shape
params.tooltip: The shape of the darkened border, circle, oval, or rectangle.

params.label: Size
params.name: size
params.tooltip: How far in from the edges the darkening starts.

params.label: Softness
params.name: softness
params.tooltip: How gradually the darkening fades in.

params.label: Strength
params.name: strength
params.tooltip: How dark the border gets.

nodes.purpose: Soft fade-to-black border. Circle = aspect-corrected true circle (cinematic); Ellipse = canvas-fit oval; Rectangle = per-edge fade (hides hard sampling cutoffs in feedback / mirror / displacement chains). `size` sets the inner full-opacity boundary, `softness` is the fade width, `strength` blends the result back against the untouched input.
nodes.summary: Darkens the edges of the frame to pull the eye inward, with a circle, oval, or rectangular falloff. The cinematic edge fade.

nodes.label: Voronoi 2D

params.label: Scale
params.name: scale
params.tooltip: How many cells fill the frame. Higher values pack in more, smaller cells.

params.label: Offset X
params.name: offset_x
params.tooltip: Slides the whole cell pattern sideways. Wire an LFO here and the cells drift.

params.label: Offset Y
params.name: offset_y
params.tooltip: Slides the cell pattern up and down.

params.label: Jitter
params.name: jitter
params.tooltip: How irregular the cells are. At 0 you get a perfect grid, at 1 they scatter into random shapes.

params.label: Output Scale
params.name: out_scale
params.tooltip: Brightens or dims the result, handy for pushing the pattern into a usable range.

nodes.purpose: Pure generator. 2D Worley / Voronoi cellular noise. `out` packs F1 in R (distance to nearest feature point), F2 in G (second-nearest), F2-F1 in B (cell-edge factor — high at boundaries), and a per-cell stable random hash in A (same value across every pixel inside a cell, uncorrelated between cells — drives per-cell variation: density threshold, twinkle frequency, per-cell colour, per-cell size). `cell_id` carries the F1-winning cell's integer coordinate in RG (constant within a Voronoi region) — feed RG + a seed into node.hash_field_by_seed for beat-reseeded per-cell composites (Voronoi Prism). Both outputs are independently optional: read only what you need and the other slot isn't allocated. Foundation for cellular patterns, cracked-glass, stained-glass, stars (sparse jitter + per-star twinkle), foam, fire embers, procedural tiles.
nodes.summary: Cellular noise that gives each cell a distance and a stable random value. Good for tiles, foam, cracked glass and starfields.

nodes.purpose: Pixel-exact wrap of the legacy WatercolorFX composite. Seven sequential passes: grain+max, flow, displacement, diffusion blur, slope displace, luma blur with persistent feedback, wet/dry. Legacy bundle pending decomposition.
nodes.summary: A watercolor look built from a seven-pass feedback simulation, with grain, flow, diffusion, and soft bleeding edges. A legacy bundle still waiting to be decomposed.

nodes.label: Wet/Dry

params.label: Wet/Dry
params.name: wet_dry
params.tooltip: How much of the processed image to show over the original. 0 is dry, 1 is fully wet.

nodes.purpose: Crossfade a processed `wet` texture back over the original `dry` texture by a `wet_dry` factor [0,1]. At 0 returns dry unchanged; at 1 returns wet. RGBA-wide lerp.
nodes.summary: Crossfades a processed image back over the original, so you can dial how much of an effect shows. At 0 you get the original, at 1 the full effect.

nodes.label: WGSL Compute
nodes.purpose: User-authored WGSL compute escape hatch. The shader is the contract: ports, uniform layout, workgroup size, binding map and output formats are all derived from the source via naga introspection. Backs effect families too varied to enumerate as fixed primitives.
nodes.summary: A blank compute node you write your own WGSL shader into. The escape hatch for effects the built-in nodes don't cover, where the shader defines its own inputs and outputs.

nodes.label: Wrap

params.label: Scale
params.name: scale
params.tooltip: Multiplies the input before keeping only the fractional part, which sets how many times the gradient repeats. 10 gives ten tiled stripes from 0 to 1.

nodes.purpose: Per-pixel fract(input.rgb * scale). Returns x - floor(x). Multiplying before fract is the classic 'tile a smooth field into N repeating stripes' trick — e.g. fract(uv.x * 10) → 10 vertical stripes from 0 to 1.
nodes.summary: Keeps only the part after the decimal point, which wraps every value back into 0 to 1. Multiply the input first to tile or repeat a gradient.

nodes.label: Wrap Around (torus)

params.label: Active Count
params.name: active_count
params.tooltip: How many particles get wrapped, counted from the front of the buffer. It is capped to the real particle count.

nodes.purpose: Per-particle toroidal wrap: position.xy = fract(position.xy + 1). The cyclic-boundary policy atom for fluid sims and any flow-driven particle pipeline whose domain is `[0, 1]²`. Dead particles (life <= 0) pass through unchanged. Decomposed out of the legacy fused `integrate_particles` kernel — kept separate so different boundary policies (boundary_death, wall_bounce) can ship as sibling atoms without forking the integrator.
nodes.summary: Wraps particles back to the opposite edge when they leave the frame, so the cloud loops seamlessly instead of escaping.

nodes.purpose: Output boundary for both effect chains and generators. The host pre-binds the final output texture here.
nodes.summary: The final image leaving a chain or generator. Wired in automatically.

nodes.purpose: Generator graph entry boundary. Emits the per-frame scalar context: time, beat, aspect, trigger_count, anim_progress.
nodes.summary: The per-frame context a generator starts from, with time, beat, aspect, and trigger count. Wired in automatically.

nodes.purpose: Effect-chain input boundary. The host pre-binds the upstream texture here.
nodes.summary: The incoming image at the start of an effect chain. Wired in automatically.
